confirmed_edits.title: Add conditional schema rule enforcing Evidence_Record when status=proven
confirmed_edits.anchor: definitions.CoverageItem JSON block (design.md:322-385); required list at line 324; evidence property at 381-383
confirmed_edits.change: Add an allOf/if-then to CoverageItem in the design.md schema block: `"allOf": [{"if": {"properties": {"status": {"const": "proven"}}, "required": ["status"]}, "then": {"required": ["evidence"]}}]` so a schema-valid item cannot be status=proven with no evidence object. Update the Phase-0 schema test row (design.md:623) to also assert a status=proven item with NO evidence object is rejected (today it only rejects a missing output_hash inside an existing evidence object). Add a note in Req 5.3/5.6 and Property 2 that the four-field gate is jointly schema- AND hook-enforced.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve status/in_scope required-vs-default contradiction in CoverageItem schema
confirmed_edits.anchor: CoverageItem.required at design.md:324; status default 'unproven' at 373; in_scope default true at 378
confirmed_edits.change: A JSON-Schema `default` never populates a value for a `required` property, so 'status defaults to unproven' (Req 5.1/Property 1) and 'in_scope DEFAULT true' (Req 5.7) are not realized by the file schema even though they ARE realized by the Postgres DEFAULTs (design.md:447-448), making a hand/tool-written file that omits status schema-INVALID while the equivalent DB row is valid-and-defaulted. Pick one: (a) drop `status` and `in_scope` from CoverageItem.required and let the schema `default` annotate, OR (b) keep them required, remove the misleading `default` keywords, and reword Req 5.1/Property 1 to 'every item is WRITTEN with status=unproven by the Initializer' and Req 5.7 likewise. Note the DB-vs-file default divergence so the conservative-merge rule (tasks.md:730) stays consistent.

confirmed_edits.title: Add append-only ordering/length preservation check and property
confirmed_edits.anchor: 'Never deleted or reordered' (design.md:126, 288); Property 3 Identity-Mutation Blockade (design.md:898-902)
confirmed_edits.change: Property 3 blocks mutation of id/type/acceptance_criteria of an existing item but does NOT catch a pure reorder or a deletion that leaves surviving items' id/type/AC intact. Extend Property 3 (and the PreToolUse status/artifact guard at design.md:214-215) to diff the incoming items[] id-sequence and length against the prior committed version of feature_list.json and block on any deletion, truncation, or reorder. Add the corresponding assertion to tasks.md task 6.1 (check_artifact_guard / a new check_append_only) and its property test (tasks.md 6.4 / Property 3).

confirmed_edits.title: Model the three-state status-transition graph in the Z3 harness or soften the machine-checked claim
confirmed_edits.anchor: Section 1 coverage/completion Booleans (lines 73-92); harness has no {unproven,proven,failed} enum or edge graph
confirmed_edits.change: The harness models coverage with standalone Booleans (unproven/passed/evidence*) and never encodes the {unproven,proven,failed} three-state machine or the permitted-edge set (unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven) with all other edges UNSAT, so Req 5.8/Property 3's 'machine-checked status graph' is overstated. Add a CHECK group (e.g. CHECK-14a..n) using an Int/enum status variable that asserts each permitted edge is SAT and every non-permitted edge (e.g. proven→failed, proven→unproven without amendment, failed→proven without evidence) is UNSAT, and reference it from requirements.md:123/129. Until added, soften 'machine-checked' wording for the status graph in requirements.md and design.md.

confirmed_edits.title: Extend amendment re-entry to flip the feature_list.json item, not only Postgres coverage_items
confirmed_edits.anchor: 'On amendment: insert a version row AND reset coverage_items.status to unproven' (design.md:1152); REQ-COV-007 / Property 25
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-COV-007/Property 25/CHECK-10 reset only Postgres coverage_items.status; on a Postgres-down amendment (the file is the Phase-0 source of truth and DB-down fallback, design.md:815) the feature_list.json item stays proven while the model is logically unproven. Extend Req 22 / the amendment design step / task 48 to ALSO flip the corresponding feature_list.json item to unproven and invalidate its plan-approval binding (re-block via feature_list_sha) on amendment, and add a Postgres-down amendment test under task 39.15.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a CI git-blob-SHA check enforcing checklist_ref.sha fidelity
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 18 Checklist Version Link Fidelity (design.md:1018-1022); task 39.9 (tasks.md:626-630)
confirmed_edits.change: Property 18 asserts checklist_ref.sha matches the actual git blob SHA, but no task computes the git blob SHA in CI — task 39.9 only writes the property test. Add a CI/task step (extend task 39.9 or the coverage-gate workflow) that runs `git hash-object` on checklist_ref.path and fails if it does not equal feature_list.json's recorded checklist_ref.sha, wiring Property 18 to an actual deterministic check.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix glossary wording implying evidence is always present on every item
confirmed_edits.anchor: Glossary feature_list.json entry, requirements.md:28
confirmed_edits.change: Line 28 reads '...with type, status, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and evidence' as if evidence is always present, but evidence is optional and present only on proven items. Reword to '...with type, status, dependencies, acceptance criteria, in_scope, and (when proven) an Evidence_Record.'

confirmed_edits.title: Enforce ears_pattern (and conditionally provenance) at the file level or relocate the guarantee to spec_validator
confirmed_edits.anchor: CoverageItem.required at design.md:324; ears_pattern at 345-349; provenance at 365-369
confirmed_edits.change: `ears_pattern` and `provenance` are schema-optional, yet Req 1.4/Property 16 require exactly one EARS pattern per item and Req 2.3 requires provenance on every inferred requirement, so both guarantees are unenforced at the file level. Either add `ears_pattern` to CoverageItem.required and add an allOf making `provenance` required when provenance=='inferred' is in play (or unconditionally), OR explicitly state in Req 1.4 and Req 2.3 that these are enforced by spec_validator.py at authoring time (not by the file schema) and add the corresponding spec_validator check + task. Pick one and make schema, requirements, and Property 16 agree.

confirmed_edits.title: Add an NFR subtype field so the Verifier can route performance vs accessibility deterministically
confirmed_edits.anchor: CoverageItem schema (design.md:331-385), type enum at 331-335; Req 25.1/25.2 (requirements.md:447-448)
confirmed_edits.change: Req 25.1/25.2 route an NFR item to k6/Lighthouse vs axe-core by subtype `performance`/`accessibility`, but CoverageItem has only `type ∈ {functional,NFR,WIRING}` and no machine-readable subtype, so perf_a11y_verifier cannot select its evidence tool from the coverage model. Add an optional `nfr_subtype` property (enum e.g. {performance, accessibility, reliability, security}) to CoverageItem, required-when type=='NFR' via an allOf, mirror it in the Postgres requirements table, and reference it from Req 25.1/25.2 and the verifier component (design.md:146).

confirmed_edits.title: Tie WIRING-type items to an integration-test Evidence_Record at the schema/Property level
confirmed_edits.anchor: type description 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence' (design.md:334); EvidenceRecord schema (design.md:387-411); Req 8.3 (requirements.md:174)
confirmed_edits.change: Req 8.3 demands an integration test exercising the real execution path as the Evidence_Record for a proven WIRING item, but EvidenceRecord is a uniform 4-field shape with no marker distinguishing an integration test from a unit test, and no schema rule/Property ties WIRING→integration evidence. Add an `evidence_kind`/`test_kind` enum field (e.g. {unit, integration, behavioral, perf, a11y}) to EvidenceRecord, add an allOf requiring evidence_kind=='integration' when the item type=='WIRING' transitions to proven, and add a Property (or extend Property 2) asserting it. Wire it to task 47.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix Property 24 PBT field-name from acting_agent to actor_agent
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 39.13 PBT body, tasks.md:655
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' and Property 24 (design.md:1068) uses actor_agent, but the task 39.13 PBT asserts on `acting_agent` ('Generate evidence chains with arbitrary acting_agent identities; assert any Evidence_Record where acting_agent == "implementer.md"...'), so the Implementer-cannot-self-verify test checks a field the design says does not exist. Replace both occurrences of `acting_agent` with `actor_agent` in tasks.md:655.

confirmed_edits.title: Cross-reference the 'Complex' EARS-pattern resolution from the glossary, Req 1.4, and Property 16
confirmed_edits.anchor: Glossary EARS line listing six patterns incl. Complex (requirements.md:31); Req 1.4 'five EARS patterns' (requirements.md:65)
confirmed_edits.change: The glossary lists six EARS patterns (incl. Complex) while the schema enum (design.md:347), Req 1.4, and Property 16 use five; design.md:1188 resolves this ('Complex = a documented composition of the five base patterns; no schema change') but that resolution is not referenced anywhere the reader hits the five-vs-six mismatch. Add a parenthetical to the glossary line 31 (and a note in Req 1.4 and Property 16) pointing to the design.md:1188 resolution so 'six' (glossary) and 'five' (schema/Req 1.4) are explicitly reconciled.

confirmed_edits.title: Block COMPLETE/merge on an empty (zero-item) feature_list.json
confirmed_edits.anchor: items array (no minItems) design.md:315-319; evaluate_stop completion gate design.md:728-736; OPA zero-evidence policy
confirmed_edits.change: tools/feature_list_init.py writes `items: []` (tasks.md:239) and the items array has no minItems, so an all-empty coverage model trivially satisfies the Stop gate (not_proven is empty → allow → COMPLETE) and the OPA zero-evidence policy, reading as COMPLETE before discovery has run. Add a precondition: gates (Stop completion path and OPA merge policy) must additionally require that discovery has run / items is non-empty (or that no domain-baseline item is UNMAPPED) before a COMPLETE verdict; document that `items: []` is a valid INIT state but not a valid COMPLETE state, and add a test asserting an empty file blocks COMPLETE.

confirmed_edits.title: Reconcile feature_list_sha raw-byte binding with the expected in-place status-flip mutation
confirmed_edits.anchor: plan-approval gate compute_sha256(read_bytes()) design.md:791-792; 'status flip is the expected mutation' design.md:288
confirmed_edits.change: The plan-approval gate binds approval to sha256 over the literal file BYTES (design.md:791-792), but the design EXPECTS in-place status flips unproven→proven (design.md:288) which change those bytes — so after the first legitimate status flip the SHA no longer matches and every subsequent implementation Write is re-blocked, forcing a fresh human approval; whitespace/key-reorder changes also re-block. Specify a canonical comparison: compute feature_list_sha over a canonicalized projection of the item set (e.g. sorted (id,type,priority,dependencies,acceptance_criteria,in_scope) excluding the mutable status/evidence fields), so status flips do NOT invalidate approval while id/type/AC/scope changes do. Update Property 6 and design.md:789-801 accordingly.

refuted.item: Critic finding: Postgres coverage_items does not mirror `type` or `acceptance_criteria`, undercutting the 'mirrored to Postgres' framing
refuted.why: Not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The design uses a deliberate NORMALIZED split: `requirements` table holds type/priority/ears_pattern/provenance (design.md:431-440), `evidence_records` holds the Evidence_Record (465-478), and `coverage_items` is intentionally just the mutable status view (id, requirement_id, status, in_scope, slice_id, updated_at). The artifacts never claim coverage_items mirrors the full item shape. The critique targets the AUDIT's own 'what' prose ('mirrored to Postgres / same item shape'), not the spec; the underlying Postgres-down amendment concern it raises is already captured by the gap-5 edit (amendment-reentry-file-mirror).

refuted.item: open_gap 6 sub-point: priority uniqueness should be schema-enforced
refuted.why: No requirement mandates global priority uniqueness. Req 7.1 requires the Implementer to take 'exactly one highest-priority unproven item' but a deterministic tie-break (e.g. by id) satisfies that without a uniqueness constraint. The schema sets priority minimum 1 (design.md:336-339); adding a uniqueness invariant would over-constrain with no backing requirement. The other parts of gap 6 (dependencies referential integrity, provenance-required, checklist_ref.sha) ARE confirmed and emitted under their own dedup_keys.

refuted.item: citation_issue: requirements.md:124 cited for both Req 5.3 and Req 5.6 (5.6 is at line 127)
refuted.why: The canonical requirements.md is correct — Req 5.3 IS at line 124 and Req 5.6 IS at line 127. The miscitation lives only in the audit's own ears_stories metadata, not in any of the four canonical artifacts, so there is nothing to edit in requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md/the harness. Out of scope for an artifact edit.

refuted.item: critic_finding 9: gap-2 blast radius — Postgres defaults fire while JSON schema defaults don't (DB row valid, file invalid)
refuted.why: DUPLICATE of open_gap 2 (same root cause: status/in_scope are simultaneously in `required` and given a `default`). Merged into the schema-required-vs-default edit, which now explicitly calls out the DB-vs-file default divergence rather than emitting a second edit.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work entries (Req 5.1, 5.2, 5.3/5.6, 5.7, Req 22, Z3 status graph)
refuted.why: All six dedup into already-confirmed gaps and need no separate add-wiring edit: Req 5.1 + Req 5.7 → schema-required-vs-default (gap 2); Req 5.2 → append-only-order-length-guard (gap 3); Req 5.3/5.6 → schema-evidence-required-when-proven (gap 1); Req 22 → amendment-reentry-file-mirror (gap 5); Z3 status graph → z3-status-three-state-graph (gap 4). The mapping each names is the same wiring already created by the confirmed edits.

confirmed_edits.title: Define MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE constant and reconcile run_state.budget_exceeded / token_budget_usd against the DDL
confirmed_edits.anchor: evaluate_stop pseudocode lines 699-707: `if run_state.iteration_count >= MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE:` and `if run_state.budget_exceeded:           # token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd`
confirmed_edits.change: MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE is used at design.md:699/701 but never defined as a module constant (only N_PROGRESS_WINDOW=3 and SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP=7 are). Add a module-constant block near line 654 (e.g. `MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE = 25  # DEFAULT, bound to the task-44 execution-bounds config module`). Also `run_state.budget_exceeded` and `token_budget_usd` (line 704) have NO backing column: the run_state DDL (481-496) defines only `token_cost_usd NUMERIC(10,4)`. Either add a `token_budget_usd` column to the run_state DDL, or replace `run_state.budget_exceeded` with a computed predicate `run_state.token_cost_usd >= TOKEN_BUDGET` where TOKEN_BUDGET is a config constant bound to the Requirement-20 registry (req:357). Bind MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE and the budget threshold to task 44 (Execution-bounds config module).

confirmed_edits.title: Reconcile budget unit: registry says 1,000,000 tokens, run_state stores USD
confirmed_edits.anchor: run_state DDL line 488 `token_cost_usd    NUMERIC(10,4) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,` vs evaluate_stop line 704 comment `# token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd`
confirmed_edits.change: The cost budget is specified as 1,000,000 TOKENS per slice in the registry (requirements.md:357) but run_state stores `token_cost_usd` in USD and the design comment compares USD-to-USD with no conversion defined. Pick ONE unit: either rename the column to `token_count` (integer tokens) and compare against the 1,000,000-token registry threshold, OR keep USD and change requirements.md:357 to a USD budget. Store both the running total and the threshold in the same unit and state the comparison explicitly in evaluate_stop. This is the same threshold-registry artifact as the MAX_TURNS/budget defect.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire with_reentrancy_guard into a stop_hook main() entrypoint
confirmed_edits.anchor: `def with_reentrancy_guard(fn):` block at design.md:741-753 (immediately after the evaluate_stop pseudocode)
confirmed_edits.change: with_reentrancy_guard(fn) is defined but no code composes it with evaluate_stop, so the guard is dead. Add a `main()` entrypoint showing `result = with_reentrancy_guard(lambda: evaluate_stop(event)); save_run_state(run_state); sys.exit(0 if result.decision=='allow' else 2)`. This wires Req 10.4 (REQ-GATE-004) into the component. Mirror in tasks.md:100/126 (task 5.1 names with_reentrancy_guard and task 5.5 names the reentrancy unit test, but neither shows the composition).

confirmed_edits.title: Amend Property 5 and design rows 209/624 to include the HANDOFF-allow carve-out
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 5 (design.md:914-916 'Termination is permitted only when zero items are `unproven`'); Hook-Wiring row design.md:209 'Termination while any in-scope item is `unproven` or `violation_count > 0`'; Phase-0 test row design.md:624 'With one unproven item → blocks'
confirmed_edits.change: Property 5, the Stop hook-wiring 'What it blocks' cell (209), and the Phase-0 test-to-pass row (624) all state the pure 'unproven blocks' rule WITHOUT the REQ-LOOP-005 HANDOFF override. At HANDOFF (cap/budget/no-progress) in-scope items are EXPECTED to remain unproven yet the hook MUST allow (exit 0); a naive implementer following Property 5/row-624 alone reproduces the infinite-block defect. Amend all three to read: 'blocks termination while any in-scope item is unproven AND no HANDOFF trigger (cap/budget/no-progress) is active.' tasks.md:948 already extends this caveat to Properties 8/9 — extend the same caveat to Property 5 and update the property-test spec at tasks.md:104-108 (5.2) which currently asserts unconditional exit-2 on any unproven mix.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve Req 4.3 strict-decrease mechanism: scalar violation_count has no prior-pass comparator in evaluate_stop
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 5.1 _Requirements_ line 102 `_Requirements: 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 10.2, 10.4, 14.1, 14.2_`
confirmed_edits.change: Req 4.3 (strict-decrease no-progress at spec level → HANDOFF) is listed under stop_hook task 5.1, but evaluate_stop only reads the scalar `run_state.violation_count` and has no access to the PRIOR pass's count to compute strict-decrease (design.md:837-840 describes it as the spec-completion loop's responsibility, NOT the Stop hook's). Either (a) remove 4.3 from task 5.1's _Requirements_ list (the strict-decrease check lives in the spec-completion loop / Initializer, design.md:832-840), OR (b) add a `prev_violation_count` column to the run_state DDL (design.md:481-496) and implement the pass-over-pass comparison inside evaluate_stop. Recommend (a) to keep the Stop hook a pure scalar gate.

confirmed_edits.title: Mark the Stop-hook audit_log.append call as a Phase-2 addition (guarded/no-op until task 52.3)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook-Wiring table Stop row, design.md:209 'calls `audit_log.append(...)` on the allow/block decision'
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:209 and Req 27.4 present `audit_log.append(...)` as always-on in the Phase-0 Stop hook, but `audit_log.py` (the producer) is Phase 2 and its wiring into stop_hook.py is task 52.3 (Phase 2). A Phase-0 hook cannot call a Phase-2 producer. Annotate the Stop row (and the SubagentStop/PreToolUse audit rows at 217/218/210) to say the audit_log.append call is a Phase-2 addition (no-op/absent until task 52.3 wires it), matching the staging already implied by tasks.md:970. Keeps the Phase-0 spine self-consistent.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a standalone Z3 check for the budget HANDOFF trigger (blocking-UNSAT and routing-SAT)
confirmed_edits.anchor: CHECK-5b (lines 266-269) and CHECK-5c (lines 272-275) — the cap/no-progress HANDOFF-exit-0 UNSAT checks; CHECK-3b (line 207-212) the cap routing-SAT check
confirmed_edits.change: budgetExceeded appears only inside the core_axioms disjunction (line 152) and as a negated literal in CHECK-1/3b/8b/10b; it has NEITHER a standalone blocking-UNSAT check (mirroring CHECK-5b/5c: `core_axioms + [budgetExceeded, hookExitsBlocking]` expected UNSAT) NOR a routing-SAT check (mirroring CHECK-3b: `core_axioms + [budgetExceeded, handoff, Not(complete), Not(hookExitsBlocking)]` expected SAT). It is the only one of the three HANDOFF triggers with no dedicated check. Add CHECK-5d (budget blocking-UNSAT) and optionally CHECK-3c (budget routing-SAT). Update the harness self-count banner (currently 34: 14 core) and the requirements.md:9 / design.md:1191 '34/34' figure accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: Reorder task 5.1 checklist so HANDOFF triggers are evaluated BEFORE the unproven-items block
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 5.1, tasks.md:99 'Check 1 — any `unproven` items → **exit 2** (block, force continuation) ...; Check 2 — no-progress ...; Check 3 — iteration cap ...'
confirmed_edits.change: task 5.1 numbers the unproven-items block as 'Check 1' (FIRST) and cap/no-progress HANDOFF as Checks 2/3 (AFTER), contradicting design.md:692-734 which evaluates the three HANDOFF triggers (cap/budget/no-progress) BEFORE the unproven gate. Order is load-bearing for REQ-LOOP-005: if unproven is checked first the hook blocks (exit 2) at the cap and reproduces the infinite-block defect. Reorder so Check 1 = cap HANDOFF (exit 0), Check 2 = budget HANDOFF (exit 0), Check 3 = no-progress HANDOFF (exit 0), Check 4 = violation_count block, Check 5 = unproven-items block (the only exit-2 path). Drop the 'force continuation' gloss on the unproven case and add the missing budget-HANDOFF check (5.1 currently omits budget entirely — only names cap and no-progress).

confirmed_edits.title: Add a run_state.phase discriminator and specify per-phase cap/no-progress routing in evaluate_stop
confirmed_edits.anchor: run_state DDL design.md:481-496 (no `phase` column); evaluate_stop pseudocode design.md:685-737 (single undifferentiated cap/no-progress path)
confirmed_edits.change: stop_hook serves BOTH the spec-completion loop (Req 4: violation_count, cap=7 passes, pass-level strict-decrease no-progress) AND the implementation loop (Req 10/14: slice completion, cap=25 turns/slice, slice-level N=3 no-progress, design.md:840). The design never states how one Stop hook discriminates the two phases, yet they have different caps and different no-progress semantics. Add a `phase TEXT CHECK (phase IN ('spec','implementation'))` column to the run_state DDL and branch evaluate_stop: in spec phase apply SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP=7 + pass-level no-progress; in implementation phase apply MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE=25 + slice-level check_no_progress. Cite design.md:832-842.

confirmed_edits.title: Add subtasks for emit_handoff_summary, write_run_state, and the slice-history counters
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 5 / 5.1 (tasks.md:95-102) — enumerates only load_feature_list/load_run_state/check_no_progress/evaluate_stop/with_reentrancy_guard
confirmed_edits.change: evaluate_stop references emit_handoff_summary, write_run_state, count_items_proven_since, and count_commits_since (design.md:700-713,663-672) and the budget branch (design.md:704) but none of these helpers is tasked anywhere; task 5.1 stops at evaluate_stop/with_reentrancy_guard. The HANDOFF-summary content contract (remaining unproven items, last N slice outputs, reason — design.md:828) also has no subtask. Add subtasks under task 5: (5.1b) emit_handoff_summary implementing the design.md:828 content contract; (5.1c) write_run_state/save_run_state persistence; (5.1d) count_items_proven_since / count_commits_since slice-history counters used by check_no_progress.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Req 13.5 (Property 21 Hook Exit-Code Contract) to stop_hook task 5.1 _Requirements_
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 5.1 _Requirements_ line 102 `_Requirements: 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 10.2, 10.4, 14.1, 14.2_`
confirmed_edits.change: Req 13.5 (requirements.md:254 — exit 0 = proceed, exit 2 = blocking via stderr, exit 1 does NOT block) is the exact contract stop_hook.py emits (exit-0 allow/COMPLETE/HANDOFF vs exit-2 block), and Property 21 (design.md:1042-1046) validating it is already wired as a stop-hook-touching property test in test_hooks.py (tasks.md:590-597). But task 5.1's _Requirements_ list omits 13.5 entirely. Add 13.5 to task 5.1's _Requirements_ (and to task 5.5's unit-test _Requirements at line 128, which currently lists only 10.4) so the Hook Exit-Code Contract is traced to its canonical emitter. This wires the EARS criterion to the component.

confirmed_edits.title: Capture the N-25 Postgres-unavailable fallback gate-consistency constraints in the Stop-hook load_run_state spec
confirmed_edits.anchor: task 5.1 line 97 `Implement load_feature_list() and load_run_state() (reading from feature_list.json and claude-progress.txt / in-memory fallback before Postgres)`
confirmed_edits.change: Note N-25 (tasks.md:730) is a load-bearing obligation on the Stop coverage gate: when db_connection.health_check() fails, the gate MUST fall back to file-backed state (feature_list.json + claude-progress.txt) and reach the SAME allow/block decision it would on Postgres — 'never fail-open', and 'on divergence the gate takes the more conservative (blocking) result.' task 5.1's load_run_state bullet captures only the read order, dropping the three correctness constraints (file-fallback equivalence, never-fail-open, conservative-on-divergence). Add these three constraints to the load_run_state bullet and cross-reference Note N-25.

confirmed_edits.title: State that an in-scope item in `failed` status also blocks the Stop completion gate
confirmed_edits.anchor: evaluate_stop completion gate design.md:731 `not_proven = [i for i in in_scope_items if i.status != "proven"]` and Property 5 (design.md:914-916)
confirmed_edits.change: With Req 5.8 / Property 3 making `failed` a first-class status, the completion gate `i.status != "proven"` means a `failed` in-scope item ALSO blocks termination (it is not-proven), exactly like `unproven`. Property 5 and the Stop hook-wiring row (design.md:209) describe the gate only in terms of `unproven`, never stating that `failed` likewise blocks. Update Property 5 / the design narrative to say 'any in-scope item whose status is not exactly `proven` (i.e. `unproven` OR `failed`) blocks termination', so the implementer does not special-case only `unproven`. Cite requirements.md:129; design.md:731,900.

refuted.item: open_gap: COMPONENT NOT BUILT — .claude/hooks/stop_hook.py is a 0-byte placeholder
refuted.why: Confirmed factually (only .gitkeep exists, stop_hook.py absent), but this is an implementation-state observation, not a gap in the canonical SPEC/DESIGN/TASKS artifacts under audit. Task 5.1 (tasks.md:96-102) already fully tasks the build (evaluate_stop, check_no_progress, with_reentrancy_guard, try/except fail-closed). The audit scope is spec-to-evidence artifact consistency, not whether code has been written yet; no artifact edit is warranted for the mere absence of the executable.

refuted.item: critic overstatement: draft's CHECK-1 ears_story claims it pins evaluate_stop's exit-0 terminal case
refuted.why: This critiques the DRAFT findings file's own framing of CHECK-1, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. CHECK-1 (formal_verification_merged.py:171-183) is correctly a whole-system reachable-COMPLETE SAT check; the canonical harness does not misrepresent it. No artifact change follows — it is a note about the draft's prose, which this validator supersedes.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a live PreToolUse secret-block gate (REQ-SEC-005 / 17.5) to the hook table and task 6
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table, after the `PreToolUse` — status guard row (design.md:215); and tasks.md task 6.1 check list (tasks.md:131-136)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a 7th PreToolUse sub-gate row to the hook-wiring table: `PreToolUse` — secret-block guard | Trigger: any tool call carrying a prompt/span-attribute/URL/Bash-arg | scans args+prompt against secret regexes (API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings) | blocks (exit 2) any call that would carry a secret | REQ-SEC-005 (17.5), Property 20. Then add `check_secret_block(event)` to task 6.1's chained checks and cite 17.5 in task 6's `_Requirements:` line. This is the live prevention gate 17.5 mandates ('NOT only via an offline test'); Property 20 (offline test, task 39.11) and task 45 (CI gitleaks, 17.2) do not satisfy it.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a Phase-0 implementation task for the PreToolUse checklist-approval guard (REQ-SPEC-016, Property 27, CHECK-12)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6 (tasks.md:130-137); cross-ref design.md:213 hook-table checklist-approval row and Property 27 (design.md:1094-1100)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a sub-task (under task 6 or task 50) implementing `check_checklist_approval(event)` in pre_tool_use_hook.py that, for an initializer/discovery Write referencing a domain-baseline checklist, blocks (exit 2) unless the referenced checklist has a non-null `approved_at` in `domain_baseline_checklists`. Cross-reference Property 27 / CHECK-12a-12b. Currently the design hook-table row (design.md:213), Property 27, and CHECK-12 exist but NO build task wires this guard into the hook (task 50 implements the abstract gate logic but does not name the PreToolUse hook check).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix task 6.4 property-test acceptance to assert the full widened status-edge set
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.4, line 'assert all are blocked; assert only `status: proven` writes are allowed' (tasks.md:154)
confirmed_edits.change: Rewrite the acceptance line to: 'assert the permitted status edges {unproven→proven (requires complete Evidence_Record), unproven→failed, failed→unproven} are allowed and all other transitions (identity mutations, into-proven without evidence, any edge outside the set) are blocked.' The current 'only status: proven writes are allowed' would fail a legitimate unproven→failed / failed→unproven write and contradicts requirements.md:123/129, design.md:215, and Property 3 (design.md:900).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix task 6.1 build step to validate the full permitted-edge set, not 'unproven → proven only'
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.1, `check_status_transition` bullet: 'validate proposed transition is `unproven → proven` only' (tasks.md:135)
confirmed_edits.change: Change the build-step spec to: 'validate the proposed transition is in the permitted set {unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven}; require a complete Evidence_Record only for *→proven; reject any transition outside the set and any identity mutation.' The current 'unproven → proven only' wording encodes the narrow pre-reconciliation edge set in the BUILD step itself (not just the test), contradicting requirements.md:123/129 and design.md:215/374. Same underlying defect as the task-6.4 test fix.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a Z3 CHECK family modeling the status-transition edge set including the failed state
confirmed_edits.anchor: New 8 checks section (CHECK-10a..13b, lines 409-500); model variables section (lines 121-131)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a CHECK family (e.g. CHECK-14a/b/c) introducing booleans for the status-transition guard: declare edge enums or booleans {edgeUnprovenProven, edgeUnprovenFailed, edgeFailedUnproven, edgeOther} plus evidenceComplete, and assert: (a) any transition into proven without evidenceComplete is UNSAT; (b) edgeOther (any transition not in the permitted set) is UNSAT; (c) edgeUnprovenFailed and edgeFailedUnproven WITHOUT evidence are SAT (the widened edges are reachable). The harness currently has no boolean for the `failed` status or the transition edge set, so the most-recently-widened guard logic (req 5.8 / Property 3) is unverified by the formal model. Update the header self-count comment accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the full ordered 6+-guard chain in task 6.1 and update 'Chain all four checks'
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.1 'Chain all four checks; return first blocking decision' (tasks.md:136); task 49.1 'chain it BEFORE the existing plan/scope/artifact/status checks' (tasks.md:964)
confirmed_edits.change: Replace 'Chain all four checks' with an explicit ordered chain that includes every guard the design assigns to this hook: integrity-guard (task 49.1) → plan → scope → artifact → status → checklist-approval (+ secret-block once added). Task 49.1 says the integrity guard chains BEFORE plan/scope/artifact/status, but task 6.1 still defines a four-check chain with no slot for the integrity or checklist guards, so the actual ordering across the 6 guards is unspecified. Pin the order here so the build is deterministic.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve REQ-AUDIT-001 Phase-0 vs Phase-2 audit-producer wiring split for pre_tool_use_hook.py
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6 (tasks.md:130-137); cross-ref design.md:210 (plan-gate row says it 'calls audit_log.append(...)') and requirements.md:460; producer-wiring lands only in task 52.3 (tasks.md:970)
confirmed_edits.change: Add an explicit note to task 6 that audit-log production (audit_log.append on every allow/block) is deferred to task 52.3 (Phase 2), and correspondingly soften the design.md:210 plan-gate row so it does not present audit production as an intrinsic Phase-0 capability of the PreToolUse plan gate. As written, design.md:210/217 describe a Phase-0 audit-producer behavior that no Phase-0/1 task delivers (audit_log.py and its hook wiring are Phase 2), so REQ-AUDIT-001 is unmet for the whole Phase-0/1 window with no acknowledgement of the deferral.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix Property 26 dangling cross-reference 'Validates: Requirements 27'
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 26, 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)' (design.md:1090)
confirmed_edits.change: Change 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)' to 'Validates: Requirements 23 (REQ-STATE-005)'. Requirement 27 is the Tamper-Evident Gate-Decision Audit Log; REQ-STATE-005 is Requirement 23 (Resumed-State Integrity), which is the requirement Property 26 / the PreToolUse integrity guard actually validates. The parenthetical REQ-ID is correct; the bare requirement number is wrong.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin the artifact guard's protected-path globs and destructive-Bash regex set canonically
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook-wiring artifact-guard row (design.md:214) and task 6.3 (tasks.md:134)
confirmed_edits.change: Define an explicit canonical PROTECTED_PATHS glob set (e.g. feature_list.json, schema/feature_list.schema.json, tests/**, .github/workflows/**, .github/policies/**) and a DESTRUCTIVE_BASH regex set covering bypass-equivalent forms (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, git reset --hard, git push --force/-f, truncate, find ... -delete, > redirection over protected paths) in design.md, and reference them from task 6.3. Also reconcile the membership disagreement: the design hook-table row (design.md:214) lists only `rm -rf` and `DROP TABLE` while task 6.3 (tasks.md:134) adds `git reset --hard` — make the two sources agree. Add a property test enumerating bypass-equivalent destructive commands.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the scope-gate's exact command/argument detection patterns
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.2 `check_scope_sequencing` (tasks.md:133); design.md:211 scope-gate row; CHECK-6a (formal_verification_merged.py:288)
confirmed_edits.change: Specify the exact Bash command/argument patterns the scope gate inspects (e.g. `git worktree add`, the worktree_manager.py create entry points, and any slice-assign command surface) and the tool surfaces it covers, in task 6.2 and the design.md:211 row. Add a property test for bypass forms (worktree created via a wrapper script or non-Bash tool) and note that CHECK-6a proves only the abstract scopeGateBlocks boolean, not the concrete command detection. As written, 'Bash worktree-create or slice-assign call' has no precise detection definition, so the gate can be bypassed.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire REQ-OBS-003 (12.3) gate-decision telemetry as an obligation on pre_tool_use_hook.py
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6 `_Requirements:` line (tasks.md:137); cross-ref task 36.1 which names PreToolUse (tasks.md:539) and requirements.md:236 (12.3)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a note to task 6 (and/or cite 12.3 in its `_Requirements:`) that PreToolUse gate decisions must emit an OTel gate-decision event (event, tool, allow/block, reason, requirement ID) per REQ-OBS-003 (12.3), with the actual emission implemented in task 36.1 (Phase 3, which already names PreToolUse). Currently 12.3 is a binding obligation on this component but task 6 omits OTel emission entirely and 12.3 is not cited anywhere in task 6, leaving the Phase-0/1 PreToolUse hook with no decision telemetry and no acknowledged deferral.

confirmed_edits.title: Add the schema-validation obligation for feature_list.json writes to task 6
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.1 check list (tasks.md:131-135); cross-ref design.md:138 ('PreToolUse validates against this') and design.md:623 (Phase-0 spine: 'JSON Schema that PreToolUse validates writes against')
confirmed_edits.change: Add a `check_schema_validation(event)` step to task 6.1 that JSON-Schema-validates every feature_list.json write against schema/feature_list.schema.json (blocking on a schema-invalid write, e.g. an item with a proven status but a missing output_hash), and cite this in design's status-guard row. Design.md:138 and the Phase-0 spine table (design.md:623, test 'rejects an example missing output_hash') both state PreToolUse validates feature_list.json writes against the schema, but task 6's check_status_transition validates only transition edges + evidence completeness, never invoking the JSON Schema — so the design-mandated schema-validation behavior has no build step.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire the Postgres-unavailable file-fallback into the PreToolUse status/scope/integrity guards in task 6
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 6.1-6.2 (tasks.md:131-135, file-only reads); cross-ref design.md:815 ('Postgres unavailable | Fall back to feature_list.json...') and the reconciliation note (tasks.md:730)
confirmed_edits.change: Add to task 6 that the scope gate (reads prior-slice statuses), status guard (reads/validates feature_list.json), and integrity guard (reads run_state.resume_integrity_ok) must follow the Postgres-unavailable fallback defined at design.md:815 and tasks.md:730 — falling back to file-backed state (feature_list.json + claude-progress.txt) and reaching the SAME (conservative/blocking) decision, never fail-open. Cite design.md:815 (not just the general note 730). Currently task 6 reads feature_list.json file-only with no DB path or fallback contract, so the gate-consistency-on-fallback obligation (N-25) is unwired for this hook.

refuted.item: open_gap: pre_tool_use_hook.py does not exist (only .gitkeep); the Phase-0 spine-runnable claim is false
refuted.why: Confirmed factually (.claude/hooks/ contains only .gitkeep), but this is an implementation-build-status observation, not a spec-artifact defect. The canonical artifacts already correctly task the build: design.md:128 inventories the component, design.md:625 lists it as Phase-0 spine #3 with pass tests, and tasks.md task 6 (130-137) implements it. There is no spec text to add or fix here — the artifacts are internally correct; the file is simply not built yet. Out of scope for a spec-reconciliation edit set.

refuted.item: open_gap: settings.json does not exist and no task creates it; PreToolUse binding is unwired
refuted.why: The claim 'no task currently creates settings.json' is false. Task 9 (tasks.md:191-199) is dedicated to it: task 9.1 'Write `.claude/settings.json` (or `.kiro/settings.json`) with all hook registrations ... all as type:command' and task 9.2 smoke-tests it. design.md:159-200 documents the exact hooks block. The settings file not yet existing on disk is a build-status fact, not a missing spec/task. Already covered.

refuted.item: open_gap (#4 portion): the integrity guard has design rows + Z3 checks but no build task tying it into the hook
refuted.why: Refuted for the integrity guard specifically. Task 49.1 (tasks.md:964) explicitly adds the PreToolUse integrity-guard check to pre_tool_use_hook.py reading run_state.resume_integrity_ok and blocking the first write with exit 2. Only the checklist-approval guard genuinely lacks an implementation task — that half is CONFIRMED and emitted separately under dedup_key checklist-approval-pretooluse-guard.

refuted.item: open_gap (#11): PRD credits a '13-row hook-wiring table' but design has 12 data rows
refuted.why: The count drift is real (design.md hook-wiring table has exactly 12 data rows; PRD_MERGED:17 says 13-row). But the anchor is the PRD file, which is outside the four canonical artifacts this validation is scoped to ('Ignore any docs/website copy'; the four named files do not include the PRD). The canonical-side remedy — adding the missing secret-block row to design's table — is the same edit already emitted under dedup_key secret-block-pretooluse-gate (adding that row makes the table 13 and closes 17.5 simultaneously). Collapsed as a duplicate of the secret-block edit.

refuted.item: critic_finding (overstatement): the draft attributes component-specific PreToolUse enforcement to CHECK-12 / CHECK-11, which are fully abstract (no PreToolUse/first-write/exit-2 model)
refuted.why: Accurate critique OF THE DRAFT component analysis, but not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The harness axioms ARE abstract by design (CHECK-11: Implies(resumeHashMismatch, Not(runProceeds)); CHECK-12: Implies(checklistIsDraft, Not(checklistUsedForDisc))) and the artifacts never claim otherwise — Property 26/27 in design.md correctly describe the concrete PreToolUse enforcement separately from the Z3 logic relation. No artifact edit follows; this is feedback on the draft's coverage characterization. (The genuinely missing piece — the checklist guard build task — is captured separately.)

refuted.item: critic_finding (weak): REQ-COV-001 (5.1) is not represented as an ears_story; the status/artifact guards enforce 5.1-defined identity
refuted.why: 5.1's structural identity (id/type/dependencies/acceptance_criteria/status defaulting to unproven) is already canonically traced and enforced: Property 1 (design.md:884, 'Validates Requirements 5.1, 5.4') covers the schema invariant, the JSON Schema (design.md:322-385) pins every field, and task 2.1 (_Requirements: 5.1) builds it. The PreToolUse identity-mutation blockade is traced via Property 3 (5.2, 13.3). Adding a separate 5.1 ears_story to the component draft does not require any change to the canonical artifacts, which already cover 5.1. Draft-completeness note, not an artifact gap.

refuted.item: critic_finding (weak): the 60s hook-validator timeout (requirements.md:354) is mis-scoped to pre_tool_use_hook.py; it governs spec_validator.py/stop_hook
refuted.why: This is a critique of the DRAFT's config_settings attribution, and it is correct that the 60s timeout is the spec_validator Z3 timeout (design.md:814) on the Stop-hook path, not a PreToolUse timeout. But the canonical artifacts already scope it correctly: design.md:814 and task 4.1 (tasks.md:69) attach the 60s timeout to spec_validator.py; requirements.md:354 lists it in the cross-cutting Req-20 threshold registry. Nothing in the artifacts misassigns it to pre_tool_use_hook.py, so there is no artifact edit to make — only the draft would need correcting.

confirmed_edits.title: Task the SessionStart→state_integrity compute/write (it is currently untasked)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 49 'Resumed-state integrity (REQ-STATE-005)' (tasks.md:963) and its only subtask 49.1; task 7.1 'Implement .claude/hooks/session_start_hook.py' (tasks.md:163-169)
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:219 and Property 26 (design.md:1088) both state SessionStart 'computes the resumed-state hash via state_integrity.py and writes run_state.resume_integrity_ok', but no task implements this: task 7.1 only does git/progress/coverage load + stdout injection, and task 49/49.1 create state_integrity.py + the PreToolUse guard with NO subtask wiring state_integrity INTO session_start_hook.py. Add a subtask 49.2 (or amend 49) naming the file: 'extend .claude/hooks/session_start_hook.py to import tools/state_integrity.py, call state_integrity.compute_hash(), and write run_state.resume_integrity_ok (Phase-2 extension of the Phase-0 hook; non-blocking, exit 0 always)'. Cross-reference it from task 7 so the Phase-0 component's later mutation is traceable. Extend task 7.2 unit tests with an assertion that resume_integrity_ok is written on resume. _Requirements: 11.3, REQ-STATE-005_.

confirmed_edits.title: Add resume_integrity_ok (and violation_count, retry_count) to migration task 28.5
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 28.5 'Write migration db/migrations/005_run_state.sql' — bullet 'run_state table with status CHECK {running,complete,handoff,blocked}, stop_hook_active boolean, no_progress_n counter'; _Requirements: 11.1, 16.1_ (tasks.md:441-443)
confirmed_edits.change: The design run_state SQL (design.md:481-496) declares resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, violation_count, and retry_count, but task 28.5 lists ONLY status CHECK + stop_hook_active + no_progress_n, so the durable column SessionStart is spec'd to write is un-tasked. Add 'resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN (REQ-STATE-005; written by SessionStart, read by the PreToolUse integrity guard), violation_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0' to the column list and add REQ-STATE-005 to the _Requirements:_ line.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix systematic Property→Requirement off-numbering (Property 26 mislabeled Req 27)
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1090 '**Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005). Machine-checked: CHECK-11...**' (and the same family: L1080 Property 25, L1100 Property 27, L1110 Property 28)
confirmed_edits.change: The canonical addendum mapping (requirements.md:507-512) makes REQ-STATE-005 = Requirement 23, but Property 26 cites 'Requirements 27' (Req 27 is the Tamper-Evident Audit Log). This mislabels THIS component's own correctness Property. Change 'Requirements 27' → 'Requirement 23' on L1090. The off-by-N is systematic across the block: Property 25 'Requirements 26' → Requirement 22 (REQ-COV-007); Property 27 'Requirements 25' → Requirement 24 (REQ-SPEC-016); Property 28 'Requirements 28' → Requirement 27 (REQ-AUDIT). Fix all four to match the requirements.md:505-512 cross-reference table.

confirmed_edits.title: Annotate SessionStart hook-wiring row + inventory that integrity compute is a Phase-2 augmentation
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table SessionStart row (design.md:219) and Component Inventory rows for session_start_hook.py (design.md:129, Phase 0) and state_integrity.py (design.md:143, Phase 2)
confirmed_edits.change: The SessionStart hook-wiring row presents the hash-compute as part of SessionStart from day one, but state_integrity.py is Phase 2 while session_start_hook.py is Phase 0. A reader building the Phase-0 spine would believe resume_integrity_ok is written from the start. Annotate the row's 'What it does' cell: 'load (git/progress/coverage): Phase 0; integrity compute + resume_integrity_ok write: Phase 2 via state_integrity.py'. Mirror the note in the session_start_hook.py inventory row (design.md:129).

confirmed_edits.title: Make OTel hook.decision span honest for non-gating hooks (SessionStart/PreCompact)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 36.1 'Extend all six hooks ... to emit OTel gate-decision events' — bullet setting attributes hook.event/tool.name/decision(allow/block)/reason/requirement.id on hook.decision span (tasks.md:539-542)
confirmed_edits.change: Task 36.1 sets a 'decision' (allow/block) attribute uniformly on a 'hook.decision' span for ALL six hooks, but SessionStart and PreCompact never make an allow/block gate decision (always exit 0), so decision=allow/block is degenerate; and requirement.id is ambiguous for SessionStart (tagged with BOTH REQ-STATE-003 and REQ-STATE-005 per design.md:219). Specify the attribute set per hook class: for non-gating hooks (SessionStart, PreCompact) emit an informational span (distinct name e.g. 'hook.session_start' or decision='n/a') and either omit requirement.id or list both ids explicitly, rather than a gate-decision allow/block attribute — so the trace schema reflects which hooks actually gate. (REQ-OBS-003 / criterion 12.3 covers only true gate decisions.)

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the resumed-state hash inputs / algorithm / baseline-store location
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 26 (design.md:1088-1090), Hook Wiring SessionStart row (design.md:219), state_integrity.py inventory + 'New / changed components' note (design.md:143, design.md:1180); Requirement 23 (requirements.md:437-438)
confirmed_edits.change: Neither requirements.md nor design.md defines WHICH state is hashed (feature_list.json items only? + run_state fields? + git HEAD?), the hash algorithm, or where the baseline 'durable store's recorded hash' it is compared against is persisted — yet SessionStart is credited with the compute. Add a short spec near Property 26 / the state_integrity.py note: exact byte-set (canonical JSON of in_scope feature_list.json items + named run_state fields), algorithm sha256 (consistent with the evidence/audit-log sha256 convention), and the run_state column holding the baseline hash, so SessionStart's compute is implementable from the spec alone.

confirmed_edits.title: Update glossary: SessionStart_Hook responsibilities + add a Progress_File term
confirmed_edits.anchor: Glossary entry 'SessionStart_Hook' (requirements.md:27) and the glossary block (requirements.md:17-48) which defines Coverage_Model (L19) and feature_list.json (L28) but no progress-file term
confirmed_edits.change: Two glossary gaps: (1) the SessionStart_Hook entry still reads only 'restores run state at the start of a session' — append '...and computes the resumed-state integrity hash, writing run_state.resume_integrity_ok (non-blocking; enforced by the PreToolUse integrity guard, Requirement 23)'. (2) criterion 11.3 (requirements.md:221) names 'the progress file' as a first-class load target, but no Progress_File glossary term exists (Coverage_Model and feature_list.json do). Add a glossary entry: 'Progress_File: the version-controlled claude-progress.txt checkpoint that SessionStart reads and PreCompact writes.'

confirmed_edits.title: Pin the SessionStart stdout contract so the two phase-0 tests assert the same fidelity
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 7.1 'Inject combined summary into Claude Code context via stdout' (tasks.md:167); smoke test 16.1 'stdout contains git/progress/coverage summary' (tasks.md:286); integration test 17.1 'stdout JSON contains git_status, unproven_count, proven_count' (tasks.md:298)
confirmed_edits.change: Task 7.1 says 'inject combined summary via stdout' without pinning the output shape, so 16.1 asserts a free-text 'contains' summary while 17.1 asserts structured JSON fields (git_status, unproven_count, proven_count) — two different fidelities for the same hook output. Pin the contract in 7.1 (emit structured JSON with named fields git_status, unproven_count, proven_count) and align 16.1 to assert the same JSON fields (or explicitly state 16.1 is the looser smoke check by design), so the output contract is single-sourced.

confirmed_edits.title: Note REQ-STATE-003 base load is example-test-verified only (no invariant)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 26 block (design.md:1084-1090) and the Hook Wiring SessionStart row (design.md:219); REQ-STATE-003 / criterion 11.3 (requirements.md:221)
confirmed_edits.change: The reconciliation-added integrity half has Property 26 + CHECK-11, but the component's PRIMARY purpose — REQ-STATE-003 base load (git status + progress + coverage so a session re-orients) — has NO correctness Property and NO Z3 CHECK; it is exercised only by example tests (7.2, 16.1, 17.1). Either add a property ('for any populated feature_list.json, the SessionStart summary reports the exact unproven/proven counts') or add a one-line design note that REQ-STATE-003 is intentionally example-test-verified only (no invariant), so the absence is documented rather than an oversight.

confirmed_edits.title: Cover where SessionStart writes resume_integrity_ok when Postgres is unavailable (N-25)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Notes bullet N-25 'Postgres-unavailable fallback gate-consistency' (tasks.md:730), which enumerates 'Stop coverage gate, PreToolUse status/integrity guards, OPA merge policy' but not SessionStart's write of resume_integrity_ok
confirmed_edits.change: N-25 mandates file-backed fallback for the read-side gates but is silent on SessionStart's WRITE of run_state.resume_integrity_ok (a Postgres run_state column per design.md:492) when health_check() fails at session start. If Postgres is down at SessionStart, the spec does not say whether the flag is written to the claude-progress.txt fallback or skipped — yet the downstream PreToolUse integrity guard reads it. Extend N-25 to state where SessionStart records resume_integrity_ok under DB-unavailable (write to the file-backed run_state duplicate, reconcile on reconnect), keeping the conservative/blocking behavior.

refuted.item: ears[2] / Requirement 19.1 (REQ-PRED-001): wire SessionStart as a predictive-routing advisory context-injection channel
refuted.why: REFUTED — not a gap. requirements.md:340 names SessionStart only as ONE allowable advisory channel for the OPTIONAL, off-gate Phase-6 predictive-routing feature ('e.g., via a UserPromptSubmit or SessionStart context-injection hook or an MCP retrieve tool'). The finding itself concedes 'No mechanism is added to session_start_hook.py... it is an off-gate optional usage, not a Phase-0 obligation.' Phase 6 is explicitly optional/out-of-v1-scope (requirements.md:11, 517; design.md Phase table). There is nothing for the Phase-0 session_start_hook.py to wire, so no edit is warranted.

refuted.item: open_gaps[1] (untasked obligation) treated as a finding separate from open_gaps[0]

confirmed_edits.title: Scope the base evidence-schema gate to verifier results (skip-when-no-evidence)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table row: `| `SubagentStop` | Subagent result returned | Validates Evidence_Record schema (all four fields present and non-empty); calls `audit_log.append(...)` ... | REQ-VERIFY-005, REQ-COV-006, REQ-AUDIT-001 |` (design.md:217)
confirmed_edits.change: Clarify that the base evidence-schema gate applies ONLY to subagent results that CARRY an `evidence` field (verifier results): the hook locates evidence field(s) and, when none are present (initializer/research results, which produce specs/checklists not Evidence_Records), it SKIPS the four-field validation rather than failing closed. State explicitly that REQ-VERIFY-005's 'required evidence markers' for NON-verifier subagents are enforced by the omission-declaration guard (omission_declaration field), not the four-field schema. Mirror this in tasks.md task 8.1 (add a 'skip when result has no evidence field' bullet). This resolves the universal-over-'a subagent's result' wording (requirements.md:190) vs the verifier-only schema gate (design.md:217).

confirmed_edits.title: Author a per-subagent output schema so REQ-SPEC-018 (29.3) has a concrete mechanism
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory rows for `omission_guard` (design.md:148) and `schema/` (design.md:138); plus tasks.md task 54 'add omission_declaration as a required non-nullable field to each subagent output schema (initializer, research, verifier)' (tasks.md:974)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a `schema/subagent_output.schema.json` (or per-role schemas) to the Component Inventory and a task to author it, declaring `omission_declaration` as a required, non-nullable field per requirement 29.3 (requirements.md:478). Define the subagent-result ENVELOPE once in the Data Models section with documented top-level keys (`evidence[]`, `omission_declaration`, `actor_agent`, `requirement_id`) so BOTH the evidence-schema gate and the omission_guard read the same documented JSON path from the stdin payload. Reference this schema from subagent_stop_hook.py. This closes the gap that 29.3 mandates a schema field with no schema artifact to validate against, AND the field-location drift (no canonical statement of WHERE omission_declaration lives) between task 8.1 and task 54.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Z3 evidence-at-SubagentStop-acceptance assertion (CHECK-7d)
confirmed_edits.anchor: evidence_schema_axioms block `Implies(passed, evidenceComplete)` (line 308) and CHECK-7a/7c (lines 312-338); model the SubagentStop omission gate at lines 481-500 (subagentAccepted13)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a CHECK-7d-style assertion that machine-checks the four-field evidence schema at the SubagentStop ACCEPTANCE boundary, symmetric with the omission gate (CHECK-13a). Introduce a `subagentAcceptedEvidence` (or reuse a SubagentStop-acceptance) bool and axiom `Implies(Not(evidenceComplete), Not(subagentAcceptedEvidence))`, then assert `(Not(evidenceComplete) AND subagentAcceptedEvidence)` is UNSAT. Currently the schema is only tied to the `passed` transition variable via `Implies(passed, evidenceComplete)`, never to the SubagentStop accept decision — so this hook's Phase-0 raison d'etre is not formally checked at its own gate. Update the harness self-count/group comments and the requirements.md/design.md '34 assertions' figures accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix omission_guard phase label (Phase 0 -> phase of task 54)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory row `| `omission_guard` | (capability within `.claude/hooks/subagent_stop_hook.py`) | 0 | Omission-declaration gate ... |` (design.md:148); subagent_stop_hook.py inventory row labelled '0/1' (design.md:130)
confirmed_edits.change: Re-label the `omission_guard` Phase from `0` to the phase of task 54, which is scheduled in dependency-graph wave 45 (tasks.md:923, :974) — effectively post-Phase-4, NOT Phase 0. Split/annotate the subagent_stop_hook.py inventory row (design.md:130, currently '0/1') so the Phase-0 deliverable is strictly the base evidence-schema validator (task 8), while the omission_guard (task 54), the audit_log.append wiring (task 52.3, Phase 2/3), and the OTel hook.decision span (task 36.1, Phase 3) are flagged as LATER additions. The hook is genuinely a 0/2/3 multi-phase component and the single '0/1' label understates this.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a dedicated design subsection assembling the full subagent_stop_hook.py obligation set
confirmed_edits.anchor: No dedicated subsection exists for subagent_stop_hook.py (contrast the 'No-Progress Watchdog' subsection at design.md:640 which fully narrates stop_hook.py); only inventory line :130 + wiring rows :217-218
confirmed_edits.change: Add a dedicated design subsection for subagent_stop_hook.py (parallel to 'No-Progress Watchdog') specifying: the stdin parse contract; skip-when-no-evidence semantics; ordering of the omission_guard check vs the evidence-schema check; the audit_log.append call site/ordering; and exception handling (exit 2 fail-closed). Currently the hook's full obligation set is scattered across tasks 8, 52.3, and 54 with no single assembling task, and task 8.1 tags only requirement 9.5. Update tasks.md task 8.1 to also reference REQ-COV-006, REQ-SPEC-018, and REQ-AUDIT-001, and cross-link tasks 52.3 and 54 as completing the same component.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify audit_log.append is called inside the decision path before exit (ordering)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 52.3 'Wire audit-log producer into the three hooks — modify stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py to call tools/audit_log.append(...) on every allow/block decision' (tasks.md:970); and the Postgres-fallback note (tasks.md:730)
confirmed_edits.change: Specify in task 52.3 (and the new design subsection) that for subagent_stop_hook.py the audit_log.append call is invoked INSIDE the decision path BEFORE the exit, wrapped so an append failure degrades to the file-backed log (per the tasks.md:730 fallback) and NEVER suppresses or skips the gate decision/exit. Add a unit test: 'audit_log.append raises (e.g. Postgres down before file-fallback engages) -> the allow/block decision is still recorded to the file-backed log AND the hook exits with the correct code'. Without this, a fail-closed unhandled-exception exit 2 (task 8.1 / tasks.md:182) could fire before any audit entry is written, breaking REQ-AUDIT-001's 'on every allow/block decision' obligation (requirements.md:460).

confirmed_edits.title: Enforce Property 24 (actor_agent != implementer) at the SubagentStop acceptance gate
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 24 (design.md:1066-1070); evidence_records.actor_agent column with 'MUST be the Verifier, never the Implementer' (design.md:473); SubagentStop wiring row (design.md:217); PBT task 39.13 (tasks.md:652-656)
confirmed_edits.change: Add an actor_agent runtime check to subagent_stop_hook.py: BLOCK (exit 2) any evidence-bearing subagent result whose `actor_agent` is `implementer.md`, enforcing Property 24 ('implementer cannot self-verify') at the acceptance choke point rather than only at the DB column (design.md:473) + property test (task 39.13). Document this in the SubagentStop wiring row (design.md:217) and add it to task 8.1 / the new design subsection. The SubagentStop gate is the natural runtime enforcement point and currently nothing tasks the hook with this check.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix non-canonical field name acting_agent in Property 24 PBT (task 39.13)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 39.13 'Generate evidence chains with arbitrary `acting_agent` identities; assert any Evidence_Record where `acting_agent == "implementer.md"` is rejected' (tasks.md:655)
confirmed_edits.change: Rename `acting_agent` to `actor_agent` in task 39.13 (both occurrences). design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' and Property 24 (design.md:1068) uses `actor_agent`, but the validating PBT in tasks.md:655 still generates and rejects on the non-canonical `acting_agent` key — so the role-separation test would read the wrong field and fail to enforce the gate. This is a live naming-drift defect.

confirmed_edits.title: Map REQ-STEER-004 (command-type fail-closed) as an explicit obligation on this hook
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 9.1 registers SubagentStop as "type":"command" (tasks.md:193-194); task 9.2 smoke test asserts all hooks command-type (tasks.md:197-199); requirement 13.4/REQ-STEER-004 (requirements.md:253)
confirmed_edits.change: Wire REQ-STEER-004 (command-type hooks fail closed; no HTTP/MCP for hard policy, requirements.md:253) explicitly onto subagent_stop_hook.py: add the requirement tag to the SubagentStop registration (task 9.1) and reference the task-9.2 smoke test (tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py) which parses settings.json and asserts EVERY registered hook including SubagentStop is command-type with no http/mcp. This is the reason the hook can fail closed (exit 2) at all; currently it is only an un-tagged behavior note.

confirmed_edits.title: Single out SubagentStop in the Property 21 exit-code-contract test
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 39.4 'Write property test for hook exit-code contract' / test_hook_exit_code_contract, generic over hooks (tasks.md:593-597); Property 21 (design.md:1042-1046); requirement 13.5 (requirements.md:254)
confirmed_edits.change: Extend task 39.4 (Property 21, Hook Exit-Code Contract) to explicitly include a SubagentStop case: assert the hook's allow(exit 0)/block(exit 2 with stderr)/fail-closed-on-exception(exit 2) behavior conforms to REQ-STEER-005 (requirements.md:254). The exit-code contract is the foundation of this hook's entire allow/block behavior, yet the validating test (test_hook_exit_code_contract) is generic across hooks and does not single out SubagentStop, leaving its exit-code conformance only transitively covered.

refuted.item: open_gap #8 — Field-location drift: no canonical statement of WHERE omission_declaration lives in the result JSON; the two guards parse the same stdin with no shared envelope.

Emitting it separately would double-count the same artifact change.

No edit needed.

The artifacts are already internally consistent: design.md:215 maps REQ-COV-006 to the PreToolUse status guard (primary) and design.md:217 maps it to SubagentStop (secondary re-use of the same validate_evidence_record semantics). Both enforcement points are correctly documented. This is a critique of the DRAFT's citation altitude, not a defect in requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md. No artifact change is warranted.

refuted.item: citation_issue — design.md:37 wrong content (Mermaid node, not prose); PRD:521 phantom line (PRD has 133 lines); tasks.md:193-194 misattributed command string; tasks.md:956 imprecise hook-validator-timeout citation.

Verified: PRD_MERGED is 133 lines so PRD:521 is indeed a phantom, but the real text lives at requirements.md:521 (and PRD:44) which ARE correct; design.md:37 is a Mermaid label as stated; the command string only appears at design.md:184; requirements.md:354 correctly lists the 60s hook-validator timeout. None of these require changing requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md/harness — they require fixing the draft's citations, which is out of scope for the canonical-artifact edit set.

confirmed_edits.title: REQ-VERIFY-004 under-specifies the PostToolUse check set (omits type-check AND AST)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 9, acceptance criterion 4 (requirements.md:189): "WHEN a file edit completes, THE PostToolUse_Hook SHALL run lint, SAST, and wiring checks..."
confirmed_edits.change: Amend criterion 9.4 to enumerate the full check set so requirement, design hook-wiring row (design.md:216), and task 18.1 (tasks.md:311) agree: change 'run lint, SAST, and wiring checks' to 'run lint, type-check (mypy), SAST (Semgrep), and wiring checks'. This reconciles the requirement with design.md:216 ('lint, type check, SAST (Semgrep), wiring check') and task 18.1 which runs ruff+mypy+semgrep+wiring_checker. Note that the structural layer's AST analysis (REQ-VERIFY-001) is performed inside wiring_checker.py via the ast module (task 19.1, tasks.md:327), so the wiring check subsumes AST — state this so 'lint+type+SAST+wiring' is not misread as omitting the structural-layer AST element.

confirmed_edits.title: PostToolUse Semgrep-only scope (CodeQL is CI-only) is nowhere stated
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table, PostToolUse row (design.md:216): "Runs lint, type check, SAST (Semgrep), wiring check on changed files"
confirmed_edits.change: Add a clause to the PostToolUse hook-wiring row (and mirror in task 18.1, tasks.md:312) stating the in-loop hook runs Semgrep ONLY for SAST; CodeQL is the CI gate (task 23 / REQ-SEC-001), not run at edit time. This prevents the glossary term Semgrep_CodeQL (requirements.md:47) and REQ-VERIFY-001 (requirements.md:186) from being misread as requiring the hook to run CodeQL. Suggested: append '(Semgrep only at edit time; CodeQL runs in CI per REQ-SEC-001)'.

confirmed_edits.title: No dedicated PostToolUse design subsection: changed-file parsing, stdout feedback schema, and MultiEdit handling unspecified
confirmed_edits.anchor: After the '`plan-approved.json` Gate' subsection (ends ~design.md:801), before '## Error Handling' (design.md:805) — mirror depth of 'No-Progress Watchdog' / 'plan-approved.json Gate'
confirmed_edits.change: Add a 'PostToolUse Feedback Hook' design subsection (parallel to the Stop-hook and PreToolUse sections) defining: (a) how changed-file path(s) are parsed from the PostToolUse event payload for Write/Edit/MultiEdit — every sibling hook documents its input parsing (e.g. subagent_stop_hook task 8.1, tasks.md:178 'Parse subagent result from stdin (JSON)'; pre_tool_use reads tool input) but task 18.1 (tasks.md:309-316) uniquely says only 'on changed files' with no stdin-parsing step; (b) the JSON/text schema of the structured feedback emitted to stdout that the agent consumes next turn; (c) how a MultiEdit batch (multiple files in one event) is fanned out to the per-file lint/type/SAST/wiring runs. Also add a matching bullet to task 18.1 spelling out the stdin payload parse step.

confirmed_edits.title: No timeout specified for PostToolUse despite shelling out to four external processes
confirmed_edits.anchor: Error Handling > Hook Failure Modes table, '`post_tool_use_hook.py` crashes' row (design.md:813)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a timeout behavior for post_tool_use_hook.py. The Req 20.1 60s validator timeout (requirements.md:354) is documented only for spec_validator.py (design.md:814) and silent for this hook, yet semgrep --config auto on a large changeset can exceed 60s. Add a Hook Failure Modes row (or extend the existing post_tool_use_hook row) and a task 18.1 bullet: apply the 60s validator-timeout default per external tool (or a dedicated post-hook budget); on timeout still exit 1 with a 'post-hook timeout' finding in the structured feedback — never exit 2, never block. This is also the concrete site to connect the N-25 'never fail-open' family note (tasks.md:730): when telemetry/DB is down (task 36.1 OTel forwarding) the hook MUST still exit 1 and not crash the edit loop.

confirmed_edits.title: REQ-OBS-003 gate-decision forwarding mis-fits PostToolUse (it makes no allow/block decision)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 36.1 (tasks.md:539-542): "Extend all six hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact) to emit OTel gate-decision events" with attribute `decision` (allow/block)
confirmed_edits.change: PostToolUse never makes an allow/block gate decision (it always exits 1, non-blocking), but task 36.1 sets a `decision`(allow/block) attribute on its span for 'all six hooks' and REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236) is triggered only 'WHEN a hook makes a gate decision'. Fix: in task 36.1 (and the design narrative) emit a distinct span event for PostToolUse — e.g. span name 'hook.feedback' with attributes `hook.event`, `tool.name`, `findings_count` (and severity breakdown) — instead of reusing the allow/block `decision` attribute that is meaningless for a non-gating hook. Clarify that REQ-OBS-003's gate-decision forwarding applies to the five gate-making hooks; PostToolUse forwards informational feedback telemetry, not an audit-logged gate decision.

confirmed_edits.title: PostToolUse intentionally NOT an audit-log producer — state it explicitly to reconcile with OTel forwarding
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory, post_tool_use_hook.py row (design.md:131); cross-ref audit producer list at design.md:144 (audit_log.py) and req 27.4 (requirements.md:460)
confirmed_edits.change: Req 27.4 (requirements.md:460) and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) wire audit_log.append into stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py only — PostToolUse is deliberately excluded — yet task 36.1 forwards PostToolUse events to OTel, so the audit log and the OTel trace appear to disagree on whether PostToolUse events are recorded. State explicitly (in the design.md:131 inventory row or the audit_log.py row at design.md:144) that PostToolUse is intentionally NOT a gate-audit-log producer because it issues no allow/block gate decision, and that its OTel forwarding is informational feedback telemetry (paired with the 'hook.feedback' span from the OBS-003 fix), not a gate-decision audit entry.

confirmed_edits.title: Zero Z3 coverage for the PostToolUse never-blocks invariant
confirmed_edits.anchor: New CHECK in the 'New checks' section after CHECK-13b (formal_verification_merged.py:500), parallel to the HANDOFF exit-0 checks CHECK-5b/5c/8c (lines 266-275, 364-370)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a CHECK pinning the PostToolUse 'cannot block' invariant, parallel to CHECK-5b/5c/8c which pin the Stop-hook HANDOFF exit-0 invariant. Confirmed gap: grep of formal_verification_merged.py for post_tool_use/postHook/lint/sast/wiring returns NO MATCH; the invariant 'PostToolUse MUST exit 1 / never exit 2 / cannot undo' (requirements.md:251, REQ-STEER-002) lives only in prose plus the generic Property 21. Add e.g. a Bool `postHookBlocks` with axiom `Not(postHookBlocks)` (PostToolUse is non-blocking by contract) and a check that `postHookBlocks` (a PostToolUse path that exits 2) is UNSAT — formally pinning the prevention(PreToolUse)/feedback(PostToolUse) split on both sides. Update the self-counting group comment/total accordingly (the harness self-counts checks_run, so no hardcoded figure needs changing, but add the new CHECK label to the printed group description).

confirmed_edits.title: Req 8.1 wiring-analysis EARS story not mapped to the PostToolUse trigger role
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table, PostToolUse row (design.md:216), 'Requirement enforced' column currently 'REQ-VERIFY-004, REQ-STEER-002'
confirmed_edits.change: Add REQ-EXEC-010 (Req 8.1) to the PostToolUse hook-wiring row's enforced-requirement list and note the trigger/engine split: PostToolUse invokes wiring_checker.py on changed files (task 18.1, tasks.md:313) which performs the static call-graph/dead-code analysis and emits WIRING coverage-item candidates (task 19, tasks.md:325-331). The hook is the trigger; wiring_checker is the engine. Integration test 26.1 (tasks.md:404-410) already exercises the hook+wiring_checker path together under Req 8.1/8.2, so the mapping is testably bound — make it explicit in the wiring row rather than leaving Req 8.1 traced only through the wiring_checker engine task.

confirmed_edits.title: Req 20.1 SAST-threshold EARS story: PostToolUse reports HIGH/CRITICAL but does not enforce — mapping unstated
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table, PostToolUse row (design.md:216), 'What it blocks' column currently 'Nothing (exit 1 = non-blocking)'
confirmed_edits.change: Clarify the Req 20.1 (0 HIGH/CRITICAL SAST findings) mapping for PostToolUse: the hook filters semgrep output for HIGH/CRITICAL findings (task 18.1, tasks.md:312) but only REPORTS them via next-turn feedback (exit 1) — it does NOT enforce/block on the 0-HIGH/CRITICAL threshold. Enforcement of that threshold is the CI SAST gate (REQ-SEC-001, CodeQL/Semgrep workflows, task 23). Add a clause to the PostToolUse 'What it blocks' cell (and a task 18.1 bullet) stating PostToolUse surfaces SAST findings as feedback only; the 0-HIGH/CRITICAL threshold (Req 20.1) is enforced at the CI gate, not at edit time.

refuted.item: critic_findings[0] — Property 21's _Requirements line (tasks.md:591) names '18.1', wiring the PBT to post_tool_use_hook.py via task 18.1
refuted.why: Misread. tasks.md:591 is the _Requirements trace for task 39.3 (property tests for hooks) and lists REQUIREMENT acceptance-criterion ids '5.5, 7.4, 7.5, 10.2, 13.5, 14.1, 14.2, 18.1' — '18.1' here is Requirement 18.1 (REQ-HITL-001, plan-approval gate), NOT task 18.1 (the post_tool_use_hook implementation task). refuted.item: ears_needing_work — Req 12.3 (hook-decision forwarding) add-wiring
refuted.why: DUPLICATE of the REQ-OBS-003 mis-fit edit (dedup_key post-hook-otel-feedback-vs-decision). refuted.why: Citation-precision note, not an artifact defect.
