confirmed_edits.title: Resolve PreCompact phase contradiction (Phase 1 vs Phase 2)
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:132 row `| `pre_compact_hook.py` | `.claude/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py` | 2 |` AND design.md:15 `| **Phase 2 (durable state)** | Postgres traceability graph + PreCompact checkpoints + SLSA attestations |`
confirmed_edits.change: Pick ONE phase for pre_compact_hook.py. The build task (task 25) sits under '### Phase 1 — Verification Depth' (tasks.md:306, tasks 18-26), Notes line 725 bounds 'Phase 1 task (18-26)', and the wave graph schedules 25.1 in wave 17 (tasks.md:810-812) between Phase-1 wave 16 (21.2-24.1) and Phase-2 wave 19 (27.1). Since PreCompact depends only on file+git artifacts (no Postgres), make it Phase 1: change design.md:132 '2'->'1' and remove 'PreCompact checkpoints' from the Phase-2 overview row (design.md:15). Alternatively move task 25 into Phase 2 (after task 26) to match design.md — but the wave graph + Notes already commit it to Phase 1, so renaming design.md is the lower-churn fix.

confirmed_edits.title: Reconcile the checkpointed artifact set across the three specs (and remove the orphan snapshot)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:392-394 (task 25.1 bullets: append claude-progress.txt; write claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json; `git add -f ... && git commit`) vs design.md:220 (PreCompact row 'Checkpoints claude-progress.txt, current evidence state, and feature_list.json') vs requirements.md:219-220
confirmed_edits.change: Make the file list identical in tasks.md:392-394 and design.md:220, and ensure every written file has a reader. As written, tasks.md commits claude-progress.txt + claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json but NOT feature_list.json, while design.md:220 commits feature_list.json and never names the snapshot; the snapshot filename appears exactly once in the whole spec (tasks.md:393 write) and is read by nothing — SessionStart (tasks.md:165-166) reads only claude-progress.txt + feature_list.json. Recommended fix: drop claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json from tasks.md:393 and instead commit feature_list.json directly (so SessionStart re-orients from it per design.md:219); add feature_list.json to the `git add`/commit at tasks.md:394; set design.md:220 to the same set {claude-progress.txt, feature_list.json}. If the derived snapshot is kept, add it to design.md:220 AND give it a reader in session_start_hook.py (task 7.1).

confirmed_edits.title: Glossary PreCompact_Hook definition narrower than its EARS criterion
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:26 `- **PreCompact_Hook**: The Claude Code `PreCompact` hook that checkpoints progress before context compaction.`
confirmed_edits.change: Widen the glossary entry to match criterion REQ-STATE-002 (requirements.md:220 'checkpoint progress AND EVIDENCE records'): change 'checkpoints progress before context compaction' to 'checkpoints progress and evidence records before context compaction.' The glossary currently summarizes the hook as progress-only, contradicting its own criterion and the design.md:220 set.

confirmed_edits.title: Add pre_compact_hook.py row to the Hook Failure Modes table + pin the commit worktree
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:809-816 Hook Failure Modes table (currently covers stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, post_tool_use_hook.py, spec_validator.py, Postgres, plan-approved.json — no PreCompact row)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a `pre_compact_hook.py` failure-mode row: 'On any exception (git index lock, detached HEAD, mid-rebase, unreadable feature_list.json), log to stderr and exit 0 — a failed checkpoint must NEVER block the run (non-blocking by REQ-STATE-002).' Also specify which ref/worktree the checkpoint commits to: the MAIN worktree, never a per-slice worktree, so the `chore: PreCompact checkpoint [skip ci]` commit (tasks.md:394) cannot collide mid-slice with the one-atomic-commit-per-slice invariant (REQ-EXEC-003 / requirements.md:158) or the implementer's 'exactly one atomic commit' rule (design.md:248). Note the checkpoint commit carries no requirement-ID trailer and is exempt from the REQ-TRACE-002 commit-trailer gate (it is a chore, not a slice commit).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix settings.json owning-file path drift and stale task cross-reference for PreCompact binding
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:16 `Create `.vscode/settings.json` (or `.kiro/settings.json`) stub for hook wiring (populated in task 8)`
confirmed_edits.change: Two defects on this line: (1) 'populated in task 8' is wrong — task 8 is subagent_stop_hook.py (tasks.md:176); the hook-wiring task that registers PreCompact is task 9 (tasks.md:191-193). Change to 'populated in task 9'. (2) The stub path `.vscode/settings.json` is both inconsistent with task 9.1 / design.md:157 (`.claude/settings.json`) AND actively gitignored (.gitignore:32 ignores `.vscode/`), so a PreCompact binding placed there would never be committed and the runtime would never load it (silent fail-open wiring hole). Standardize the path to `.claude/settings.json` at tasks.md:16. The actual PreCompact command binding `python3 .claude/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py` is specified at design.md:193-198 and registered by task 9.1.

confirmed_edits.title: Drop misleading `git add -f` on non-gitignored checkpoint files (or add the intended .gitignore entry)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:394 `Commit both files via `git add -f ... && git commit -m "chore: PreCompact checkpoint [skip ci]"` if anything has changed`
confirmed_edits.change: The `-f` (force-add) implies claude-progress.txt / the snapshot are gitignored, but the actual .gitignore (lines 1-47) ignores only __pycache__, venvs, dist/build, test/coverage caches, .idea//.vscode/, OS files, .playwright-mcp/, and .env* — there is NO claude-progress entry. Resolve the intent mismatch: either (a) drop the `-f` so it reads `git add claude-progress.txt feature_list.json` (the files are tracked normally), or (b) if checkpoint files are meant to be ignored, add them to .gitignore and make task 1 (tasks.md:17 'Create ... .gitignore entries') actually create that entry — which justifies the `-f`. Recommend (a) given SessionStart must read feature_list.json back.

confirmed_edits.title: PreCompact wrongly included in gate-decision OTel forwarding (makes no allow/block decision)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:539-541 task 36.1 `Extend all six hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact) to emit OTel gate-decision events ... attributes: hook.event, tool.name, decision (allow/block), reason, requirement.id`
confirmed_edits.change: PreCompact makes no gate decision — it always exits 0, has no allow/block, no requirement.id, and the design.md:220 PreCompact row explicitly lists 'Nothing (checkpoint write; non-blocking)' with no audit_log.append (unlike Stop:209, PreToolUse:210, SubagentStop:217-218 which DO call audit_log.append). REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236) scopes the event to 'WHEN a hook makes a gate decision'. Fix task 36.1: either (a) exclude PreCompact from gate-decision forwarding and emit a distinct non-gate span for it (e.g. `hook.checkpoint` with item-count attributes, no decision/requirement.id), or (b) state that for PreCompact the `decision` attribute is a fixed checkpoint marker (not allow/block) and requirement.id is omitted. Update the ears_story for Requirement 12.3 accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: REQ-STATE-002 has no Z3 check and no Correctness Property — state the verification basis explicitly
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:220 PreCompact row (REQ-STATE-002) AND the Correctness Properties list (design.md ~1084-1130, which contains Property 26 for REQ-STATE-005 but no PreCompact/REQ-STATE-002 property)
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-STATE-002 is the sole reason this component exists yet is verified by nothing in formal_verification_merged.py (a grep shows only the CHECK-11 family covers REQ-STATE, and that maps to REQ-STATE-005, not 002) and by no Correctness Property. This is defensible (a non-blocking checkpoint has no SAT/UNSAT gating invariant) but must be stated, not silent. Add a one-line note near the PreCompact design row / Property list: 'REQ-STATE-002 is verified by unit + integration test (task 25.2), not Z3 — a checkpoint write has no gating invariant.' Optionally add an integration test asserting that after a simulated PreCompact the checkpoint files + commit exist and SessionStart reads them back.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire REQ-STATE-001 (persist run state outside model context) to PreCompact as a contributing writer
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:220 PreCompact row (Requirement enforced column currently lists only REQ-STATE-002)
confirmed_edits.change: PreCompact partially realizes REQ-STATE-001 (requirements.md:219: 'persist all mutable run state outside model context, in files, git history') — it is the write-side that persists progress + evidence to files and git before compaction. The current PreCompact row maps only REQ-STATE-002. Add REQ-STATE-001 to the PreCompact row's Requirement-enforced column (as a contributing writer alongside REQ-STATE-002) so the file+git persistence obligation is traced to this component, not only to the durable-Postgres path. (Note: this is a partial mapping — Postgres persistence under REQ-STATE-001 is covered by Phase 2 tasks 31.x.)

confirmed_edits.title: Relabel the REQ-STATE-001 ears-story's 11.4 reference (per-slice commit, not PreCompact)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:18 task 1 `_Requirements: 11.1, 11.4_`
confirmed_edits.change: When mapping PreCompact's contribution to durable state, do NOT cite criterion 11.4 as a PreCompact/claude-progress.txt anchor: 11.4 (requirements.md:222) is 'record an incremental commit per completed Slice as the durable execution trail' — owned by the implementer's per-slice commit, NOT by PreCompact. PreCompact's genuine REQ-STATE anchors are 11.1 (persist outside model context) and 11.2 (the checkpoint itself). Keep task 1's tag `11.1, 11.4` as-is (task 1 legitimately scaffolds claude-progress.txt for 11.1 and the git/.gitignore trail relevant to 11.4), but in any PreCompact coverage note label the 11.4 link as 'task 1's scaffolding tag, unrelated to PreCompact' so no false PreCompact->11.4 coverage edge is implied.

refuted.item: Critic 'overstatement': task 1's `_Requirements: 11.1, 11.4_` tag is itself wrong and should drop the 11.4 reference.
refuted.why: The task-1 tag is defensible and need not change. Task 1 (tasks.md:13-18) scaffolds the repo: it creates claude-progress.txt (serving 11.1) AND the git scaffold + .gitignore entries that underpin 11.4's 'incremental commit per completed Slice ... durable execution trail.' The only real defect is using 11.4 as a *PreCompact* coverage anchor in the ears-story, which is captured by the separate relabel edit (dedup_key unique:reqstate-001-114-mislabel). The tag on task 1 stays.

refuted.item: Implied gap: REQ-STATE-005 / Property 26 / CHECK-11 are mishandled for PreCompact.
refuted.why: No finding claims this, and the artifacts handle it correctly: REQ-STATE-005 is owned by state_integrity.py + the PreToolUse integrity guard (design.md:143, :212, :219, :492, Property 26 at design.md:1084-1090, CHECK-11a/11b in formal_verification_merged.py:444-461) — entirely separate from PreCompact. The absence of a Z3 check for REQ-STATE-002 is the genuine gap (captured by dedup_key unique:reqstate002-no-z3); REQ-STATE-005 coverage is intact and needs no edit.

refuted.item: Critic 'weak': the draft's .gitignore citation '.gitignore:41-47 / OS files' is inaccurate and mislocates the IDE/.vscode block.
refuted.why: This is a citation-correction about the DRAFT findings file itself, not a defect in the canonical artifacts (requirements/design/tasks/verification). The substantive claim it carries (claude-progress.txt is not gitignored, so `git add -f` is misleading) is already CONFIRMED and captured by dedup_key precompact-gitadd-f-gitignore; the .vscode/-is-gitignored fact is captured by settings-json-precompact-binding. Nothing further to edit in the canonical set.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the producer that copies validate_spec() violation_count into run_state.violation_count
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:231-232 initializer Key behaviors: 'Runs spec_validator.py after every elaboration pass' / 'Loops (bounded to DEFAULT=7 passes) until violation_count == 0'
confirmed_edits.change: Add an explicit producer step to the Initializer key-behaviors (design.md:231-232) AND to Task 10.1 (tasks.md:204): on each elaboration pass call save_run_state(run_state with violation_count = validate_spec(...)['violation_count']) — including the -1 timeout sentinel — so the Stop hook's run_state.violation_count (design.md:490, read at design.md:722-725, REQ-SPEC-021) has a specified writer. Currently validate_spec RETURNS violation_count and stop_hook READS run_state.violation_count, but no step wires producer→consumer (Task 31.1 only wires save_run_state mechanically, never with the validator's output).

confirmed_edits.title: Reconcile spec 7-pass cap vs 25-turn slice cap — Stop hook keys HANDOFF on the wrong counter for the lex-specialis tiebreak
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:699 'if run_state.iteration_count >= MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE' vs design.md:842 / requirements.md:110 keying on 'iteration_count < SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP'
confirmed_edits.change: Introduce a distinct run_state field spec_pass_count (separate from iteration_count which counts the 25-turn MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE) and add a Stop-hook (or Initializer-loop) HANDOFF branch firing when spec_pass_count >= SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP (7), independent of the MAX_TURNS_PER_SLICE check at design.md:699. Fix design.md:842 and requirements.md:110 (Req 4.6) which currently say the exit-2 block applies while 'iteration_count < SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP' — iteration_count is the 25-turn counter (design.md:487), so as written the 7-pass lex-specialis tiebreak is not realizable. Name the same counter in the tiebreak that the cap-HANDOFF branch checks.

confirmed_edits.title: Define dangling REQ-SPEC-020..024 / REQ-SPEC-021 EARS statements or replace with canonical Req 4.x numbers
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:101 'PRD references: REQ-SPEC-020..024' (Requirement 4); REQ-SPEC-021 referenced at design.md:209,718,726,842 but never EARS-defined
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-SPEC-020..024 appear ONLY as PRD references (requirements.md:101, 385) and REQ-SPEC-021 is cited 4x in design.md as the Stop-hook spec-gate requirement but is never defined as an EARS statement anywhere. Either (a) add explicit REQ-SPEC-020..024 EARS definitions under Requirement 4 mapping REQ-SPEC-021 = criterion 4.2 (the violation_count>0 Stop-block), or (b) replace the dangling REQ-SPEC-0xx citations in design.md:209/718/726/842 with the canonical Requirement 4.2/4.6 criterion numbers, removing the dangling references.

confirmed_edits.title: Replace harness-exit proxy test-to-pass with a direct validate_spec() fixture assertion
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:632 row 10 Test to pass: 'python3 formal_verification_merged.py exits 0 (all 34 checks pass)'
confirmed_edits.change: The Phase-0 test-to-pass for spec_validator.py (design.md:632, component #10) is the STATIC 34-assertion harness exit code; the harness contains zero validate_spec/spec_validator references (grep confirms), so it never exercises the NLP parse, EARS encoding, the four consistency/completeness/vacuity/independence checks, or the four-field return. Change the test-to-pass to invoke validate_spec() on a fixture spec containing a known contradiction, a known UNMAPPED item, and a vague adjective, asserting the returned {contradictions, ambiguities, uncovered, violation_count} matches expected counts; keep the harness run as a separate check.

confirmed_edits.title: Amend glossary 'Complex' EARS pattern to a documented composition of the five base patterns
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:31 glossary 'EARS ... Patterns: Ubiquitous, Event-driven, State-driven, Unwanted behaviour, Optional, Complex' vs requirements.md:65 (Req 1.4 'exactly one of the five EARS patterns')
confirmed_edits.change: requirements.md:31 lists SIX patterns including 'Complex' while Req 1.4 (line 65) says 'exactly one of the five EARS patterns' — internally contradictory within requirements.md. Annotate the glossary 'Complex' entry at line 31 as a documented composition of the five base patterns (not a 6th assignable enum value), matching design.md:1188 and the schema ears_pattern enum (design.md:347, which lists only the five). This makes requirements.md internally consistent and aligns with what spec_validator.py and Property 16 actually assign.

confirmed_edits.title: State the authoritative 7-element vague-adjective set in the requirements layer
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:63 (Req 1.2) 'non-deterministic adjective (e.g., "fast", "secure", "scalable", "optimized")'
confirmed_edits.change: Req 1.2 gives a 4-element 'e.g.' example set {fast, secure, scalable, optimized}, but the implemented Property 17 (design.md:1012) and Task 4.4 (tasks.md:87) use the 7-element set {fast, secure, scalable, optimized, efficient, reliable, performant}. The requirement never states the authoritative full set, so the scanner's coverage is only test-backed. State the 7-element set once in Req 1.2 (or the Req 20 threshold registry) and reference it from Property 17 and Task 4.4 so the implemented set is requirement-backed.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the analysis-pass → four-return-field mapping in the EARS→SMT section
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:554-563 'Consistency Checks Run Per Spec' (four checks) vs design.md:133 four-field return {contradictions, ambiguities, uncovered, violation_count}
confirmed_edits.change: The four analysis passes (Consistency/Completeness/Vacuity/Independence, design.md:554-563) are never mapped to the four return fields (design.md:133), and violation_count's composition is undefined. Add a one-line mapping after design.md:563: contradictions = unsat-core count (Consistency); uncovered = UNMAPPED count (Completeness); ambiguities = vacuity hits + vague-adjective hits; violation_count = sum of the three (>=0), or -1 on Z3 timeout (per design.md:814). This fully defines the four-field contract from Property 14 / Req 4.1.

confirmed_edits.title: Assign prior-pass violation_count storage (prev_violation_count) for Property 15 convergence
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:231 'Runs spec_validator.py after every elaboration pass'; Property 15 (design.md:994-998); run_state schema (design.md:481-496, no prev field)
confirmed_edits.change: Property 15 (strict-decrease-or-HANDOFF, design.md:994-998) and Req 4.3 require comparing the current pass's violation_count against the prior pass's, but no run_state field stores the prior value (run_state schema design.md:481-496 has violation_count but no prev_violation_count). Add a run_state.prev_violation_count field and specify in the Initializer key-behaviors (design.md:231) / Task 10.1 that the loop compares current vs prior to enforce strict-decrease-or-HANDOFF. Mirror with a column in migration 005_run_state.sql (Task 28.5).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix validate_spec() signature gap — Completeness/UNMAPPED check needs the domain-baseline item set as input
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:133 / tasks.md:66 signature 'validate_spec(requirements: list[dict]) -> dict'; design.md:559 Completeness check 'for each domain-baseline item'
confirmed_edits.change: The Completeness check (design.md:559) flags UNMAPPED 'for each domain-baseline item', but the domain-baseline checklist set is NOT a parameter of validate_spec(requirements: list[dict]) (signature at design.md:133, tasks.md:66) — it lives in domain_baseline_checklists / the checklist file. As written the validator cannot compute 'uncovered' from its inputs. Either extend the signature to validate_spec(requirements: list[dict], baseline_items: list[dict]) -> dict (and update Task 4.1, Property 14, the design.md:632 test) or specify how validate_spec loads the baseline set, so the uncovered/UNMAPPED field has a defined source.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the 'without a numeric bound' bound-detection predicate for the vague-adjective scanner
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:63 'without a quantified numeric bound'; design.md:1010-1014 (Property 17 'without an associated numeric bound'); tasks.md:68,87 'without a numeric bound'
confirmed_edits.change: Req 1.2, Property 17 (design.md:1010-1014), and Task 4.4 (tasks.md:68,87) all hinge the scanner's accept/reject on a vague adjective appearing 'without a numeric bound', but no doc defines how the presence of a numeric bound is detected (digit regex? unit grammar? proximity window to the adjective? a parsed comparison predicate?). Specify the bound-detection rule in the EARS→SMT section (e.g. a numeric literal with an optional unit within the same clause / N-token window of the flagged adjective) so the core accept/reject decision is reproducible rather than implementation-defined.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify variable-construction mechanism for the Independence check inside validate_spec(), or scope it out of the per-spec checks
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:561 Independence: 'confirms gate decisions are independent of prediction variables by asserting predA != predB AND gateA = gateB is SAT'
confirmed_edits.change: Independence is listed as one of the four per-spec checks run by validate_spec (design.md:561), but a plain requirements: list[dict] contains no gate/prediction variables, and design.md:561 describes it only generically with no mechanism to construct gateA/gateB/predA/predB from an arbitrary EARS spec. The harness CHECK-5 (formal_verification_merged.py:255-261) tests this against fixed completion-gate variables, NOT against validate_spec output, and Property 4 is tested via test_completion_gate.py (tasks.md:658-662), not test_spec_validator.py. Either specify how validate_spec derives gate/prediction variables from a requirements list, or move Independence out of the four per-spec validate_spec checks and document it as the static design-time harness check it actually is (design.md:561 / CHECK-5), reconciling design.md:554-563 with the harness scope.

refuted.item: Critic 'missing': Req 29 Structured Omission Declaration (REQ-SPEC-018, Property 30, CHECK-13a/13b) absent from the validator's scope
refuted.why: Not a gap in the canonical artifacts. Req 29 is fully EARS-defined (requirements.md:474-478), with Property 30 (design.md:1124-1130), the SubagentStop omission_guard component (design.md:148, hook-wiring row design.md:218), and Task 54 (tasks.md:974). The validator (spec_validator.py) does NOT itself emit omission_declaration — it is the Initializer (the validator's caller) whose subagent OUTPUT carries the field, and that is the SubagentStop hook's concern, already fully specified. The finding flags the draft's ears_stories omission, not a defect in requirements/design/tasks. Nothing for spec_validator.py to fix.

refuted.item: Critic 'overstatement': draft marks Req 1.1 'addressed' by spec_validator.py and implies the validator enforces the atomic-requirement schema (unique ID, EARS pattern, priority, >=1 acceptance criterion)
refuted.why: This critiques the DRAFT's own ears_stories, not the canonical artifacts. None of the four Z3 analyses verify ID/priority/AC presence and the docs never claim they do. No edit to the artifacts is warranted — the requested 'fix' would be to the draft, which is out of scope for the canonical edit set.

refuted.why: DUPLICATE of dedup_key unique:spec-pass-cap-vs-slice-cap. refuted.item: ears_needing_work Req 1.4 — partial: validator assigns five base patterns, glossary's sixth 'Complex' is a documented composition
refuted.why: DUPLICATE of dedup_key unique:complex-six-vs-five-pattern. refuted.item: ears_needing_work Phase-0 spine test (design #10) — partial: test-to-pass is harness exit code, not a direct validate_spec() test
refuted.why: DUPLICATE of dedup_key unique:validator-test-to-pass-proxy (open-gap #4). Same design.md:632 anchor and same fix (replace harness-exit proxy with a direct validate_spec fixture assertion). Emitted once.

confirmed_edits.title: collect() must emit timezone-aware ISO-8601, not datetime.utcnow()
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:45 — 'capture ISO-8601 `collected_at` from `datetime.utcnow()`'
confirmed_edits.change: Change the collect() spec at tasks.md:45 from `datetime.utcnow()` to `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` (timezone-aware, emits a '+00:00' offset). Rationale to add inline: the EvidenceRecord schema requires `format: date-time` (RFC 3339) at design.md:407 and the `evidence_records.collected_at` column is `TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL` (design.md:472) with an `evidence_complete` CHECK requiring `collected_at IS NOT NULL` (design.md:476). A naive utcnow() string has no offset, can fail strict date-time validation, and would be coerced to a different instant on Postgres insert — breaking Property 19's 'all four fields shall match the original capture values without loss' (design.md:1028). Mirror the same correction in the unit-test task 3.3 (tasks.md:56-61) so a test asserts the emitted timestamp carries an offset.

confirmed_edits.title: validate_evidence_record() must validate output_hash/collected_at formats, not just presence
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:46 — 'validate_evidence_record(record: dict) -> bool that checks all four fields present and non-empty'
confirmed_edits.change: Strengthen validate_evidence_record() at tasks.md:46 to validate `output_hash` against the anchored pattern `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` and `collected_at` as a parseable ISO-8601/RFC-3339 timestamp (or run jsonschema against the EvidenceRecord definition, design.md:387-411), not merely presence+non-emptiness. Reason: the SubagentStop hook calls this same validator (tasks.md:179), so a non-empty but malformed output_hash (uppercase hex, missing `sha256:` prefix, wrong length) currently passes both the validator and the SubagentStop gate (only the separate JSON-Schema path in the PreToolUse status guard catches it). This also closes the requirements.md:521 deferral rationale, which justifies skipping Pydantic/BAML runtime enforcement by asserting 'the SubagentStop schema-validation gate (REQ-COV-006) already enforces Evidence_Record structure at the hook layer' — a claim that is false while tasks.md:46/179 wire the hook to a presence-only validator. Cite design.md:402 (canonical sha256 pattern).

confirmed_edits.title: store_to_feature_list() and PreToolUse status-guard task text must admit the failed-state transitions
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:47 ('flips status to `proven`') and task 6.1, tasks.md:135 ('validate proposed transition is `unproven → proven` only')
confirmed_edits.change: Update tasks.md:47 and tasks.md:135 to match the 2026-06-15 reconciliation that widened permitted transitions. tasks.md:135 currently says 'validate proposed transition is `unproven → proven` only' — change to: permit {unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven}; only any transition *into* proven additionally requires a complete Evidence_Record (unproven→failed and failed→unproven require none). Align tasks.md:47's store_to_feature_list() description to the same edge set. This reconciles the stale task text with requirements.md:123,129, design.md:215 (PreToolUse status guard) and design.md:372,374 (status enum {unproven,proven,failed}) and Property 3 (design.md:900).

confirmed_edits.title: Property 19 over-claims Postgres round-trip while filed as a Phase-0 collector subtask
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.2, tasks.md:50-54 — '**Property 19: Evidence Round-Trip (Store and Retrieve)** ... Validates: Requirements 16.2'
confirmed_edits.change: Property 19 (design.md:1026-1030) asserts the record is 'stored in Postgres ... retrievable by requirement_id and commit_sha', but task 3.2 is a Phase-0 collector subtask with NO Postgres (store_to_postgres is Phase-2 task 30.1, tasks.md:476). Split the property: keep a Phase-0 in-memory/file round-trip (collect→validate→hash-match, no Postgres) under task 3.2 and validate against 5.3/5.6 not 16.2; add the Phase-2 Postgres store/retrieve round-trip assertion under task 30.2 (tasks.md:480) validating 16.2. Reconcile the 'stored in Postgres' wording in the Phase-0 portion of Property 19 at design.md:1028 accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: actor_agent: add as a store_to_postgres parameter (NOT to the 4-field JSON record) and fix stale acting_agent name
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 30.1, tasks.md:477 ('store_to_postgres(record: EvidenceRecord, req_id: str, commit_sha: str)') and Property 24 test, tasks.md:655 ('acting_agent == "implementer.md"')
confirmed_edits.change: Property 24 requires actor_agent (= verifier.md, never implementer.md) on every evidence row (design.md:473,1068), but store_to_postgres()'s signature (tasks.md:477) carries no verifier identity, so the role-separation invariant has no enforcement point at the collector boundary. Add `actor_agent` as a parameter: `store_to_postgres(record, req_id, commit_sha, actor_agent)`, asserting it equals 'verifier.md' and rejecting implementer-authored evidence at write time. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: do NOT add actor_agent to the EvidenceRecord JSON object — that schema is `additionalProperties: false` over exactly the four fields (design.md:390; requirements.md:29), so adding a fifth field would break Property 2/CHECK-7 and be rejected by the SubagentStop and PreToolUse status-guard validators. actor_agent lives only on the 7-column evidence_records Postgres row (design.md:464-478). Separately fix the stale name at tasks.md:655: the Property 24 test still references `acting_agent`, but design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' — rename `acting_agent` → `actor_agent` in the task-655 test description.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a design subsection defining output_hash byte-source, canonicalization, and artifact retention
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component inventory row evidence_collector.py (design.md:134) and Phase 0 list row 11 (design.md:633); no narrative subsection exists
confirmed_edits.change: design.md covers evidence_collector in only inventory cells (134, 263, 633) plus one test line (859) with no narrative on the load-bearing hashing semantics. Add a design subsection (near the EvidenceRecord data model, design.md:387-423) defining: (1) the exact byte source for output_hash — raw test stdout vs the Playwright trace file vs a normalized artifact; (2) a canonicalization rule so non-deterministic test output (timestamps, ordering, temp paths) rehashes identically across runs; (3) whether/where the raw artifact is retained (blob store vs hash-only) — requirements.md:297 says 'artifact OR content-addressed reference', leaving retention unspecified. Without this, output_hash is not reproducibly verifiable and OPA's zero-evidence query cannot detect a fabricated hash (design.md:1052).

confirmed_edits.title: Decide empty/zero-byte artifact validity in collect()
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.3, tasks.md:57 — 'collect() with empty bytes still produces valid sha256 hash'
confirmed_edits.change: tasks.md:57 asserts empty bytes 'still produces valid sha256 hash' (sha256('') = e3b0c442...), but the spec never decides whether a zero-byte artifact is a VALID proof — such a hash is well-formed and passes all four-field and schema checks, so the gate would silently accept an empty/short-circuited test as proof. Decide and specify one of: (a) collect() raises on empty output_bytes; or (b) the Verifier must assert the artifact is non-trivial before calling collect(). Then make tasks.md:57's test assert the chosen behavior (reject-empty, or document why empty is acceptable) rather than blessing the meaningless hash. Add a matching EARS criterion or note under Requirement 5.3 / 9.3 if option (a) changes the collector contract.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire REQ-EXEC-012 / 8.3 WIRING integration-test evidence into the collector scope
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:44-48 (_Requirements: 5.3, 5.6, 9.3_) and task 47, tasks.md:959 (WIRING integration-test evidence)
confirmed_edits.change: Requirement 8.3 (requirements.md:174: 'WHEN a WIRING item is claimed proven, THE System SHALL require an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record') and the schema note 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence' (design.md:334) bear directly on evidence_collector.py — it is the assembler of that integration-test Evidence_Record. Task 47 (tasks.md:959) names the obligation but the collector task 3.1 only frames output_bytes around behavioral/Playwright output. Add 8.3 to task 3.1's `_Requirements:` line and a sub-point that collect() must accept and assemble an integration-test artifact (not only a behavioral/Playwright one), so the WIRING-proof path has an explicit assembly point.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire REQ-VERIFY-007/008 (Req 25.1/25.2) NFR Evidence_Record assembly into the collector
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:44-48 (_Requirements: 5.3, 5.6, 9.3_) and task 51, tasks.md:966 (perf_a11y_verifier emits NFR Evidence_Records)
confirmed_edits.change: Requirements 25.1/25.2 (requirements.md:447-448) require performance verification to 'attach the result as the Evidence_Record' (k6/Lighthouse) and accessibility verification (axe-core) to produce evidence; task 51 (tasks.md:966) emits these as 'NFR Evidence_Records.' These records are assembled by evidence_collector.py, but task 3.1 frames collect()/output_bytes only around behavioral test output. Add REQ-VERIFY-007/008 to the collector's bearing-requirements and a note that output_bytes for NFR items is the k6/Lighthouse/axe-core result artifact — which makes resolving the byte-source question (dedup evidence-hash-byte-source) mandatory for non-test artifacts.

confirmed_edits.title: store_to_postgres fallback must be flagged as an N-25 gate-consistency obligation (never fail-open)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 30.1, tasks.md:477 ('fall back to feature_list.json update if DB unavailable') and task 30.2, tasks.md:482
confirmed_edits.change: store_to_postgres's feature_list.json fallback (tasks.md:477,482) is specified only mechanically. Per the N-25 reconciliation obligation (tasks.md:730), when db_connection.health_check() fails the file-backed fallback MUST reach the SAME allow/block decision as Postgres and NEVER fail-open, taking the more conservative (blocking) result on divergence. Add this gate-consistency constraint to task 30.1/30.2 and cite REQ-STORE-001/003 (requirements.md:296,298 — Postgres single source of truth, reconstructable independently of any model session). A fail-open or divergent collector fallback would silently weaken the OPA zero-evidence gate.

confirmed_edits.title: Add evidence-quality caveat: no Z3 check exercises collect()'s real sha256/datetime/format behavior
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 3.3, tasks.md:56-61 (unit tests for evidence_collector.py) and the CHECK-7 family in verification/formal_verification_merged.py:301-338
confirmed_edits.change: CHECK-7a/7b/7c (formal_verification_merged.py:311-338) operate purely on abstract booleans (`evidenceComplete == And(evidenceHasHash, evidenceHasTestFile, ...)`, line 302) — they model the four-field GATE LOGIC, not the collector's hashing/timestamp behavior. NO Z3 check exercises collect()'s actual sha256 computation, datetime output, or the `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` format; those are only unit-tested (task 3.3, tasks.md:57-59). Add an explicit caveat to task 3.3 (or the testing-strategy note) stating that the sha256/timestamp/format correctness is unit-test-verified only and is NOT in the Z3 coverage, so the Z3 'evidence schema enforced' claim is not mistaken for runtime hash-format coverage.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: REQ-VERIFY-003 (Req 9.3) is only partially wired to the collector
refuted.why: The mapping already genuinely exists. Task 3.1's `_Requirements:` line at tasks.md:48 reads `5.3, 5.6, 9.3` — Requirement 9.3 (requirements.md:188, 'WHEN a behavioral check runs, THE Verifier SHALL produce a captured artifact ... attached as an Evidence_Record') is explicitly cited on the collector task, and the verifier subagent (design.md:260,263) feeds the captured artifact into evidence_collector.py. The artifact-bytes→collect()→EvidenceRecord path is mapped; there is no missing wiring to add for the behavioral case. (The distinct WIRING-8.3 and NFR-25 cases ARE genuinely unmapped and are emitted as separate add-wiring edits.)

refuted.item: critic 'weak' finding: validate_evidence_record() over-scoped as the sole Req 9.5 enforcement; SubagentStop is now a compound gate (four-field evidence AND omission_declaration)
refuted.why: Not an evidence_collector defect — the critic itself concedes 'Not a collector defect.' The omission-declaration guard is already fully and correctly specified as a separate SubagentStop capability: Req 29/REQ-SPEC-018 (requirements.md:474-478), the omission-declaration guard hook row (design.md:218), the omission_guard component (design.md:148), Property 30 (design.md:1124-1130), and task 54 (tasks.md:974). The collector's validate_evidence_record() correctly owns only the four-field evidence portion; the compound nature of the SubagentStop gate is already documented elsewhere, so no edit to the collector's artifacts is warranted.

refuted.item: open_gap fix as written: 'Add actor_agent to collect()/the EvidenceRecord'
refuted.why: The EvidenceRecord JSON Schema is `additionalProperties: false` over exactly the four required fields (design.md:390; requirements.md:29,127), so adding actor_agent to the JSON record would be rejected by the very SubagentStop and PreToolUse status-guard validators it must pass, breaking Property 2/CHECK-7. The underlying need (enforce Property 24 at write time) is real and CONFIRMED, but only via the Postgres-row/store_to_postgres path — captured in the rescoped edit dedup_key 'evidence-actor-agent-store-not-schema'. The literal 'add to EvidenceRecord' phrasing is refuted as self-contradicting.

confirmed_edits.title: No ingestion path turns the WIRING candidate into a feature_list.json coverage item (8.1 second clause)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 19.1 body, tasks.md:329 "For each dead-code finding, emit a WIRING coverage item candidate (JSON to stdout)" — the candidate stops at stdout
confirmed_edits.change: Add a subtask (e.g. 19.3) OR extend post_tool_use_hook.py task 18.1 specifying the ingestion path that satisfies criterion 8.1's second clause ('SHALL represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_list.json'). State the actor and the write path: the implementer/initializer, on receiving the wiring_checker.py candidate as PostToolUse next-turn feedback, MUST add a type:'WIRING' CoverageItem (status default 'unproven', in_scope true, ≥1 acceptance criterion per Property 1) to feature_list.json via the PreToolUse status-guard-permitted creation path (design.md:215). Cite REQ-EXEC-010. Today the data path stops at stdout/next-turn text and no task creates the item.

confirmed_edits.title: Criterion 8.2 (Verifier marks WIRING item failed on unreachable symbol) has no mechanism
confirmed_edits.anchor: verifier.md key-behaviors list, design.md:257-266 (Runs structural/semantic/behavioral/security/perf checks) — no WIRING/unreachable behavior listed
confirmed_edits.change: Add a verifier.md key-behavior bullet: 'On each slice, the Verifier consumes wiring_checker.py findings (or re-runs it); for any WIRING item whose symbol is reported unreachable from a real execution path, performs the unproven→failed transition (permitted by the PreToolUse status guard, design.md:215).' Pair it with a tasks.md subtask wiring the checker's unreachable-symbol finding to a Verifier-authored unproven→failed transition, citing REQ-EXEC-011. Currently the Verifier definition never reads wiring_checker output, never mentions WIRING items, and no task connects the finding to a failed transition.

confirmed_edits.title: Task 19.1 falsely claims criterion 8.2 coverage its body does not deliver
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 19.1 traceability line tasks.md:331 "_Requirements: 8.1, 8.2_" vs body tasks.md:326-330 (AST analysis + stdout candidate + 'Exit 0 even when findings exist')
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve the false traceability: either (a) remove 8.2 from task 19.1's _Requirements_ line, leaving it _Requirements: 8.1_ (since the body only builds AST analysis + a stdout candidate and explicitly exits 0, performing no Verifier unproven→failed transition), and route 8.2 to the new Verifier-side task created for the failed-transition mechanism; or (b) extend the 19.1 body to actually deliver the 8.2 mechanism. Option (a) is the minimal fix. This is an internal contradiction between the task's traceability claim and its body.

confirmed_edits.title: Requirement 8 / wiring_checker.py is pinned by no Z3 CHECK and no correctness Property
confirmed_edits.anchor: Correctness Properties section (Properties 1-30, design.md:882-1130) — none addresses call-graph/dead-code/wiring; and verification/formal_verification_merged.py has no REQ-EXEC-010..012 check (grep: zero wiring/dead-code/reachability assertions, CHECKs run only 1..13b)
confirmed_edits.change: Pick one and apply: (a) add a new Property (e.g. 'Property 31: Wiring / Dead-Code Detection — for any file with a defined-but-never-called non-test symbol, wiring_checker.py emits exactly one WIRING candidate naming that symbol; for any reachable handler, zero candidates') with a Hypothesis test seeded in the PBT suite; OR (b) explicitly document in design.md that wiring detection is a runtime/example-tested property with no Z3 model (mirroring the audit-log note at design.md:1110 'no Z3 check — runtime/CI verified'). Today the absence is silent while every other major gate carries a CHECK or Property; the integration claim rests solely on example tests 19.2 and 26.1.

confirmed_edits.title: No design narrative defines reachability scope (per-file vs repo-wide) for wiring_checker.py — cross-file false-positive risk
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component inventory single row design.md:135 (`wiring_checker.py` ... 'Static call-graph / dead-code analysis; emits WIRING coverage items') — no B07/Requirement-8 narrative subsection anywhere
confirmed_edits.change: Add a B07 design subsection for wiring_checker.py specifying: (1) the definition of 'real execution path'; (2) entry-point seeding (CLI mains, route/handler decorators, registered callbacks; test files excluded); (3) whether reachability is whole-repo or per-file — and reconcile against tasks.md:327 which says 'build a call graph for each file'. A per-file AST graph structurally cannot distinguish 'defined-but-never-called-in-this-file' from 'called-from-another-file', so per-file scope false-positives on every public/exported symbol; the analysis likely must be repo-wide, or per-file with an explicit exported-symbol exemption. Today there is zero narrative/interface/algorithm description.

confirmed_edits.title: Division of labor between wiring_checker.py (AST) and wiring_dead_code.yml (Semgrep, task 24.1) is unresolved
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 19.1 body tasks.md:328 (AST 'never called' detection) vs task 24.1 tasks.md:385-386 (Semgrep route/handler decorators + registered-but-uninvoked callbacks); both carry _Requirements: 8.1, 8.2_
confirmed_edits.change: Add a one-line division-of-labor note (in task 19.1 and cross-referenced in 24.1): 'wiring_checker.py (AST) covers plain defined-but-never-called functions; wiring_dead_code.yml (Semgrep) covers decorator-routed handlers/routes and registered-but-uninvoked callbacks; both emit type:WIRING candidates, de-duplicated by symbol before ingestion.' This resolves which tool authoritatively detects the route/handler/callback obligations named in criterion 8.2 and prevents double-reporting. Currently both tools claim the same 8.1/8.2 scope with no de-dup or boundary stated.

confirmed_edits.title: Changed-files input contract between post_tool_use_hook.py and wiring_checker.py is unspecified (incl. MultiEdit)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 18.1 tasks.md:310 'Triggered on Write, Edit, MultiEdit completion' / tasks.md:313 'Run python3 tools/wiring_checker.py on changed files'; and task 19.1 tasks.md:326 'Accept a list of changed files as CLI args'
confirmed_edits.change: Specify the handoff contract on both sides: in task 18.1 state how the PostToolUse hook derives the changed-file list from the hook payload (e.g. tool_input.file_path for Write/Edit; the set of edited paths for MultiEdit, since one MultiEdit call can mutate multiple files) and passes them as argv; in task 19.1 state the exact argv format (one file path per positional arg). The matcher is Write|Edit|MultiEdit (design.md:178,216) so the MultiEdit multi-file case must be handled. Today neither side pins the format.

refuted.item: open_gap: wiring_checker.py output is invisible to observability/audit (no OTel span, no audit entry)
refuted.why: Not a defect in the current artifacts. The finding's own fix concedes the path is already in scope: task 36.1 (tasks.md:539) extends 'all six hooks' — including PostToolUse, which runs the wiring check — to emit OTel gate-decision events, and the PostToolUse row (design.md:216) is non-gating/informational by design, so it is correctly NOT audit-chained (audit_log.append is wired only to the three gating hooks stop/pre_tool_use/subagent_stop per REQ-AUDIT-004 / design.md:217-218). Wiring findings surfacing via the PostToolUse OTel event is the designed behavior; nothing additional is missing.

refuted.item: critic 'missing': draft omits task 42.1 as a third verification artifact tracing 8.1
refuted.why: This is a complaint about the draft findings file's own enumeration being incomplete, not a gap in requirements/design/tasks. Task 42.1 (tasks.md:718) already exists and correctly lists 8.1 in its _Requirements_ line and gates the full PBT suite. No edit to the canonical artifacts is warranted.

refuted.item: critic 'missing': draft never cites Property 1 (Coverage Item Schema Invariant) as governing WIRING items
refuted.why: Property 1 already exists and is correct as written (design.md:882-886, 'Validates: Requirements 5.1, 5.4'): every item has type ∈ {functional,NFR,WIRING}, ≥1 acceptance criterion, status default unproven. It already governs ingested WIRING items. The criticism is that the draft did not name it — a meta-observation about the findings file, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The substantive wiring gap (that no item is ever ingested for Property 1 to govern) is captured by the wiring-candidate-ingestion-path edit.

refuted.item: critic 'missing': no ears_story for criterion 13.2 (REQ-STEER-002) on the PostToolUse wiring path
refuted.why: 13.2 is already fully wired into this component's path in the canonical artifacts: the Hook Wiring Table PostToolUse row maps to REQ-VERIFY-004 AND REQ-STEER-002 (design.md:216), task 18.1 carries _Requirements: 9.4, 13.2_ (tasks.md:316), and task 26.1 includes 13.2 (tasks.md:410). The criticism targets the draft's missing ears_story, not a missing mapping in requirements/design/tasks. Nothing to fix in the artifacts.

refuted.item: critic 'weak': 8.3 ears_story omits the design.md:334 schema note ('WIRING items require integration-test evidence')
refuted.why: The schema note already exists at design.md:334 and criterion 8.3 is already tasked (task 47, tasks.md:959 — 'a WIRING item flips proven only with an integration test attached as Evidence_Record', _Requirements: 8.3_). 8.3 is genuinely covered. The criticism is that the draft under-cited an already-present anchor — a draft-completeness note, not an artifact defect.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: criterion 8.3 (REQ-EXEC-012) status 'partial'
refuted.why: Covered. Task 47 (tasks.md:959) implements the WIRING-item integration-test Evidence_Record gate and the schema note at design.md:334 anchors it. The finding itself states 8.3 is the generic Evidence_Record gate that does not involve wiring_checker.py at proof time — i.e. no wiring_checker.py-specific mapping is missing. No add-wiring edit needed.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: Property 11 (Orphan Detection) status 'gap' relative to wiring_checker.py
refuted.why: Correctly does NOT bear on wiring_checker.py. Property 11 (design.md:962-966, tasks.md:347-350) validates orphan_detector.py (REQ-6.3), a different Phase-1 tool. The finding itself records this as informational. The genuine gap — that wiring_checker.py has no dedicated Property — is captured by the unique:wiring-property-or-z3-note edit; no separate fix for Property 11.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: criterion 5.4 (REQ-COV-004) status 'partial'
refuted.why: Duplicate of the 8.1 ingestion gap, not a separate defect. The 'first-class coverage item in feature_list.json, not informal afterthought' obligation is satisfied for WIRING once the candidate is ingested; the only missing piece is the ingestion path, already captured by the wiring-candidate-ingestion-path confirmed edit. The CoverageItem type enum {functional,NFR,WIRING} already exists (requirements.md:122, design.md:333, tasks.md:27), so 5.4's representation requirement is met at the schema level.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: Glossary WIRING status 'partial'
refuted.why: The glossary entry (requirements.md:35) is accurate and complete as a definition. Its 'partial' rating stems entirely from the route/handler/callback decorator detection being split between AST and Semgrep tools — which is the same underlying issue as the unresolved division-of-labor, already captured by the wiring-ast-vs-semgrep-division confirmed edit. No separate glossary edit is warranted.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire orphan_detector.py as a blocking traceability gate to satisfy REQ-6.3 'block the run'
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 'Implement tools/orphan_detector.py' (tasks.md:339-344), _Requirements: 6.3; and task 14.2 required-checks list (tasks.md:270)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a blocking caller for orphan_detector. Create a new task (e.g. 20.x / 40.x) 'traceability-gate CI workflow': add `.github/workflows/traceability-gate.yml` that runs `python3 tools/orphan_detector.py` and fails the merge (non-zero exit) when any orphan is found; register `traceability-gate` as a REQUIRED status check. Add `traceability-gate` to the required-checks list at tasks.md:270 (`docs/github-ruleset.md`) alongside formal-verification/coverage-gate/secrets-scan/audit-chain-verify. State the exit-code-to-block contract (CI required check fails merge; equivalently a PreToolUse exit-2 gate). This is the production mechanism the REQ-6.3 'block the run' obligation currently lacks — orphan_detector's non-zero exit is consumed by nothing but a test (task 26.1).

confirmed_edits.title: REQ-6.3 'block the run' vs exit-non-zero altitude mismatch — pin the gate altitude in the requirement and task
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 6.3 (requirements.md:143): 'THEN THE System SHALL fail the orphan-detection check and block the run.'
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve the enforcement-altitude mismatch: REQ-6.3 mandates blocking the run (an enforcement-layer action like PreToolUse exit-2 or a required CI check) but tasks.md:343 only specifies 'exit non-zero + JSON' (reporting). Amend REQ-6.3 to name the blocking caller and its exit-code contract — classify orphan detection as a BLOCKING gate (required CI status check failing the merge, OR PreToolUse exit 2), explicitly NOT advisory PostToolUse feedback (which is non-blocking, exit 1 never exit 2, per tasks.md:314 and REQ-STEER-002). Cross-reference the new traceability-gate caller. Mirror this in task 20.1.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the authoritative store for the backward-orphan 'maps to no verification' lookup
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1, bullets at tasks.md:341-342 ('Read feature_list.json for all known requirement IDs' / 'requirement IDs with no source file or verification artifact')
confirmed_edits.change: Specify the authoritative source for the backward-orphan verification-artifact link. Postgres is the declared single source of truth for coverage queries (requirements.md:298) and the requirement<->evidence graph lives in traceability_links + evidence_records (design.md:453-478), yet task 20.1 reads feature_list.json (tasks.md:341), which can diverge. Add a bullet: query evidence_records / traceability_links (link_type IN ('test','evidence')) for the verification-artifact lookup, with a documented feature_list.json fallback consistent with the Postgres-unavailable fallback rule (design.md:815, tasks.md:730 N-25). Reconcile with REQ-16.3.

confirmed_edits.title: Define 'verification artifact' concretely for the backward-orphan check
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:342) and Property 11 (design.md:962-966)
confirmed_edits.change: Define what counts as a 'verification artifact' so the backward-orphan pass/fail boundary is implementable. The Postgres schema already provides the exact candidate definition (design.md:457-458 fixes traceability_links.link_type to the closed set including 'test'/'evidence'; design.md:465-477 defines evidence_records) but it is never bound to task 20's rule. Add to task 20.1 AND Property 11: a backward orphan = a requirement ID with NO traceability_links row of link_type IN ('test','evidence') AND/OR no evidence_records row. State this is the canonical definition the check keys on.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin orphan_detector run scope, file glob, language scope, and excludes
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340) 'Scan all source files...'
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve scan-scope ambiguity. tasks.md:340 says 'all source files' (full-repo, CI-suited) while the sibling wiring_checker/PostToolUse pipeline operates on changed files (tasks.md:313,326). Add bullets to task 20.1: (1) state full-repo scope (recommended, since it is wired as a CI blocking gate per the traceability-gate edit); (2) define the file glob and exclude list (e.g. exclude tests/, generated dirs, __init__.py, migrations); (3) state the language scope (Python source). This determines where it can be wired and bounds false positives.

confirmed_edits.title: Add an orphan-exemption / allowlist mechanism to bound forward-orphan false positives
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:342) 'source files/functions with no requirement ref (forward orphans)' and Property 11 (design.md:962-966)
confirmed_edits.change: Add an exemption mechanism so the forward-orphan rule does not fail-noisy on legitimately un-annotated units (helpers, __init__.py, config, generated code). Add to task 20.1 and Property 11: an explicit ignore mechanism — a `# orphan-exempt: <reason>` inline marker AND/OR a config file of excluded paths — and define granularity (file-level vs function-level). Without this the check is unimplementable as a non-noisy blocking gate.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a design.md subsection for orphan_detector defining its full contract
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component inventory row (design.md:136) 'orphan_detector.py ... Detects implementation units with no requirement ref and vice versa'; add a new subsection peer to 'No-Progress Watchdog' (design.md:640)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design.md subsection (peer to the No-Progress Watchdog / plan-approved gate subsections) for orphan_detector, since the component currently has only one inventory line plus Property 11 — no CLI signature, no output schema, no caller, no blocking semantics, no data source, and it is absent from the Phase 0 spine list. The subsection must define: the CLI signature; the structured JSON report schema (forward_orphans[], backward_orphans[]); the data source for each orphan class (feature_list.json for forward; traceability_links/evidence_records for backward per the source-of-truth edit); the caller (the traceability-gate CI check); the exit-code-to-block mapping; the run scope and excludes. This consolidates the dedup'd detail edits into one design home.

confirmed_edits.title: Factor commit-trailer ID extraction into one shared helper and state ownership
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340, commit-trailer scan) and task 29.1 (tasks.md:464-466, parse_commit_trailers / assert_commit_has_req_id)
confirmed_edits.change: Two tools implement the same `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}` commit-trailer scan with no division of labor: orphan_detector (tasks.md:340) and traceability_writer.parse_commit_trailers/assert_commit_has_req_id (tasks.md:464,466). Add a bullet factoring trailer-ID extraction into one shared helper (e.g. tools/req_id_scan.py) imported by both, and state authority: traceability_writer.assert_commit_has_req_id owns the commit-has-req-id gate (REQ-6.2 / Property 10); orphan_detector owns bidirectional orphan detection (REQ-6.3 / Property 11). Prevents regex drift / double-maintenance.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix task 26.1 _Requirements mapping to include 6.3 (the only task that runs orphan_detector)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 26.1 _Requirements line (tasks.md:410): '_Requirements: 8.1, 8.2, 9.4, 10.3, 13.2_'
confirmed_edits.change: Add `6.3` to the _Requirements line of task 26.1. Task 26.1 (tasks.md:404-410) is the ONLY task whose body actually runs orphan_detector.py ('run orphan_detector.py; assert exit non-zero and report contains both orphan types', tasks.md:406) yet its mapping omits 6.3. The spec's own forward 6.3->task link points at the wrong task. Add 6.3 to 26.1; optionally drop 6.3 from 33.1's mapping (or keep for parse_commit_trailers' 6.x coverage but note 26.1 owns the orphan-detection assertion).

confirmed_edits.title: Wire a blocking enforcer for REQ-6.2 commit-trailer obligation (shares orphan_detector's unwired-gate defect)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 29.1 (tasks.md:466) 'assert_commit_has_req_id(commit_sha): raise TraceabilityError if no requirement ID found in trailer'; REQ-6.2 (requirements.md:142)
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-6.2 ('WHEN a commit is created, require >=1 referenced requirement ID in the commit trailer', requirements.md:142) has no blocking enforcer wired: assert_commit_has_req_id (tasks.md:466) only raises TraceabilityError with no stated caller/hook/CI gate. Both 6.2 and 6.3 share the same unwired-enforcement defect. Add a bullet naming the caller for assert_commit_has_req_id — fold it into the same traceability-gate CI check (or a commit-msg/PreToolUse hook) so a single shared commit-trailer gate satisfies both REQ-6.2 and REQ-6.3's trailer obligation.

confirmed_edits.title: Document the deliberate assurance level for orphan detection (no Z3, audit-log routing if it blocks)
confirmed_edits.anchor: New orphan_detector design subsection (peer to No-Progress Watchdog, design.md:640); Property 11 (design.md:962-966)
confirmed_edits.change: State orphan detection's assurance level explicitly. It has zero Z3 coverage (confirmed: no orphan/trace/6.3 hits in formal_verification_merged.py) and, being unwired, produces no gate_audit_log entry (audit producers are stop/pre_tool_use/subagent_stop hooks only, design.md:144,460). Document one of: (a) runtime-only hypothesis Property 11 is the deliberate assurance level (weaker than peer Z3-backed gates) — state this in design.md; OR (b) if orphan detection becomes a blocking gate per the traceability-gate edit, route its allow/block decision through audit_log.append(...) like the other gate producers so the block is tamper-evidently logged.

confirmed_edits.title: Disambiguate the requirement-ID regex for multi-segment IDs in the orphan scan
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340) regex `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}`; CoverageItem.id pattern `^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$` (design.md:328)
confirmed_edits.change: Clarify the ID regex's match scope. The detector scans for unanchored `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}` (tasks.md:340) and ties it to CoverageItem.id `^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$` (design.md:328), but the actual feature_list.json IDs are multi-segment (REQ-TRACE-001, REQ-VERIFY-007, REQ-SPEC-018; requirements.md:137, design.md:146,148) and the EARS dotted short-IDs (e.g. 6.3) are a different namespace the same regex cannot match. Add a bullet stating the regex matches the canonical 3-segment CoverageItem.id form (unanchored within comments/trailers), explicitly NOT the dotted EARS criterion numbers, and confirm it matches all REQ-<DOMAIN>-NNN IDs present in feature_list.json.

confirmed_edits.title: Bind REQ-TRACE-001..004 bidirectional-link obligation to orphan_detector + traceability_links store

Add a bullet to task 20.1 stating orphan_detector enforces forward+backward link existence AGAINST the traceability_links graph written by traceability_writer (task 29), not only feature_list.json — closing the gap where the detector reads feature_list.json while the durable bidirectional graph it should check lives in Postgres (design.md:453-462).

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve the feature_list.json-vs-evidence_records input-model contradiction for the Rego policy
confirmed_edits.anchor: ### Property 22: OPA Zero-Evidence Policy at Merge — '*For any* merge attempt, the OPA/Conftest policy shall deny the merge if the query `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM evidence_records WHERE requirement_id = ANY(approved_req_ids)` returns fewer rows...' (design.md:1052)
confirmed_edits.change: Pick the feature_list.json Conftest-document model as canonical (it matches the actual `conftest test feature_list.json` invocation at tasks.md:264 and all integration tests at tasks.md:407-408). REWRITE Property 22's statement to express the rule over `input.items` (Conftest document input) rather than a SQL `SELECT ... FROM evidence_records`: e.g. 'the policy shall deny the merge if any in_scope item in input.items is not status=="proven", or any proven item lacks a complete four-field Evidence_Record.' Also rewrite tasks.md:354 first bullet ('deny merge if any requirement ID in the approved set has zero rows in `evidence_records`') to the same document model, and add one sentence to the design.md:137 Component-Inventory row (or a new subsection) stating the single canonical input is the feature_list.json document evaluated by Conftest, not a live Postgres table.

confirmed_edits.title: Make the Rego deny rule honor in_scope, so it matches the Stop gate
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 21.1 — 'policy reads `items[_].status == "unproven"` and denies if any found' (tasks.md:355)
confirmed_edits.change: Amend task 21.1 (and Property 22 in design.md:1052) so the policy filters `input.items` on `in_scope == true` before applying the status/evidence checks, mirroring evaluate_stop's `in_scope_items = [i for i in feature_list.items if i.in_scope]` (design.md:690) and Requirement 5.7 (requirements.md:128). Without this filter a naive `items[_].status == "unproven"` rule blocks merge on an out-of-scope unproven item, diverging from the Stop gate. State: 'deny rules iterate only over items where in_scope == true.'

confirmed_edits.title: Close the fail-open hole on `failed` status: deny if status != proven
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 21.1 — 'policy reads `items[_].status == "unproven"` and denies if any found' (tasks.md:355)
confirmed_edits.change: Change the deny rule in task 21.1 (and Property 22) from 'deny if status == "unproven"' to 'deny if status != "proven"' (anything not exactly proven blocks), so a `failed` in-scope item also blocks merge. The status enum is {unproven, proven, failed} (design.md:372) but the policy only references unproven/proven, leaving a `failed` item to silently pass a status==unproven rule. This mirrors the Stop hook's `not_proven = [i for i in in_scope_items if i.status != "proven"]` logic (design.md:731).

confirmed_edits.title: Specify fail-closed / default-deny posture for the Rego merge policy
confirmed_edits.anchor: Error Handling → Hook Failure Modes table (design.md:809-816); add a coverage-gate row / note near design.md:137 or 815
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design note giving coverage_query.rego the same fail-closed posture REQ-GATE-005 mandates (requirements.md:207) and that design.md:811-816 already specifies for stop_hook.py / pre_tool_use_hook.py. State default-deny semantics explicitly: an empty `input.items` array, a parse failure, or a missing/malformed feature_list.json MUST produce a deny / non-zero Conftest exit; the coverage-gate.yml CI step MUST treat any Conftest error (non-zero for reasons other than a clean pass) as a merge block, never default-allow. Closes REQ-GATE-005 (ears_needing_work 10.5, status=gap) for the OPA leg.

confirmed_edits.title: Enumerate the four Evidence_Record fields in the Rego proven-without-evidence rule
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 21.1 — 'Add second rule: deny if any item has `status == "proven"` but `evidence` object is missing or has empty fields' (tasks.md:356)
confirmed_edits.change: Make task 21.1 Rule 2 assert all four Evidence_Record fields (`test_file`, `test_name`, `output_hash`, `collected_at`) are present and non-empty, mirroring the schema `required` list (design.md:389) and the SubagentStop four-field check (design.md:217, Property 2). Current wording 'evidence object missing or has empty fields' is vague and could pass a proven item carrying a partial 1-field evidence object that the hook layer (Property 2 / Glossary Evidence_Record, requirements.md:29) would reject — an inconsistency between the merge gate and the in-session gate.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix the forward-reference defect: 'deferred to Phase 1 task 20' should be task 21
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 14.1 — 'full Postgres-backed policy deferred to Phase 1 task 20' (tasks.md:265)
confirmed_edits.change: Change 'deferred to Phase 1 task 20' to 'deferred to Phase 1 task 21' in task 14.1. Task 20 (tasks.md:338) is `orphan_detector.py`, NOT the coverage policy; the full coverage_query.rego policy is task 21 (tasks.md:352). The build chain currently misroutes the component's own forward reference to the wrong task.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a Phase-1 subtask to re-confirm coverage-gate.yml invokes the swapped policy and stays a required check
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 21 — '`coverage_query.rego` — Full OPA Zero-Evidence Merge Policy' (tasks.md:352-358)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a subtask under task 21 to (a) confirm `.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml` (created in task 14.1) invokes the Phase-1 full policy after the stub→full swap, and (b) confirm `coverage-gate` remains in the GitHub repository ruleset's required checks (cross-ref task 14.2 at tasks.md:270, whose required-checks list `formal-verification`, `coverage-gate`, `secrets-scan`, `audit-chain-verify` is the only place coverage-gate is named as required). Task 21 currently replaces the .rego content with no task verifying the swapped policy is still the gating one. Also note in task 14.1/21 that the Phase-0 stub (status-only, passes on zero unproven) is intentionally weaker than the Phase-1 full policy (adds evidence presence), so Phase-0 green does NOT imply Phase-1 green — the required-status-check registration must survive the swap (re-register, do not assume continuity).

confirmed_edits.title: Document that coverage-gate.yml is an overloaded workflow gating three tools, not OPA-only
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 14.2 — required status checks list naming `coverage-gate` as a single required check (tasks.md:270); cross-ref tasks.md:264 (OPA), 274 (Playwright), 371 (SonarQube) all in coverage-gate.yml
confirmed_edits.change: Add a note (in task 14.2 or task 21) that the single required status check `coverage-gate` co-hosts three independent tools in `.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml`: OPA/Conftest (task 14.1/15... tasks.md:264), Playwright (task 15.1, tasks.md:274), and SonarQube (task 22.1, tasks.md:371). Because all three run under one check name, a Playwright or SonarQube failure blocks merge under the same name, so the OPA policy is NOT independently gating and REQ-GATE-003's 'THE OPA_Conftest required CI status check SHALL run' is satisfied only as a co-tenant. Either (a) document this co-tenancy explicitly so the OPA leg's gating role is traceable, or (b) split the OPA policy into its own job/required check so it gates independently.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve the Postgres-fallback contradiction with the file-document input model
confirmed_edits.anchor: Notes — 'when `db_connection.health_check()` fails ... the OPA merge policy MUST fall back to the file-backed state (feature_list.json + claude-progress.txt) and reach the SAME allow/block decision it would on Postgres' (tasks.md:730)
confirmed_edits.change: Reconcile tasks.md:730 with the canonical input model. As written, 'falling back to feature_list.json' presumes the OPA policy's PRIMARY input is the Postgres evidence_records table (design.md:1052) — but a Conftest .rego policy already takes feature_list.json as its ONLY input (tasks.md:264), making the fallback a no-op under the document model. Once the coverage-rego-input-model edit makes the feature_list.json document canonical, remove the OPA merge policy from the Postgres-fallback clause at tasks.md:730 (its input is already file-backed), OR if a separate SQL-backed merge check is kept, name it as a distinct non-Conftest gate so the fallback statement is coherent.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix the formal_verification_merged.py CI-invocation path mismatch
confirmed_edits.anchor: Phase 0 Component List row 10 — '`python3 formal_verification_merged.py` exits 0 (all 34 checks pass)' (design.md:632); same bare path in tasks.md:252 and tasks.md:301
confirmed_edits.change: Update the invocation path from `python3 formal_verification_merged.py` to `python3 verification/formal_verification_merged.py` in design.md:632 and tasks.md:252-254 / task 17.1 (tasks.md:301), because the harness actually lives at `verification/formal_verification_merged.py` (verified: no file at repo root). The spec wires a bare repo-root path into CI (task 13.1) that does not match the file's real location — a CI-wiring defect that would make the formal-verification required check fail to find the harness.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a dedicated 'Coverage Merge Policy' design subsection for coverage_query.rego
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory row '`coverage_query.rego` | `.github/policies/coverage_query.rego` | 1 | OPA/Conftest policy; denies merge if any requirement has zero passing evidence.' (design.md:137) — the ONLY dedicated mention; no subsection exists
confirmed_edits.change: Add a 'Coverage Merge Policy' design subsection bringing coverage_query.rego to parity with the stop_hook.py / plan-approved.json code-bearing subsections. It must state: (1) the canonical input model (feature_list.json Conftest document); (2) an example Rego policy body with the two deny rules over `input.items`; (3) the in_scope filter, the `status != "proven"` rule (covers failed+unproven), and the four-field Evidence_Record completeness rule; (4) default-deny error semantics (empty/missing/parse-error => deny); (5) the workflow wiring (coverage-gate.yml co-tenancy + required-check registration). This consolidates dedup_keys coverage-rego-input-model / in-scope-filter / failed-status / four-field-evidence / fail-closed into one authoritative location; the individual tasks.md edits remain the task-level mirrors.

refuted.item: open_gap: 'OPA merge decisions are NOT audit-logged' (REQ-AUDIT-001 producer wiring missing for the OPA/Conftest merge gate; cit requirements.md:457,460, design.md:144, tasks.md:970)
refuted.why: Not a coverage_query.rego defect and already addressed at the audit-log layer. REQ-AUDIT-001.4 (requirements.md:460), design.md:144, and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) scope the audit-log PRODUCER explicitly to the three hooks (stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, subagent_stop_hook.py). The merge-time chain-verification side IS gated: task 40.3 / 52.2 register `audit-chain-verify` as a required CI check at merge (tasks.md:700-703, 969). Whether CI-side gate decisions (OPA, SonarQube, Playwright) should also be audit-log producers is a cross-cutting REQ-AUDIT-001 scope question about the audit_log component, not a coverage_query.rego gap — it belongs to the audit-log component's findings, not this one. Refuted here to avoid mis-scoping it onto the .rego file.

refuted.item: open_gap: ''approved set' provenance undefined' — no component defines how the policy obtains the approved requirement set; suggests binding to plan-approved.json.feature_list_sha (cit requirements.md:205, design.md:1052,762-769)
refuted.why: Largely dissolves once the input-model contradiction is fixed, and is over-engineered as stated. Under the canonical feature_list.json document model (see coverage-rego-input-model edit), the 'approved set' is simply the in_scope items in the feature_list.json that Conftest evaluates — there is no separate SQL 'approved_req_ids' to source. The plan-approved.json.feature_list_sha binding (design.md:792) is already enforced at the PreToolUse plan gate, which blocks writes when feature_list.json diverges from the approved SHA; by merge time the gated file is the approved one. Adding a second SHA-binding inside the Rego policy duplicates an existing gate. The residual ('approved set = in_scope items') is already folded into the coverage-rego-in-scope-filter and coverage-rego-design-subsection edits, so no separate edit is warranted.

refuted.item: critic overstatement: 'the draft claims REQ-GATE-001 (10.1) is addressed indirectly and Property 4/22 cover it, but Property 22 Validates 10.3 only and Property 4 covers prediction-independence only' (cit design.md:1050-1054, 906-910)
refuted.why: This is a critique of the DRAFT findings file's own ears_stories trace, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The artifacts are internally consistent: Property 22 correctly Validates 10.3 (design.md:1054) and Property 4 correctly Validates 10.1/13.6/19.2 on the prediction-independence axis (design.md:910). There is no text in requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md to fix here. The substantive residue — that the OPA policy cannot observe live 'passing tests' (REQ-GATE-001's middle limb) — is captured separately below.

refuted.item: critic weak finding: 'REQ-GATE-001 passing-tests limb is structurally unobservable to a Conftest document policy' (a Rego policy over static feature_list.json can read status/evidence but cannot observe live passing tests; cit requirements.md:203)
refuted.why: Not a defect in the current artifacts and not a coverage_query.rego responsibility gap. REQ-GATE-001 (requirements.md:203) computes the verdict from THREE facts across MULTIPLE gates, not one: 'passing tests' is observed by the Verifier's semantic layer (design.md:259, REQ-VERIFY-001) and recorded into the Evidence_Record's output_hash before an item is flipped proven; the OPA policy then checks for present, complete evidence. The Completion_Gate is explicitly a multi-component layer (Glossary: 'Stop hook + OPA/Conftest CI check + GitHub required status check', requirements.md:21). No single gate is required to observe all three limbs; expecting the .rego file to observe live test execution misreads the architecture. The evidence-presence check the policy DOES owe is already covered by the coverage-rego-four-field-evidence edit.

confirmed_edits.title: Schema file does not exist — materialize it and check off task 2.1

Every Phase-0 gate (PreToolUse status guard, feature_list_init validation at tasks.md:240, design.md:623 acceptance smoke) depends on this concrete artifact existing; today there is no schema/ directory and git ls-files returns nothing.

confirmed_edits.title: Add if/then so evidence is required when status==proven
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:381-384 (CoverageItem.evidence $ref with prose 'Required when status = proven') and design.md:372-374 (status enum)

Without it an item with status:proven and no evidence object validates, leaving Property 2 (design.md:890-894) and Req 5.3/5.6 unenforceable by the schema alone.
