confirmed_edits.title: Add minLength:1 to EvidenceRecord string fields (non-empty enforcement)
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:392-409 (EvidenceRecord.properties test_file/test_name/collected_at) and Property 2 'present and non-empty' at design.md:892
confirmed_edits.change: Add `"minLength": 1` to test_file, test_name, and collected_at (output_hash and acceptance_criteria items are already de-facto non-empty via pattern/minItems). Req 5.6 and Property 2 demand each of the four fields be 'present AND non-empty', but `required`+`type:string` accepts the empty string. EITHER add minLength here OR change Property 2 (design.md:890-894) to state non-emptiness is delegated to evidence_collector.validate_evidence_record (tasks.md:46) rather than the schema — pick one and make schema/Property consistent.

confirmed_edits.title: actor_agent forbidden by additionalProperties:false but NOT NULL in Postgres + Property 24
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:387-390 (EvidenceRecord required=4 fields, additionalProperties:false) vs design.md:473 (evidence_records.actor_agent TEXT NOT NULL) vs design.md:1066-1072 (Property 24, Validates Req 9.2)
confirmed_edits.change: Add `actor_agent` (type:string, minLength:1) to the JSON-Schema EvidenceRecord properties so the schema and the Postgres NOT NULL column agree, OR change additionalProperties to allow it. As written, an EvidenceRecord carrying actor_agent (required by the DB CHECK and by Property 24's 'actor_agent must be the Verifier') FAILS jsonschema.validate, and one that passes the schema cannot satisfy the NOT-NULL column — a real schema/DB/Property-24 three-way divergence. Reconcile in design.md:387-411 and note that EvidenceRecord field count becomes 5 (the four-field gate stays the proven-transition gate; actor_agent is provenance).

confirmed_edits.title: Make provenance required in schema to match NOT NULL Postgres column and Req 2.3
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:324 (CoverageItem.required omits provenance) and design.md:365-369 (provenance enum) vs design.md:438 (requirements.provenance TEXT NOT NULL CHECK)
confirmed_edits.change: Add `provenance` to CoverageItem.required (design.md:324) so the JSON schema matches the NOT NULL Postgres column (design.md:438) and Req 2.3 (requirements.md:79: 'SHALL mark inferred-vs-stated provenance on each requirement'). Today a schema-valid item can omit provenance entirely while the DB rejects it, so the file and DB disagree on whether provenance is mandatory. (Note: governing requirement is Req 2.3, not Req 4.3.) Alternatively document explicitly that provenance lives only in Postgres and is intentionally optional in the JSON file.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire schema validation into pre_tool_use_hook status guard
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:131-136 (task 6.1, pre_tool_use_hook check functions) and design.md:138 ('PreToolUse validates against this')
confirmed_edits.change: Add a sub-bullet to task 6.1 (tasks.md:131-136): 'load schema/feature_list.schema.json and call jsonschema.validate on the proposed feature_list.json write; block (exit 2) on ValidationError'. design.md:138 claims PreToolUse validates writes against the schema, but the only task that loads the schema is feature_list_init.py (tasks.md:240); no hook task wires jsonschema.validate into the PreToolUse path, so the design.md:138 claim has no implementing code path.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Req 5.6 to task 6.1 _Requirements to match design.md:215 status-guard mapping
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:137 (task 6.1 _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3, 7.4, 7.5, 13.2, 13.3, 13.4, 18.1) vs design.md:215 (status guard enforces REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003, REQ-COV-006)
confirmed_edits.change: Add `5.6` to the _Requirements line of task 6.1 (tasks.md:137). The hook-wiring table at design.md:215 maps the PreToolUse status guard to REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003 AND REQ-COV-006, but the building task that implements check_status_transition omits 5.6, so the four-field/non-empty gate (Req 5.6) is mapped to the status guard in design but not traced to it in the task.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a task implementing the design.md:623 schema negative-validation acceptance
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:24-41 (task 2 / 2.1 / 2.2 / 2.3) and design.md:623 (Phase-0 spine row 1 acceptance: 'Schema validates a hand-written valid example; rejects an example missing output_hash')
confirmed_edits.change: Add a non-optional subtask under task 2 (e.g., 2.4) that implements the design.md:623 acceptance directly: jsonschema.validate a hand-written valid feature_list.json passes, and an item whose evidence is missing output_hash is rejected, against schema/feature_list.schema.json. The smoke/integration tests (task 16.1 tasks.md:281-288, task 17.1) only exercise the four hooks and target subagent_stop_hook's output_hash check — not jsonschema.validate against the schema file — and tasks 2.2/2.3 are optional ([ ]*) hypothesis property tests, so the component's own stated pass condition has no implementing task.

confirmed_edits.title: Correct Property 1 'Validates' citation (drop dangling 5.4)
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:886 (Property 1 'Validates: Requirements 5.1, 5.4')
confirmed_edits.change: Change Property 1's 'Validates' line to Requirements 5.1 (and add 5.7 for the in_scope default if desired) and drop 5.4. Property 1's body (non-empty id, type enum, ≥1 acceptance criterion, status default unproven) maps to Req 5.1; Req 5.4 is the WIRING/NFR-first-class requirement, which Property 1 does not assert, so the 5.4 citation is dangling. Also align task 2.2 (tasks.md:33) and task 2.1 (tasks.md:29), which disagree (2.2 says 5.1,5.4; 2.1 says 5.1,5.3,5.6).

confirmed_edits.title: Add ID pattern to dependencies items (or note referential integrity is ungated)
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:354-358 (dependencies array, items {type:string}, no pattern) vs design.md:326-329 (id with pattern ^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$)
confirmed_edits.change: Apply the same `"pattern": "^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$"` to the items of dependencies so a dependency at least has a well-formed ID shape, OR add an explicit note that dependency referential integrity (the referenced ID actually exists and is proven) is delegated to the hook/verifier and is intentionally not schema-enforceable. Today a dependency can be any string, including a malformed or non-existent ID, and still validate, which the artifact never acknowledges.

confirmed_edits.title: Add enum-sync consistency test (JSON schema enums vs Postgres CHECK / spec_validator)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:24-29 (task 2) and design.md:333,347 (JSON type/ears_pattern enums) vs design.md:434,436 (requirements table: type CHECK present, ears_pattern TEXT NOT NULL with NO CHECK)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a consistency test task asserting the JSON schema's `type` enum {functional,NFR,WIRING} equals the coverage/requirements Postgres `type` CHECK (design.md:434) and the spec_validator pattern set, and that the schema's `ears_pattern` enum (5 base patterns, design.md:347) is kept in sync with spec_validator (design.md:1188) — note that requirements.ears_pattern at design.md:436 is `TEXT NOT NULL` with NO CHECK, so that enum can silently drift. There is currently no task ensuring these three enum sources stay aligned.

refuted.item: Status-transition legality is not enforced by the schema (open_gap 4): proven→unproven/proven→failed not blocked at schema layer; relies on unbuilt status guard.
refuted.why: Already covered and correctly attributed. design.md:374 (status enum description), design.md:215 (PreToolUse status guard maps the permitted edges and REQ-COV-002/003/006), and Property 3 (design.md:898-902) all state transition legality is hook-enforced, not schema-enforced. The finding's own fix ('state explicitly that transition legality is hook-enforced') is already done. task 6.1 (tasks.md:135) implements check_status_transition. The only genuine residual is the Req 5.6 traceability omission, captured separately as a confirmed edit.

refuted.item: WIRING integration-test obligation (Req 8.3/5.4) is free-text only; schema cannot distinguish a WIRING EvidenceRecord (open_gap 5 / ears gap Req 8.3).
refuted.why: Correctly delegated, not a gap. design.md:334 describes the obligation, Req 8.3 (requirements.md:174) states it, and task 47 (tasks.md:959) is a dedicated non-optional task: 'a WIRING item flips proven only with an integration test exercising the real execution path attached as Evidence_Record.' Adding an `if type==WIRING then evidence.test_file matches tests/integration/...` schema conditional would be brittle over-specification; the obligation is properly verifier/hook-enforced and already tasked.

refuted.item: Append-only / never-reordered invariant (design.md:288) is unenforceable by schema and asserted only in prose; no concrete diff/order-check mechanism specified (open_gap 8).
refuted.why: The mechanism is named. design.md:214 (PreToolUse artifact guard: 'any tool targeting feature_list.json schema ... destructive operations') plus the status guard (design.md:215) and Property 3 (identity-mutation blockade, design.md:898-902) and task 6.4 (Property 3 test) cover delete/reorder/identity-mutation blocking via PreToolUse. The schema is never claimed to enforce append-only. A finer prefix-sequence check is an enhancement, not a true current gap.

refuted.item: Overstatement: CHECK-7a/7b/7c are attributed as evidence FOR the schema component but are generic Z3 Bools (critic finding 'overstatement').
refuted.why: This is a critique of the draft findings file's own evidence framing, not a defect in the canonical artifacts (requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md/formal_verification_merged.py). Confirmed by reading formal_verification_merged.py:301-338: evidenceComplete is over free Z3 Bools with no reference to the schema file — but that is the correct, intended abstraction level for the harness and the spec text never claims the Z3 checks validate the JSON file. No artifact edit follows.

refuted.item: EARS-pattern reconciliation citation is under-surfaced (critic 'weak' finding re design.md:1187-1189).
refuted.why: Already present and correct in the canonical text. design.md:1187-1189 explicitly states the Complex pattern is a documented composition and the ears_pattern enum + spec_validator operate over the five base patterns with 'No schema change.' Nothing to fix; this is a citation-precision note about the draft, not an artifact gap.

refuted.item: citation_issues: requirements.md:15 mislabeled as Phase-0 spine, and requirements.md:79 mislabeled Req 4.3 instead of Req 2.3.
refuted.why: Verified accurate (line 15 = '## Glossary'; line 79 = Req 2.3 provenance criterion; Phase-0 spine is at requirements.md:9), but these are corrections to the draft findings file's internal citations, not defects in the canonical artifacts. The substantive provenance/Req-2.3 point is folded into the confirmed provenance-required edit, which uses the correct Req 2.3 attribution.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify canonical (deterministic-JSON) form for feature_list_sha
confirmed_edits.anchor: ## `plan-approved.json` Gate — line 791: `current_sha = compute_sha256(Path("feature_list.json").read_bytes())` and line 801 prose
confirmed_edits.change: Replace the raw-bytes SHA with a canonical-form SHA: compute feature_list_sha over a deterministic/sorted-key JSON serialization of feature_list.json (mirroring the gate_audit_log 'Canonical form = deterministic JSON' rule at design.md:1170), so that whitespace-only or key-reordering rewrites the human did not review do not break the bind. State this canonical rule in the `plan-approved.json` Gate subsection AND add it to the glossary entry (requirements.md:43). Update the hook snippet to canonicalize before hashing (e.g., `compute_sha256(canonical_json(json.loads(Path('feature_list.json').read_text())))`).

confirmed_edits.title: Model the feature_list_sha-mismatch block path in Z3 and scope Invariant 3 wording
confirmed_edits.anchor: CHECK-4f at lines 250-253 (`codeWrite, Not(planApproved)` → unsat); Bool decls at lines 68-69
confirmed_edits.change: Add a Boolean `shaMatches = Bool("shaMatches")` and a core axiom that an implementation write requires BOTH presence and a matching SHA, e.g. `Implies(codeWrite, And(planApproved, shaMatches))`. Add CHECK-4g asserting `codeWrite ∧ planApproved ∧ ¬shaMatches` is UNSAT (the SHA-mismatch block path). Then narrow Critical Invariant 3 (requirements.md:369) and the CHECK-4f description so the existing CHECK-4f claim is scoped to the PRESENCE branch only, and the new CHECK-4g covers the SHA bind — so the docs no longer imply the whole gate (including the cryptographic SHA core) is proven by CHECK-4f alone.

confirmed_edits.title: Add EARS criterion + glossary text backing the feature_list_sha bind
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 18 acceptance criteria (lines 324-328); glossary entry `plan-approved.json` (line 43); Property 6 'Validates: 7.4, 18.1' (design.md:926)
confirmed_edits.change: Add acceptance criterion 18.4: 'IF plan-approved.json exists BUT its feature_list_sha does not equal the canonical SHA-256 of the current feature_list.json, THEN the PreToolUse_Hook SHALL block all implementation Write/Edit/MultiEdit tools and require re-approval.' Add the SHA-binding to the glossary definition at requirements.md:43 (currently presence-only) so the glossary matches the load-bearing design behavior. Update Property 6's 'Validates' line (design.md:926) to cite the new 18.4 alongside 7.4/18.1, since neither 7.4 nor 18.1 states the SHA rule.

confirmed_edits.title: Add plan_approved.schema.json, validate before indexing, and test the corrupt-file path
confirmed_edits.anchor: Inventory row `schema/` (design.md:138); check_plan_approval bare index `approval["feature_list_sha"]` (design.md:792); tasks 6.1 (tasks.md:132) and smoke tests (tasks.md:285, 295-296)
confirmed_edits.change: Author a sibling `schema/plan_approved.schema.json` (required: approved_by, approved_at, feature_list_sha, spec_version; notes optional) and have check_plan_approval validate the parsed approval object against it BEFORE indexing `approval["feature_list_sha"]`, so a present-but-malformed file (missing key / non-JSON) fails closed via an explicit schema check rather than relying on the bare KeyError + try/except. Add a smoke/integration test for the present-but-corrupt case (currently only the ABSENT case is tested at tasks.md:285 and 295) asserting exit 2 when plan-approved.json exists but is malformed or missing feature_list_sha.

confirmed_edits.title: Bind or explicitly demote plan-approved.json.spec_version
confirmed_edits.anchor: `plan-approved.json` example, `"spec_version": "1.0.0"` (design.md:767); hook never reads it (design.md:790-798)
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve spec_version dead-metadata: EITHER bind it (check_plan_approval also compares approval.spec_version against the spec version recorded in the durable store / spec header and blocks on mismatch, so a stale approval that still matches feature_list_sha cannot unlock) OR explicitly document it as advisory-only metadata in the `plan-approved.json` Gate subsection. State which in design.md.

confirmed_edits.title: Wire the 18.2 missing-context 'notes' injection to a consumer or drop the phrasing
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 18.2 (requirements.md:327, 'present them in plan mode for human approval and missing-context injection'); design.md:768-769 `notes` field
confirmed_edits.change: Req 18.2 makes 'missing-context injection' a SHALL obligation but the only realization is a free-text `notes` field consumed by nothing. EITHER specify a consumer in design.md (e.g. the Initializer reads plan-approved.json.notes on next session and injects it into run_state / the Implementer's context, with a task to do so) OR strike 'and missing-context injection' from the 18.2 criterion so the SHALL no longer promises an unimplemented behavior. As written the obligation is unaddressed, not merely 'partial'.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify revocation / staleness semantics for plan-approved.json
confirmed_edits.anchor: `plan-approved.json` Gate subsection (design.md:758-801); no expiry/revoke/nonce exists anywhere in design.md or requirements.md
confirmed_edits.change: Add an explicit operator revoke path (operator deletes/invalidates plan-approved.json to re-block writes) as a documented action, and optionally an `expires_at` or `approved_for_session`/nonce field so a stale approval cannot silently authorize a much later run for the same coverage model. Today the only de-facto revocation is mutating feature_list.json to force a SHA mismatch, which is never specified as an operator action.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve plan-approved.json against repo root, not cwd
confirmed_edits.anchor: `plan_path = Path("plan-approved.json")` and `Path("feature_list.json")` (design.md:781, 791); inventory notation `/plan-approved.json` (design.md:139); worktree cwd isolation (design.md:514-527)
confirmed_edits.change: The hook resolves plan-approved.json (and feature_list.json) cwd-relative, but the system deliberately runs agents inside per-slice git worktrees whose cwd is NOT the repo root — so the root-level marker is missed and the gate mis-fires. Pin resolution to the repo root explicitly (e.g. `repo_root = Path(subprocess.check_output(['git','rev-parse','--show-toplevel']).decode().strip()); plan_path = repo_root / 'plan-approved.json'`) for BOTH plan-approved.json and feature_list.json, and reconcile the inventory's `/plan-approved.json` notation (design.md:139) with the resolved path used in code.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a Phase 0 producer task: author plan_approved.schema.json + human authoring doc, cross-link from Initializer hand-off
confirmed_edits.anchor: plan-approved.json appears in tasks.md only as a consumer (6.1 at line 132) and in tests (16.1 line 285, 17.1 lines 295-296); Initializer hand-off step (tasks.md:204 'enter plan mode; do NOT write plan-approved.json')
confirmed_edits.change: Add a Phase 0 task to (a) author `schema/plan_approved.schema.json`, and (b) document the human authoring procedure (how a human writes plan-approved.json correctly and how to compute the canonical feature_list_sha). Cross-link this from the Initializer's plan-mode hand-off step (tasks.md:204) so the single file that unlocks ALL implementation has a documented producer/format task, not only consumer/test references.

confirmed_edits.title: State whether a non-byte amendment (Postgres-only) leaves a stale plan-approved.json validly unlocking writes
confirmed_edits.anchor: `plan-approved.json` Gate (SHA over feature_list.json bytes only, design.md:791); requirement_versions reset (design.md:1152); Property 25 Amendment Monotonicity (design.md:1074-1080); Req 22 (requirements.md:433)
confirmed_edits.change: Document the interaction between Req 22 / Property 25 amendment versioning and the plan gate: when a requirement is amended and the coverage item re-enters `unproven` by mutating ONLY the Postgres requirement_versions / coverage_items tables (not feature_list.json bytes), the feature_list_sha still matches, so the existing plan-approved.json stays valid and continues to unlock implementation writes against a now-stale coverage model. Either require amendments to also rewrite feature_list.json (forcing a SHA re-block) or have the plan gate additionally check for any post-approval amendment / unproven re-entry. State the chosen rule so the stale-approval hole is closed.

refuted.item: critic_finding #1 — Req 4.5 / REQ-SPEC-005 ('request human plan approval') should be a first-class ears_story
refuted.why: Req 4.5 already exists verbatim in the artifact (requirements.md:109: 'WHEN violation_count == 0, THE System SHALL emit the validated spec and feature_list.json and request human plan approval before any implementation begins'). The finding is about the DRAFT's ears_stories array missing it, not a gap in the canonical artifacts. No artifact change is needed — the upstream gating criterion is present and addressed.

refuted.item: critic_finding #2 — plan gate as audit-log producer / dependency on audit_log.py + task 52.3 not surfaced
refuted.why: Already covered in the artifacts: the hook-wiring table row 'PreToolUse — plan gate' (design.md:210) explicitly states 'calls audit_log.append(...) on the decision ... REQ-AUDIT-001', and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) wires audit_log.append into pre_tool_use_hook.py on every allow/block. The finding's own complaint is that the DRAFT's dependencies/outputs did not surface this — a draft-metadata issue, not an artifact gap.

refuted.item: critic_finding #3 — CHECK-4f is purely propositional (no tool-set/file/path/existence model)
refuted.why: Duplicate. The actionable artifact fixes are already captured: the SHA-bind / presence-only scoping is confirmed_edit dedup_key unique:plan-approved-z3-sha-mismatch, and the cwd/worktree path-resolution defect it cites is confirmed_edit dedup_key unique:plan-approved-cwd-path-resolution. No additional edit.

refuted.item: critic_finding #4 — corrupt-file fail-closed is a distinct (untested) behavior
refuted.why: Duplicate of open_gap #4. The corrupt-path schema validation + smoke test is already captured by confirmed_edit dedup_key shared:plan-approved-schema-and-corrupt-test (design.md:816 documents 'missing or corrupt → fail closed'; the test/validation addition handles the corrupt branch).

refuted.item: critic_finding #5 — CHECK-4f wording overstates even the 'presence branch'
refuted.why: Duplicate of open_gap #2 / confirmed_edit unique:plan-approved-z3-sha-mismatch, which already scopes the Invariant 3 / CHECK-4f wording. The nuance that CHECK-4f is a tautological consistency check of the axiom is addressed by narrowing the same Invariant-3/CHECK-4f description; no separate edit.

refuted.item: critic_finding #6 — Req 18.2 missing-context is a SHALL, stronger than the draft's 'partial' framing
refuted.why: Duplicate of open_gap #6 / confirmed_edit shared:plan-approved-notes-injection. The fix (wire notes to a consumer OR strike the phrasing) already resolves the SHALL-vs-dead-field problem; the severity nuance does not require a separate edit.

confirmed_edits.title: requirement_versions column set in tasks.md 28.7 contradicts the authoritative DDL
confirmed_edits.anchor: 28.7 Write migration `db/migrations/007_requirement_versions.sql` — bullet: "`requirement_versions` table: FK to `requirements`, monotonically increasing `version`, `amended_at`, `amendment_reason`, and a flag/marker for whether the amended item has been re-proven"
confirmed_edits.change: Rewrite the 28.7 column bullet to mirror the design.md DDL (design.md:1141-1151) verbatim: columns `requirement_id` (FK), `version` INTEGER (monotonically increasing), `prior_text`, `new_text` NOT NULL, `author` NOT NULL, `rationale` NOT NULL, `created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), `UNIQUE (requirement_id, version)`. Drop the non-canonical names `amended_at`/`amendment_reason` and the standalone 're-proven flag' (re-proof is tracked via resetting `coverage_items.status` to `unproven`, per design.md:1152). Keep the prose 'on amendment the linked coverage item re-enters unproven and COMPLETE is blocked while un-reproven'.

confirmed_edits.title: actor_agent column missing from task 28.4 evidence_records description
confirmed_edits.anchor: 28.4 Write migration `db/migrations/004_evidence_records.sql` — bullet: "`evidence_records` table with FK to `requirements`, `evidence_complete` CHECK constraint enforcing no empty fields"
confirmed_edits.change: Add `actor_agent TEXT NOT NULL` to the 28.4 column list, annotated 'MUST be the Verifier, never the Implementer — Property 24 (design.md:473, 1066-1070)'. Update the _Requirements_ line to also cite 9.2. Without this a builder following the task omits actor_agent and silently breaks Property 24 and the Property 19 round-trip.

confirmed_edits.title: run_state DDL has no budget threshold column the Stop hook compares against
confirmed_edits.anchor: CREATE TABLE run_state (...) block at design.md:481-496; evaluate_stop branch at design.md:704 `if run_state.budget_exceeded:  # token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd`
confirmed_edits.change: Add a `token_budget_usd NUMERIC(10,4) NOT NULL` (operator-set DEFAULT) column to the run_state DDL, and define `budget_exceeded` as the derived predicate `token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd` (either a generated column or documented as computed in run_state_manager). Cross-reference the 1,000,000-token-per-slice budget (requirements.md:357, REQ-LOOP-001/002) and the design.md:823 circuit breaker. Also add the budget column to task 28.5 (currently lists only status CHECK, stop_hook_active, no_progress_n). Currently the Stop hook reads `run_state.budget_exceeded` against a threshold that has no schema backing.

confirmed_edits.title: Properties 25/26/27 cite the wrong Validates requirement NUMBER (PRD codes are right)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 25 line 1080 'Validates: Requirements 26 (REQ-COV-007)'; Property 26 line 1090 'Validates: Requirements 27 (REQ-STATE-005)'; Property 27 line 1100 'Validates: Requirements 25 (REQ-SPEC-016)'
confirmed_edits.change: Correct the requirement numbers to match the PRD codes and the cross-reference table at requirements.md:507-509: Property 25 → 'Validates: Requirements 22 (REQ-COV-007)'; Property 26 → 'Validates: Requirements 23 (REQ-STATE-005)'; Property 27 → 'Validates: Requirements 24 (REQ-SPEC-016)'. As written they point at Req 26 (Reasoning-Loop), Req 27 (Audit Log), and Req 25 (Performance/Accessibility) respectively — a self-contradiction against requirements.md and the harness CHECK-10/11/12 PRD tags.

confirmed_edits.title: No migration runner, schema_migrations bookkeeping, or apply-on-deploy step specified
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 28 header 'Postgres schema migrations' (tasks.md:423); only application path is the integration tests (28.9, 33.1) on a test Neon branch
confirmed_edits.change: Add a sub-task under 28 (and a matching design subsection) naming the migration runner and a `schema_migrations` tracking table: pick one of golang-migrate / dbmate / sqitch / alembic (decide a default), define the apply order = file-name prefix 001..008, and add a CI/deploy step that applies pending migrations to the target (non-test) Neon branch on merge/deploy — distinct from the test-branch application in 28.9. Currently no runner, no bookkeeping table, and no real-DB apply step exist anywhere.

confirmed_edits.title: No idempotency / re-run / rollback contract for the migrations
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 28 'Postgres schema migrations' (tasks.md:423-456); migration 008 gate_audit_log (28.8) is append-only
confirmed_edits.change: State the idempotency contract for the migration set: either use one-time tracked apply via the schema_migrations table OR `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`; declare whether DOWN/rollback files exist (and if not, say migrations are forward-only); explicitly document `gate_audit_log` (008) as create-once so a re-run does not error or duplicate the append-only log. No idempotency/rollback text exists today.

confirmed_edits.title: gate_audit_log append-only is convention-only — no DB-level mutation prevention
confirmed_edits.anchor: gate_audit_log DDL in the Merge Reconciliation Addendum (design.md:1156-1167); requirements.md:457 (REQ-AUDIT-001 append-only)
confirmed_edits.change: Add to migration 008 / the design DDL a DB-level append-only enforcement: `REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON gate_audit_log FROM <app_role>` and/or a `BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE` trigger that RAISEs. Today append-only relies solely on the hash chain (after-the-fact tamper detection) plus convention; the DB still accepts row mutation, which is weaker than REQ-AUDIT-001 intent. Add the corresponding DDL line to task 28.8.

confirmed_edits.title: evidence_complete CHECK does not enforce the sha256 output_hash format the JSON schema enforces
confirmed_edits.anchor: evidence_complete CONSTRAINT at design.md:474-477 (`output_hash <> ''`) vs the feature_list JSON schema output_hash pattern `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` at design.md:402
confirmed_edits.change: Either add a CHECK that `output_hash ~ '^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$'` to the evidence_complete constraint (and to task 28.4) so the DB matches the JSON-schema format, OR add an explicit note that output_hash format enforcement is delegated to the JSON-schema / evidence_collector layer. As-is, a malformed hash passes the DB CHECK (non-empty only) but fails JSON-schema validation — a DB/JSON enforcement asymmetry.

confirmed_edits.title: No index DDL anywhere; migrations component lacks an index/performance design narrative
confirmed_edits.anchor: Postgres Schema section (design.md:425-509) and inventory row `db/migrations/` (design.md:140); Property 22 OPA COUNT against evidence_records (design.md:1052)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a Migrations design subsection (apply order, owning Postgres role, required indexes) and add the index DDL to the relevant 28.x tasks. At minimum: index on `evidence_records.requirement_id` (Property 22 per-requirement COUNT), `traceability_links.requirement_id` (queried in both directions un-indexed), and `coverage_items.requirement_id`. The current schema has zero CREATE INDEX statements.

confirmed_edits.title: evidence_records has no uniqueness/index to dedup or support requirement_id+commit_sha lookup
confirmed_edits.anchor: 28.4 Write migration `db/migrations/004_evidence_records.sql`; Property 19 retrieval by requirement_id+commit_sha (design.md:1028) and Property 22 COUNT (design.md:1052)
confirmed_edits.change: Add to migration 004 a `UNIQUE (requirement_id, commit_sha, test_name)` constraint (or at least an index on `(requirement_id, commit_sha)`) so duplicate evidence rows for the same requirement/commit/test are rejected and Property 19 lookup is indexed. Without it, duplicate inserts are silently allowed and can inflate the Property 22 OPA COUNT.

confirmed_edits.title: Postgres store cannot reconstruct acceptance_criteria / dependencies / priority — REQ-16.3 fidelity gap
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements DDL design.md:431-440 (has priority, lacks acceptance_criteria & dependencies) and coverage_items DDL design.md:443-451 (lacks priority & dependencies) vs feature_list JSON schema required set `[id, type, priority, dependencies, acceptance_criteria, status, in_scope]` at design.md:324; REQ-16.3 at requirements.md:298
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve the source-of-truth fidelity gap one of two ways: (a) add `acceptance_criteria` and `dependencies` storage (TEXT[]/JSONB columns or a child table) to requirements, and add `priority`+`dependencies` to coverage_items, so the DB can reconstruct Property 1 (≥1 acceptance criterion) and Property 7 prior-slice ordering (priority/dependencies, design.md:336-358, 932); OR (b) explicitly scope REQ-16.3 (requirements.md:298) so feature_list.json is canonical for acceptance criteria/dependencies/priority and Postgres mirrors only the coverage-status subset. Pick one and make design + tasks + requirements consistent. This single root cause covers both the missing acceptance_criteria and the missing slice-ordering dependencies/priority.

confirmed_edits.title: Property 22 prose claims a Postgres COUNT but the built Conftest policy reads feature_list.json
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 22 EARS prose at design.md:1052 (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM evidence_records WHERE requirement_id = ANY(approved_req_ids)`) vs task 21.1 (tasks.md:355 'Accept feature_list.json as Conftest input; policy reads items[_].status') and Phase-0 stub task 14.1 (tasks.md:264-265)
confirmed_edits.change: Reconcile Property 22's store: either (a) rewrite the Property 22 prose so the gate is expressed over the feature_list.json embedded evidence object that Conftest actually reads (since no task wires Conftest to Postgres), keeping the evidence_records SQL as an optional/secondary check; OR (b) add a task that exports/queries evidence_records into the Conftest input. As written, design asserts a SQL COUNT against evidence_records that the realized merge gate never performs — feature_list.json is the store the gate queries.

confirmed_edits.title: Property 22 counts approved requirements without excluding out-of-scope (Req 5.7) and evidence_records has no in_scope linkage
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 22 prose design.md:1050-1052 (counts over approved_req_ids, no in_scope filter) vs Req 5.7 'all completion and Stop gates SHALL count ONLY in_scope items' (requirements.md:128); coverage_items.in_scope at design.md:448
confirmed_edits.change: Make Property 22 honor Req 5.7: restrict the approved-requirement set (and the evidence count) to in_scope coverage items by joining to coverage_items.in_scope (or pre-filtering approved_req_ids to in_scope ones). evidence_records itself has no in_scope column, so the query must reach in_scope via coverage_items. Without this, an out-of-scope-but-approved requirement with zero evidence either wrongly fails the merge or the gate silently ignores the in_scope rule.

confirmed_edits.title: in_scope flip to false has no DB-level enforcement or audit of the human-authored transition
confirmed_edits.anchor: coverage_items.in_scope comment design.md:448 ('leaves scope only via a human-authored transition'); requirements.md:128 (human-authored only); no DB mechanism exists
confirmed_edits.change: Specify enforcement for the 'human-authored only' in_scope=false rule: either document that the hook/audit layer captures every in_scope flip into gate_audit_log (actor + justification), or add a DB trigger requiring an actor/justification on UPDATE that sets in_scope=false. Today the DB accepts any UPDATE flipping in_scope, and since gates count only in_scope items an unguarded flip silently drops a requirement from all completion gates.

confirmed_edits.title: Error Handling 'Postgres unavailable' row is thinner than the N-25 fallback contract it should mirror
confirmed_edits.anchor: Error Handling table row 'Postgres unavailable | Fall back to feature_list.json as sole source of truth for gate decisions. Log warning to OTel.' (design.md:815)
confirmed_edits.change: Expand the design.md:815 Error Handling row (or cross-reference tasks.md:730 N-25) so the design states the full fallback contract that already lives in tasks: every gate must reach the SAME allow/block decision on file-backed state (never fail-open), DB and file are kept as duplicates, the more conservative (blocking) result wins on divergence, and the gate_audit_log producer appends to a file-backed log when the DB is down and reconciles on reconnect. The requirement-level contract is captured in tasks.md:730 but the design Error Handling row reduces it to a one-liner.

refuted.item: COMPONENT DOES NOT EXIST ON DISK — no db/migrations/ directory or .sql files; tasks 28.x all unchecked
refuted.why: Verified true (find for *.sql returns nothing, no db/ dir), but this is the universal baseline condition of the entire repo — every Phase-2 component is spec-only and the tasks are correctly marked unchecked as Phase-2 work. design.md:140-142 and tasks.md:423-460 already state the eight migrations are Phase-2 and unbuilt. There is no actionable spec-text edit here; it is the premise of the audit, not a gap in the artifacts.

refuted.item: Postgres-unavailable fallback obligation is 'entirely absent' (conservative-divergence + file-backed audit-log reconnect)
refuted.why: REFUTED as 'absent': the full contract IS present in canonical text at tasks.md:730 (note N-25) — same allow/block decision, never fail-open, DB+file duplicates, more conservative result wins on divergence, gate_audit_log file-backed when DB down and reconciles on reconnect. The critic finding critiques the DRAFT summary, not the artifacts. The only real residue (design.md:815 Error Handling row is thinner than N-25) is captured separately as an add-wiring edit, dedup_key shared:postgres-fallback-n25-design-vs-tasks.

refuted.item: REQ-AUDIT-003 retention row marked 'addressed' overstates what the migration delivers — missing required-CI chain-verify wiring
refuted.why: The required-CI chain-verification wiring exists in the canonical artifacts: tasks.md:700 (task 40.3 registers `audit-chain-verify` as a REQUIRED status check) and tasks.md:969 (52.2 audit_verify.py, dual trigger), design.md:1108 and 1175 (Property 28 'required CI status check at merge PLUS on-demand'), requirements.md:459 (retention as execution-proof complement) and 462 (dual verification trigger). The retention-plus-verification contract is fully wired; this is a critique of the draft's 'addressed' label, not an artifact gap.

refuted.item: Migration 005 run_state column list 'fully addressed' overstatement (critic) — separate from the budget column
refuted.why: Merged into the CONFIRMED budget edit (unique:runstate-token-budget-column). The only genuine run_state schema gap is the missing token_budget_usd / budget_exceeded backing for the Stop hook; the rest of the column enumeration critique is a draft-wording issue, not a distinct artifact defect.

confirmed_edits.title: design.md Property 25 cites wrong requirement number AND non-canonical CHECK token
confirmed_edits.anchor: **Validates: Requirements 26 (REQ-COV-007). Machine-checked: CHECK-10 (`formal_verification_merged.py`).**
confirmed_edits.change: Rewrite the Property 25 Validates line (design.md:1080) to: '**Validates: Requirement 22 (REQ-COV-007). Machine-checked: CHECK-10a/10b (`formal_verification_merged.py`).**' — REQ-COV-007 is Requirement 22 (requirements.md:409,432,507), NOT Requirement 26 (which is REQ-OBS-006 Reasoning-Loop Detection, requirements.md:413). The canonical Z3 identifier is the CHECK-10a/10b pair (formal_verification_merged.py:422,430; requirements.md:507), not the bare 'CHECK-10'.

confirmed_edits.title: task 28.7 column set conflicts with canonical requirement_versions DDL
confirmed_edits.anchor: - `requirement_versions` table: FK to `requirements`, monotonically increasing `version`, `amended_at`, `amendment_reason`, and a flag/marker for whether the amended item has been re-proven; on amendment the linked coverage item re-enters `unproven` and COMPLETE is blocked while any amended item is un-reproven
confirmed_edits.change: Reconcile task 28.7's column list (tasks.md:450) to the canonical DDL at design.md:1141-1151: rename `amended_at`→`created_at`, rename `amendment_reason`→`rationale`, add `prior_text` (nullable) and `new_text TEXT NOT NULL` and `author TEXT NOT NULL`, and DROP the 'flag/marker for whether the amended item has been re-proven' column. State explicitly that re-proof is observed via `coverage_items.status` flipping back (not a column on this table), so the two specs no longer describe different tables.

confirmed_edits.title: REQ-COV-007 requirement citations inconsistent across tasks 28.7, 39.15, and 48
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 48. Requirement-amendment versioning (**REQ-COV-007**) — `requirement_versions` table (migration `db/migrations/007_requirement_versions.sql`, task 28.7); on amendment re-enter `unproven`; block COMPLETE while any amended item un-reproven. Only `*→proven` requires a complete Evidence_Record. Property 25 (test in task 39.15). _Z3 CHECK-10a/10b._
confirmed_edits.change: Standardize all REQ-COV-007 task citations to Requirement 22. (a) Append a '_Requirements: 22 (REQ-COV-007)_' line to the currently-uncited parent task 48 (tasks.md:962, which ends at '_Z3 CHECK-10a/10b._' with no _Requirements:_ line). (b) Fix task 28.7's '_Requirements: 16.1 (REQ-COV-007)_' (tasks.md:451) — Req 16 is REQ-STORE-001..003 durable storage; change to '_Requirements: 22 (REQ-COV-007); 16.1 (durable storage)_'. (c) Fix task 39.15's '_Requirements: 5.2 (REQ-COV-007)_' (tasks.md:667) — Req 5 is the coverage model; change to '_Requirements: 22 (REQ-COV-007)_'.

confirmed_edits.title: No runtime writer wired for the amendment flow (insert version row + reset coverage status)
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 48. Requirement-amendment versioning (**REQ-COV-007**) — `requirement_versions` table (migration `db/migrations/007_requirement_versions.sql`, task 28.7); on amendment re-enter `unproven`; block COMPLETE while any amended item un-reproven. Only `*→proven` requires a complete Evidence_Record. Property 25 (test in task 39.15). _Z3 CHECK-10a/10b._
confirmed_edits.change: Add a subtask 48.1 naming the amendment producer/caller, mirroring the audit_log producer pattern (design.md:1173-1174): author `tools/amendment_handler.py` exposing `record_amendment(requirement_id, new_text, author, rationale)` that, in a single transaction, (1) inserts the next `requirement_versions` row and (2) resets the linked `coverage_items.status` to 'unproven'; and name the invoking actor (human operator action; not an autonomous agent). Without this, the table can be created but nothing populates it or triggers the status reset.

confirmed_edits.title: Amendment status-reset (proven→unproven) is blocked by the PreToolUse status guard's permitted-edge set
confirmed_edits.anchor: | `PreToolUse` — status guard | Any write to `feature_list.json` | Validates proposed status transition; checks evidence schema completeness *(Reconciliation 2026-06-15: `unproven→failed` and `failed→unproven` are now allowed; only `*→proven` requires a complete Evidence_Record)* | Transitions into `proven` without a complete Evidence_Record; any transition not in {`unproven→proven`, `unproven→failed`, `failed→unproven`} | REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003, REQ-COV-006 |
confirmed_edits.change: The amendment flow resets `coverage_items.status` from `proven` to `unproven` (design.md:1078,1152; Property 25), but `proven → unproven` is NOT in the permitted-edge set {unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven} listed at design.md:215, design.md:374, design.md:900, requirements.md:123, and requirements.md:129 — so the status guard would BLOCK the amendment reset. Add `proven → unproven` (human-authored / amendment-triggered) to the permitted-transition set in all five locations, scoped so it is allowed only on a requirement-amendment event (e.g. gated on a corresponding requirement_versions insert), and update Property 3 accordingly.

confirmed_edits.title: Amendment version monotonicity and initial-version seeding are asserted but not mechanized
confirmed_edits.change: Property 25 / design.md:1078 require a 'monotonically increasing version', but the only DDL constraint (UNIQUE (requirement_id, version), design.md:1150) permits gapped/out-of-order sequences (e.g. insert version=5 then version=3). Specify the mechanism near design.md:1150 and in task 28.7: compute `version = COALESCE(MAX(version),0)+1` inside the amendment_handler transaction (or a BEFORE INSERT trigger enforcing version = previous_max+1). Also specify seeding semantics: state whether the table holds amendments only (first amendment is version 1, its `prior_text` = the original approved text from requirements.id, hence `prior_text` is non-null after the first amendment) and what a null `prior_text` means (reserve for an optional seed/baseline row, or forbid null in practice).

confirmed_edits.title: Append-only 'never overwritten' guarantee has no DDL or named application-layer enforcement
confirmed_edits.anchor:
-- On amendment: insert a version row AND reset coverage_items.status to 'unproven'.
-- Completion gate already blocks COMPLETE while any item is 'unproven' (CHECK-10a).
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:1078 says prior version text 'shall be retained and never overwritten', but requirement_versions has no append-only protection (no REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE, no BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger). Add to the migration spec EITHER an append-only guard (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON requirement_versions, or a trigger that RAISEs on UPDATE/DELETE) OR an explicit note that append-only is enforced at the application layer, naming where (e.g. amendment_handler is the sole writer and never updates). Document alongside design.md:1152.

confirmed_edits.title: FK ON DELETE behavior and requirement→coverage_item reset mapping unspecified
confirmed_edits.anchor:
requirement_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES requirements(id),
    version        INTEGER NOT NULL,
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:1143's `REFERENCES requirements(id)` has no ON DELETE clause (defaults to NO ACTION). Decide and document ON DELETE RESTRICT (history must survive deletion of the requirement). Also clarify the join the amendment relies on: the row references `requirement_id`, but the status reset targets coverage_items — state the cardinality (requirement_id → coverage_item(s), 1:1 or 1:many) and how amendment_handler locates the coverage_item(s) to reset. Add an index on `requirement_id` for amendment-history lookups. Add near design.md:1143-1152.

confirmed_edits.title: Postgres glossary entry omits amendment history and audit log from durable-store contents
confirmed_edits.anchor: - **Postgres**: The managed relational database used as the durable store for requirements, coverage state, traceability links, evidence, and run state.
confirmed_edits.change: Update the Postgres glossary entry (requirements.md:44) to enumerate the now-eight-table durable store: add 'requirement-amendment history' and 'gate-decision audit log' to the list 'requirements, coverage state, traceability links, evidence, and run state' — both are first-class durable tables post-reconciliation (design.md:427 lists eight tables; migrations 007/008).

confirmed_edits.title: Phase-2 integration tests assert only generic existence for requirement_versions, not its constraints
confirmed_edits.anchor: - Against a test Neon branch: run all 8 migrations (001–008); assert all EIGHT tables exist (`requirements`, `coverage_items`, `traceability_links`, `evidence_records`, `run_state`, `domain_baseline_checklists`, `requirement_versions`, `gate_audit_log`) with correct columns and constraints *(Reconciliation 2026-06-15: was "all 6 migrations")*
confirmed_edits.change: Strengthen task 33.1 (tasks.md:501) and task 28.9 (tasks.md:459) to assert requirement_versions-specific constraint behavior, matching the granularity already applied to evidence_records/coverage_items: assert the `UNIQUE (requirement_id, version)` constraint rejects a duplicate (requirement_id, version) insert; assert `created_at` is populated by DEFAULT now() when omitted; assert the `requirements(id)` FK rejects a row with a dangling requirement_id. Currently both tasks cover this table only by generic 'correct columns and constraints'.

refuted.item: Open gap: 'The file does not exist on disk... mark the component status as not-yet-built in the design.md inventory.'
refuted.why: This is build-state, not a spec defect. The whole repo is pre-implementation (no db/, no .sql, no tools/*.py exist; verification/ and .kiro/ only). The design.md:141 inventory row describes what the file creates, not a claim it exists; tasks 28.7 and 48 are unchecked '[ ]', correctly tracking it as not-yet-built. No artifact text needs fixing for this — flagging it would misclassify normal pre-build state as a gap.

refuted.item: Critic finding: classify REQ-COV-007 by its EARS Unwanted sub-pattern and strengthen the note that the block-COMPLETE prohibition is the load-bearing clause this DDL cannot satisfy.
refuted.why: The substantive obligation (the prohibition: SHALL NOT permit COMPLETE while any amended item is un-reproven) IS already wired and machine-checked: requirements.md:433 / Requirement 22.1 carries the full Unwanted-pattern EARS clause, the Stop completion gate blocks while any in-scope item is unproven (design.md:728-734), and CHECK-10a proves 'amended ∧ ¬reproven ∧ complete' UNSAT (formal_verification_merged.py:422-427). The remaining real gap (the status-reset that feeds the unproven state) is already captured by the status-guard-edges and amendment-handler-wiring edits. A standalone 'classify by sub-pattern' edit is editorial and adds no wiring.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: Requirement 16.1 / REQ-STORE-001 durable-storage mapping for this component (cited by task 28.7 as 16.1).
refuted.why: Not a missing mapping — it is the same citation defect already captured by the REQ-COV-007 task-citation-standardization edit, which retains 16.1 as the durable-storage cross-cut while adding Requirement 22 as the substantive citation. Emitting a separate wiring edit would duplicate dedup_key 'unique:reqcov007-task-citation-standardization'.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: Glossary Coverage_Item / status enum dependency and Glossary Postgres entry (two separate ears stories).
refuted.why: The Postgres-glossary half is already covered by the 'requirements-postgres-glossary-durable-contents' edit. The Coverage_Item status-enum half is a true statement about a dependency, not a gap in the artifact: the status enum {unproven,proven,failed}, default unproven, and the failed↔unproven transitions are fully specified at requirements.md:123/129, design.md:374, and design.md:215; the only real defect there (missing proven→unproven edge) is already captured by 'status-guard-permitted-edges'. No additional artifact text is lacking.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin the write protocol that supplies DB-assigned seq/created_at to the hash producer
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1173 `--   * Producer: tools/audit_log.append(event, tool, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent),` (and requirements.md:461 Requirement 27.5; tasks.md:968 task 52.1)
confirmed_edits.change: Resolve the seq/created_at chicken-and-egg: entry_hash is defined to INCLUDE seq (BIGSERIAL, DB-assigned) and created_at (DEFAULT now(), DB-assigned) but append(event,tool,decision,reason,requirement_id,actor_agent) receives neither, so the hash cannot be computed before INSERT without an UPDATE (violating append-only). Pin ONE mechanism in design.md:1168-1177, requirements.md:461 (Req 27.5), and task 52.1: have append() pre-assign seq via `SELECT nextval('gate_audit_log_seq_seq')` and set created_at client-side, then INSERT the row with those exact values so the stored seq/created_at equal the hashed ones; OR drop seq+created_at from the canonical hashed tuple. State the chosen mechanism so audit_log.py and audit_verify.py compute identical hashes.

confirmed_edits.title: Make append-only DB-enforced in the 008 DDL (REVOKE + trigger), not advisory
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:454 (task 28.8 bullet) `- \`gate_audit_log\` append-only table: \`seq\` (monotonic) ...` and requirements.md:457 (Req 27.1) / design.md:1156-1167 DDL
confirmed_edits.change: append-only is asserted (Req 27.1 requirements.md:457; DDL header design.md:1155) but never enforced — no REVOKE, no trigger, no rule, so a superuser/agent can silently UPDATE/DELETE rows and tamper-evidence is detection-only. Add to task 28.8 and the design.md DDL block (design.md:1156-1167): `REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON gate_audit_log FROM <app_role>;` plus a `BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE` trigger that RAISEs an exception. Update requirements.md:457 to state append-only is DB-enforced (REVOKE + trigger), not merely advisory.

confirmed_edits.title: Normalize 'timestamp' to created_at in REQ-AUDIT-001 and note it is DB-assigned
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:457 `1. THE System SHALL append every gate decision (event, tool, allow/block, reason, requirement ID, actor agent, timestamp) ...`
confirmed_edits.change: Field-name drift: Req 27.1 lists 'timestamp', the DDL/canonical form name the column 'created_at' (design.md:1164,1170), and the producer append() signature omits it entirely. Change 'timestamp' to 'created_at (timestamp)' in requirements.md:457 and add a note in Req 27.4/27.5 that created_at is DB-assigned (DEFAULT now()) and therefore not a producer argument — one canonical name across all three surfaces.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix dangling Z3 'CHECK (audit chain)' citation in the cross-ref matrix
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:510 matrix row `| — (audit-log tamper-evidence) | Property 28 | CHECK (audit chain) | Requirement 27 |`
confirmed_edits.change: The matrix names Z3 group 'CHECK (audit chain)' for Property 28, but no such check exists in verification/formal_verification_merged.py (the new-8 group is CHECK-10a..13b = amendment, resumed-state, checklist, omission; grep for audit/chain/tamper/prev_hash/entry_hash returns zero). design.md:1110 itself says 'no Z3 check — runtime/CI verified'. Change the Z3-group cell from 'CHECK (audit chain)' to '— (runtime/CI, no Z3)' so the matrix matches design.md:1110 and the harness self-count (34 = 14+12+8, no audit check). Also correct the harness path reference to verification/formal_verification_merged.py where cited.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin the NULL/None canonical-JSON serialization rule for the hash
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1170-1172 `--   * Canonical form = deterministic JSON of (seq, event_name, tool_name, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent, created_at).` (and requirements.md:461 Req 27.5)
confirmed_edits.change: tool_name, reason, requirement_id are nullable (design.md:1159,1161,1162) but the canonical-JSON serialization of NULL is never pinned (omit key? json null? empty string?), so producer and verifier may serialize NULL differently and produce divergent entry_hash (spurious tamper failures). Pin in design.md:1168-1172 and Req 27.5 (requirements.md:461): keys ALWAYS present, NULL serialized as JSON `null` literal, `sort_keys=true`, UTF-8, no insignificant whitespace (compact separators), and require audit_log.py (task 52.1) and audit_verify.py (task 52.2) to import ONE shared canonicalizer function.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify how file-backed (DB-down) audit entries reconcile their seq on reconnect
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:730 `... The \`gate_audit_log\` producer (task 52.1) appends to the file-backed log when the DB is unavailable and reconciles on reconnect.`
confirmed_edits.change: Under DB-down, the producer appends to the file-backed log and reconciles on reconnect, but seq (BIGSERIAL) does not exist in the file backend and seq is part of the hashed canonical form — so DB-assigned seq on reconnect differs from any file placeholder and re-hashing cannot reproduce the file-time entry_hash; reconciliation is undefined. In tasks.md:730 and Req 27 (requirements.md:461), specify either (a) a client-generated monotonic seq (UUIDv7 / hybrid logical clock) that survives both backends and is what gets hashed, or (b) that file-era entry_hashes are explicitly recomputed and re-chained against DB-assigned seq on reconnect (and that audit_verify treats the reconnect boundary accordingly).

confirmed_edits.title: Decide FK from gate_audit_log.requirement_id to requirements(id), or document the exception
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1162 `    requirement_id TEXT,` (inside CREATE TABLE gate_audit_log)
confirmed_edits.change: Every sibling table FKs requirement_id to requirements(id) (design.md:445,456,467,484,1143) but gate_audit_log.requirement_id is bare nullable TEXT, so a decision can reference a non-existent requirement and still persist — weakening REQ-AUDIT-001 traceability. Either add `requirement_id TEXT REFERENCES requirements(id)` to the DDL (design.md:1162) and task 28.8, OR add a one-line comment in the DDL stating audit rows intentionally allow dangling/absent requirement_id (e.g. plan-gate decisions logged before any requirement is active) so the omission is a documented choice, not drift.

confirmed_edits.title: Correct shuffled requirement numbers in design.md Property 25–28 'Validates' lines
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1110 `**Validates: Requirements 28 (REQ-AUDIT-001/002). ...**` (and design.md:1080 Property 25, :1090 Property 26, :1100 Property 27)
confirmed_edits.change: The Property 25–28 'Validates' lines carry shuffled bare requirement numbers that contradict requirements.md headings and the requirements.md:507-510 matrix: design.md:1080 Property 25 says 'Requirements 26' but amendment/REQ-COV-007 = Requirement 22 (26 = Reasoning-Loop Detection); design.md:1090 Property 26 says 'Requirements 27' but resumed-state/REQ-STATE-005 = Requirement 23 (27 = the audit log); design.md:1100 Property 27 says 'Requirements 25' but checklist/REQ-SPEC-016 = Requirement 24 (25 = Perf/A11y); design.md:1110 Property 28 says 'Requirements 28' but audit/REQ-AUDIT = Requirement 27 (28 = Capability-Role Taxonomy). The parenthetical REQ-* tags are correct; only the numbers are wrong. Fix to: Property 25 -> Requirement 22, Property 26 -> Requirement 23, Property 27 -> Requirement 24.1, Property 28 -> Requirement 27 — matching requirements.md:431/435/440/455 and the matrix at requirements.md:507-510. (Property 28 line is the one directly on this component.)

confirmed_edits.title: Fix wrong machine-readable _Requirements_ tag on task 28.8 (16.1 -> 27/REQ-AUDIT)
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:456 `    - _Requirements: 16.1 (REQ-AUDIT-001..003)_`
confirmed_edits.change: Task 28.8 (write 008_gate_audit_log.sql) is tagged '_Requirements: 16.1 (REQ-AUDIT-001..003)_', but 16.1 is the spine-smoke-test requirement (tasks.md:281, 'Write tests/smoke/test_spine_e2e.py') — '16.1' is a boilerplate phase tag copy-pasted across the whole Phase-2 migration block (tasks.md:421,426,431,435,439,447,451,456). Change task 28.8's tag to '_Requirements: 27 (REQ-AUDIT-001..003)_' so the machine-readable pointer matches the actual requirement. (The sibling migration tags carrying boilerplate 16.1 are a broader pattern, but 28.8 is the one for this component.)

confirmed_edits.title: Operationalize REQ-AUDIT-003 retention (window / archival / immutability)
confirmed_edits.anchor: requirements.md:459 `3. THE System SHALL retain the hash-chained gate-decision log as the execution-proof complement to SLSA build provenance ...`
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-AUDIT-003 asserts 'SHALL RETAIN' the log as a long-lived compliance substrate but no retention window, archival/partitioning strategy, storage location, or immutability-of-archived-segments is defined anywhere in requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md. Add a concrete retention sub-clause to Req 27 (requirements.md:459) and a corresponding design note near design.md:1156-1177: define the minimum retention duration, whether/how old segments are archived or partitioned, and that archived segments remain hash-verifiable (the chain extends across archive boundaries), then wire it as a task under task 52.

confirmed_edits.title: Add verification-access INDEX(es) to gate_audit_log for chain-walk and lookups
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:1156-1167 (CREATE TABLE gate_audit_log) and tasks.md:701 (`Run python3 tools/audit_verify.py to recompute the hash chain over gate_audit_log`)
confirmed_edits.change: audit_verify.py and the required audit-chain-verify CI check (tasks.md:700-701) full-scan gate_audit_log ordered by seq on an unbounded append-only table (one row per gate decision, written by all three hooks on every allow/block per design.md:209-217). Add to the design.md DDL block and task 28.8 a `CREATE INDEX ON gate_audit_log (requirement_id)` (and/or a session/created_at index), or state explicitly that bounded whole-chain re-hash at merge is acceptable given the retention policy.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin the resumed-state hash algorithm (inputs, serialization, hash function)
confirmed_edits.anchor: ## Merge Reconciliation Addendum (canonical merge) / New / changed components bullet: "- `state_integrity.py` (Phase 2) — recompute resumed-state hash on `SessionStart`; block proceed on mismatch (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)." (design.md:1180); and the SQL hash-chain block at design.md:1168-1176 as the sibling pattern to mirror
confirmed_edits.change: Add a 'Resumed-State Hash' spec subsection (sibling to the gate_audit_log hash-chain spec at design.md:1168-1176) pinning: (a) exact input set — recommend the deterministic JSON of the durable run_state progress fields (current_item_id, iteration_count, no_progress_n, last_commit_sha) PLUS the feature_list.json content SHA PLUS last_commit_sha; (b) canonical serialization = deterministic/sorted-key JSON; (c) state_hash = sha256(canonical_form); (d) WHERE the baseline is recorded (a new run_state.state_hash TEXT column) and WHEN it is written (each PreCompact checkpoint and each per-Slice commit). Currently 'recomputes the resumed-state hash' is asserted in design.md:143/219/1088/1180 with zero algorithm, so the SessionStart recompute and the recorded baseline cannot be guaranteed to hash identical inputs.

confirmed_edits.title: Add run_state.state_hash baseline column + a producer that records it
confirmed_edits.anchor: -- run_state: per-session execution state for resumption (design.md:481-496); specifically the column list ending '... resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, ... last_commit_sha TEXT,' — no state_hash / recorded-hash column exists
confirmed_edits.change: Add a `state_hash TEXT` column to the run_state DDL (design.md:481-496) to hold the recorded baseline that state_integrity.py compares its recompute against — requirements.md:437 says it compares to 'the durable store's recorded hash' but no column holds it. ALSO name the PRODUCER and moment: the PreCompact hook-wiring row (design.md:220, currently 'Checkpoints claude-progress.txt, current evidence state, and feature_list.json to git') and the per-Slice commit must additionally WRITE run_state.state_hash. Without an assigned producer the 'durable store's recorded hash' is never created by any specified writer (the comparison input does not exist).

confirmed_edits.title: Wire state_integrity.py into the SessionStart hook implementation task
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 7.1 Implement `.claude/hooks/session_start_hook.py` (tasks.md:163-169), whose body lists only read git status / progress file / feature_list.json / inject summary, _Requirements: 11.3_; contrast task 49 (tasks.md:963) which never edits 7.1, and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) which explicitly wires audit_log.append into three hooks

Reference Requirement 11.3 / REQ-STATE-005 and keep it non-blocking (exit 0 always).

confirmed_edits.title: Scope design.md:143 row to state_integrity.py only (non-blocking; flag-based block)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component-inventory row design.md:143: "Recomputes the resumed-state hash on `SessionStart` and writes `run_state.resume_integrity_ok`; the PreToolUse integrity guard blocks on mismatch (REQ-STATE-005 / CHECK-11)."
confirmed_edits.change: The row conflates two components and implies state_integrity.py blocks 'on mismatch'. It does not: per design.md:212 the PreToolUse guard blocks when run_state.resume_integrity_ok == false (a flag), not on a live recompare. Reword to scope the row to state_integrity.py only ('computes the hash and writes the flag; non-blocking') and let the existing PreToolUse-integrity-guard row (design.md:212) own the block, stated as 'blocks when resume_integrity_ok == false' rather than 'on mismatch'.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the operator-reconciliation / unblock mechanism
confirmed_edits.anchor: ## Error Handling → ### Hook Failure Modes table (design.md:805-816), alongside the existing fail-closed rows ('plan-approved.json missing or corrupt | PreToolUse blocks (fail closed)')
confirmed_edits.change: requirements.md:438 says the guard releases 'only after operator reconciliation flips it true' but no file defines HOW. Add an Error-Handling entry defining a concrete reconciliation entry point, e.g. `python3 tools/state_integrity.py --reconcile --session <id>` that re-records run_state.state_hash from the current accepted state and sets resume_integrity_ok=true, and state what reconciliation entails (re-recording the durable baseline hash). The unblock path is currently a dangling obligation.

confirmed_edits.title: Define first-run / no-baseline behavior so the guard never blocks a fresh run
confirmed_edits.anchor: run_state DDL line design.md:492: "resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, -- ... PreToolUse integrity guard blocks the first write when FALSE (REQ-STATE-005)" (plain BOOLEAN, no SQL DEFAULT) and PreToolUse integrity-guard row design.md:212
confirmed_edits.change: resume_integrity_ok is a plain BOOLEAN with no default; on a fresh (non-resumed) session there is no recorded baseline, so the comparison is undefined and a NULL/FALSE flag would make the PreToolUse guard block the first legitimate write. State explicitly that on a session with no prior durable state_hash, state_integrity.py sets resume_integrity_ok=TRUE (and/or the PreToolUse guard at design.md:212 treats NULL/absent-baseline as ALLOW), so only an actual recorded-vs-recomputed mismatch blocks. Mirror this with a unit/property case in task 39.16 asserting a first-run session is never blocked by the integrity guard.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a 'first write of a resumed session' detection mechanism to run_state
confirmed_edits.anchor: PreToolUse integrity-guard row design.md:212 ('First `Write`/`Edit` of a resumed session ...') and Property 26 design.md:1088 (scoped to 'the first Write/Edit of a resumed session'); run_state DDL design.md:481-496 (no is_resume / first_write_done / session-origin column)
confirmed_edits.change: The guard is scoped to the FIRST implementation write of a RESUMED session, but the PreToolUse hook is registered with no matcher (design.md:168-173, fires on every tool) and run_state has no field letting the guard recognize that moment. Add durable state — e.g. an `is_resume BOOLEAN` (set by SessionStart when a prior run_state row exists) and a `first_write_done BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` (set true after the guard passes the first write) — and have task 49.1's guard logic key on (is_resume AND NOT first_write_done). Otherwise the guard cannot implement the 'first write of a resumed session' scope that Property 26 and Requirement 23.2 require.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve design-internal contradiction: spine row 4 vs hook-wiring row on SessionStart duties
confirmed_edits.anchor: Phase 0 Component List spine row 4 (design.md:626): "SessionStart hook | ... | Loads git status + progress file + coverage model | ..." — no integrity computation; contradicts hook-wiring row design.md:219 and Property 26 design.md:1088 which say SessionStart computes the resumed-state hash
confirmed_edits.change: design.md is internally inconsistent about the SessionStart hook's responsibilities: spine row 4 (design.md:626) lists only 'Loads git status + progress file + coverage model' (matching task 7.1) while design.md:219 and Property 26 say it also computes the resumed-state hash and writes resume_integrity_ok. Update spine row 4 to add 'computes the resumed-state hash via state_integrity.py and writes run_state.resume_integrity_ok (non-blocking)' so the spine row agrees with design.md:219.

confirmed_edits.title: Give resume_integrity_ok and the baseline hash a file-backed home for Postgres-down fallback
confirmed_edits.anchor: tasks.md:730 Postgres-unavailable fallback note ('PreToolUse status/integrity guards ... MUST fall back to the file-backed state (feature_list.json + claude-progress.txt) ... never fail-open') and task 31.1 save_run_state (tasks.md:486-488) which writes claude-progress.txt as a duplicate but does NOT enumerate resume_integrity_ok or state_hash
confirmed_edits.change: tasks.md:730 requires the PreToolUse integrity guard to reach the SAME allow/block decision on DB-down using file-backed state, but resume_integrity_ok lives only in run_state (Postgres) and task 31.1's save_run_state does not write it (nor the baseline state_hash) to claude-progress.txt. Amend task 31.1 so save_run_state persists resume_integrity_ok and state_hash into the claude-progress.txt file-backed duplicate, giving the integrity guard a file-backed source on DB-down (otherwise the fallback decision is undefined and risks fail-open).

confirmed_edits.title: Add a 'Resumed-State Integrity' design prose subsection covering failure modes
confirmed_edits.anchor: ## Error Handling (design.md:805-816) — there is no dedicated 'Resumed-State Integrity' narrative subsection (contrast the dedicated 'No-Progress Watchdog' (design.md:640) and 'plan-approved.json Gate' (design.md:758) subsections)
confirmed_edits.change: state_integrity.py — the sole guard against corrupted resume — has no narrative subsection, only three table rows + one Property + a one-line addendum bullet; failure modes (hash recompute fails, durable baseline absent on first run, Postgres down at SessionStart) are discussed nowhere. Add a 'Resumed-State Integrity' subsection covering: hash inputs (cross-ref the hash-algo spec), where/when the baseline state_hash is stored, first-run no-baseline behavior (default resume_integrity_ok TRUE / treat NULL as allow), and SessionStart-time DB-unavailable behavior (fall back to file-backed hash per tasks.md:730).

confirmed_edits.title: Define the central REQ-STATE-005 domain terms in the glossary
confirmed_edits.anchor: ## Glossary (requirements.md:17-48) — defines Coverage_Model, Postgres, SessionStart_Hook, PreToolUse_Hook, etc., but has no entry for 'resumed-state hash', 'durable store's recorded hash', or 'operator reconciliation'
confirmed_edits.change: The three concepts on which Requirement 23 correctness rests are undefined glossary terms, which is what makes the hash inputs and the reconciliation action ambiguous at the requirements layer. Add glossary entries for 'Resumed-State Hash' (the sha256 over the canonical durable+file state — cross-ref the design hash-algo spec), 'Recorded State Hash' (the baseline persisted in run_state.state_hash), and 'Operator Reconciliation' (the human-driven re-record-and-unblock action that flips resume_integrity_ok true).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix acting_agent → actor_agent naming-canon violation in Property 24 test task

confirmed_edits.title: Field-align the OTel hook-decision attribute set with the audit-log tuple (add actor_agent)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 540 (task 36.1): 'set attributes: hook.event, tool.name, decision (allow/block), reason, requirement.id'

Add 'actor.agent' (or 'actor_agent') to the task 36.1 OTel attribute list so the live OTel span and the durable audit row carry the same per-agent attribution that closes pain-point category 4. Cross-reference REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236).

confirmed_edits.title: Specify chain-linearization / concurrency control for audit_log.append
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 968 (task 52.1): 'Audit-log PRODUCER tools/audit_log.py — expose append(...)' — and the gate_audit_log DDL at design.md:1156-1167
confirmed_edits.change: The hash chain is strictly sequential (entry_hash = sha256(canonical_row || prev_hash) and INCLUDES the BIGSERIAL seq, per design.md:1166/1172), so two concurrent append() calls from different hook processes that read the same prev_hash but receive distinct seq values fork the chain at identical prev_hash — exactly the prev_hash != recomputed(prior) condition audit_verify treats as tampering (Property 28), a guaranteed false positive that bricks the merge gate. Add to task 52.1: append() MUST serialize prev_hash-read + insert under a lock (e.g. pg_advisory_xact_lock keyed on the log, or SELECT … FOR UPDATE on a chain-head sentinel) within one transaction so the chain linearizes. Mirror this as an Error-Handling note.

confirmed_edits.title: Add audit_log.py pseudocode and a dedicated write-side unit-test subtask
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 968 (task 52.1): 'Audit-log PRODUCER tools/audit_log.py' — has no unit-test subtask; contrast task 39.18 which tests audit_verify.py, not the producer's hashing
confirmed_edits.change: audit_log.py (the security-critical write side) has no pseudocode block (unlike evaluate_stop in design.md:685-737 and check_plan_approval in design.md:777-799) and no unit-test subtask of its own — only the indirect Property 28 PBT (task 39.18) which exercises audit_verify.py. Add a unit-test subtask under task 52.1 (mirroring the Phase-0 'Test to pass' pattern at design.md:621-637) asserting: (a) genesis prev_hash == sha256(""); (b) entry_hash determinism for a fixed input tuple; (c) a round-trip append→audit_verify passes on an intact chain. Optionally add an audit_log.append pseudocode block to design.md (read-prev-hash → canonicalize JSON → sha256 → insert).

confirmed_edits.title: Specify a retention/archival policy (or accept unbounded) for REQ-AUDIT-003
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 459: Requirement 27.3 'THE System SHALL retain the hash-chained gate-decision log as the execution-proof complement to SLSA build provenance.'
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-AUDIT-003 says 'retain' and append-only but bounds neither growth nor retention duration; the gate_audit_log DDL (design.md:1156-1167) has no retention column or policy. An append-only hash chain cannot prune the middle without breaking verification. Add a retention clause: either (a) a chain-compatible checkpoint-and-archive of a verified prefix that records the archived prefix's tip hash as the new genesis-equivalent prev_hash, or (b) an explicit statement that unbounded retention is accepted for v1.

confirmed_edits.title: State audit-completeness scope start point (pre-52.3 decisions unlogged) for REQ-AUDIT-001
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 970 (task 52.3): 'Wire audit-log producer into the three hooks'
confirmed_edits.change: The producer is wired into its caller hooks only at task 52.3, so during Phase 0/1 (and most of 2/3) the Stop/PreToolUse/SubagentStop hooks make gate decisions WITHOUT producing audit entries — the 'append every gate decision' invariant (REQ-AUDIT-001, requirements.md:457) does not hold until 52.3 lands. Either (a) add an explicit note at task 52.3 that REQ-AUDIT-001 completeness begins at 52.3 and pre-52.3 decisions are out of audit scope, or (b) move the append wiring earlier (it only needs the file-backed fallback, which has no Phase-2 DB dependency) so the chain is complete from Phase 0.

refuted.item: open_gaps[0] alternative (b): add a Z3 CHECK-14a/14b family modeling 'tampered link ⇒ verify fails' / 'intact chain ⇒ verify passes' and bump the self-count
refuted.why: Refuted as the chosen remedy: design.md:1110 and the harness banner (formal_verification_merged.py:17-24) deliberately state Property 28 is runtime/CI-verified via audit_verify.py with NO Z3 check, and the harness self-counts to exactly 34 (14+12+8). Adding a Z3 family contradicts the canonical 'no Z3 check' design decision and would require re-baselining the 34 total across requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md. The defect is purely the dangling citation; the confirmed edit fixes that (option a). Emitting the Z3-addition as a separate edit would be a competing, non-canonical change.

refuted.item: critic_findings — 'OTel-6-hooks vs audit-3-hooks divergence proves REQ-AUDIT-001 self-contradiction / PostToolUse-SessionStart-PreCompact decisions are mistreated'
refuted.why: Partially refuted as a NEW defect: PostToolUse never gates (exit 1, design.md:216), and SessionStart/PreCompact are non-blocking informational hooks (design.md:219-220) — they make no allow/block gate decision, so excluding them from the audit producer is correct, not a contradiction. REQ-AUDIT-001 says append on every allow/block *gate* decision; OTel forwards a decision tuple for all six hooks (including non-gating ones) for observability. The legitimate residual is (1) the un-audited PreToolUse blocking sub-gates — captured by the pretooluse-subgate edit — and (2) documenting PostToolUse's exemption — same edit. No separate edit needed.

refuted.item: citation_issue — design.md:815 cited as the audit-log Postgres-unavailable fallback
refuted.why: Refuted as an artifact defect: design.md:815 ('Postgres unavailable | Fall back to feature_list.json … Log warning to OTel') is correct for the general gate fallback; the audit-log-specific file-backed fallback is correctly located at tasks.md:730 (N-25). This is a finding-internal citation imprecision, not a gap in the canonical artifacts. The substantive need (an audit_log.append failure-mode row) is captured by the Error-Handling edit, which already cites tasks.md:730.

confirmed_edits.title: Add merged-chain (DB + file-backed log) verification spec for audit_verify.py
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 52.2 Audit-chain verifier `tools/audit_verify.py` — recompute the chain and fail on any broken link.
confirmed_edits.change: Add a bullet to task 52.2 specifying that when Postgres is unavailable the verifier reads BOTH the `gate_audit_log` table AND any file-backed log segments written by the producer (per Notes line 730), orders the merged entries by `seq`, and verifies the single combined chain; define the seam rule (the file segment's first `prev_hash` MUST chain to the DB segment's last `entry_hash`). Currently tasks.md:730 specifies only the PRODUCER's DB-down file fallback and reconcile-on-reconnect; nothing tells the VERIFIER how to reconstruct a chain split across Postgres + a file-backed log, so a split chain could falsely fail at the seam or be silently skipped.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify structured failure report for audit_verify.py
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 52.2 Audit-chain verifier `tools/audit_verify.py` — recompute the chain and fail on any broken link.
confirmed_edits.change: Add to task 52.2 that on a broken link audit_verify.py emits a machine-readable report to stdout (JSON: `first_broken_seq`, `expected_hash`, `stored_prev_hash`, `computed_prev_hash`) in addition to the non-zero exit, so the audit-chain-verify CI check (task 40.3) can annotate the specific failing entry. Currently every spec mention defines only an opaque non-zero exit, identifying neither which `seq` broke nor the expected-vs-actual hashes.

confirmed_edits.title: Add audit_verify.py design subsection: signature, pseudocode, edge cases, error-handling row
confirmed_edits.anchor: | `audit_verify.py` | `tools/audit_verify.py` | 3 | Recomputes the `gate_audit_log` hash chain; on-demand verifier plus the merge-time CI required check (REQ-AUDIT-002/003). |
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design.md subsection for audit_verify.py (it currently has only the one-line inventory row, the New-components bullet, and the Property 28 statement — no algorithm, no interface, no error-handling row, unlike stop_hook.py/pre_tool_use_hook.py which have full pseudocode). Specify: (1) function signature e.g. `verify_chain(rows) -> Result`; (2) recomputation pseudocode mirroring audit_log.py's canonical form `entry_hash = sha256(canonical_row || prev_hash)` over `(seq, event_name, tool_name, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent, created_at)`, genesis `prev_hash = sha256("")`; (3) explicit EMPTY-chain and GENESIS-ONLY handling (both PASS); (4) a new row in the Hook Failure Modes / Error Handling table (design.md:807-816) for audit_verify.py: 'gate_audit_log unreadable / Postgres down with no file segment -> FAIL verification, non-zero exit (fail closed)' consistent with the never-fail-open rule at tasks.md:730.
