confirmed_edits.title: Add a subtype discriminator field to CoverageItem so REQ-VERIFY-007/008 triggers are evaluable
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-VERIFY-007 (requirements.md:447-448) gates 'WHEN an NFR item of subtype performance/accessibility', but the CoverageItem schema (design.md:322-385) has no subtype field — `type` is only {functional,NFR,WIRING} and `ears_pattern` is the five EARS patterns, so there is no representable discriminator on which the Verifier can dispatch perf vs a11y vs ui-screen-render. Add an optional `subtype` enum field to CoverageItem, e.g. enum ["performance","accessibility","ui-screen"] (valid only when type==NFR/WIRING), AND mirror it as a `subtype TEXT` column on the coverage_items Postgres table (design.md:443-451). Note the ui-screen value covers the REQ-VERIFY-008 render branch (per critic findings: subtype alone solves perf-vs-a11y but the render branch also needs a discriminator). The Verifier dispatches the fifth layer on this field. Without it the WHEN/Ubiquitous conditions are unrealizable.

Pick one phase: change the inventory Phase cell from '1/3' to '1' (and the addendum line 1182 'Phase 1/3' to 'Phase 1'), and add task 51 to the Phase-1 section of tasks.md with an explicit dependency on task 10.3 (the verifier.md author).

confirmed_edits.title: Define how k6/Lighthouse/axe-core outputs map into the four-field Evidence_Record
confirmed_edits.anchor: **Evidence_Record** has exactly four required fields with no additional properties allowed:
confirmed_edits.change: k6/Lighthouse/axe-core emit reports, not pytest results, yet Evidence_Record requires test_file/test_name/output_hash/collected_at with additionalProperties:false (design.md:387-411). How a Lighthouse JSON or axe violation list maps into these four fields is never defined, so the SubagentStop schema gate may reject valid perf/a11y evidence. Add a canonical mapping to the Verifier/perf_a11y_verifier section: test_file = path to the k6/Lighthouse/axe runner script; test_name = the budget id (or screen/state id for ui-screen render); output_hash = sha256:<hex> of the report artifact; collected_at = run timestamp (ISO-8601). This keeps perf/a11y evidence conformant to the four-field schema and the additionalProperties:false constraint.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin k6/Lighthouse/axe-core tool dependencies and add a CI step that runs perf/a11y
confirmed_edits.anchor: Create `pyproject.toml` (or `requirements.txt`) pinning `z3-solver`, `hypothesis`, `jsonschema`, `psycopg2-binary`, `semgrep`, `playwright`
confirmed_edits.change: Scaffold task 1 (tasks.md:15) pins z3-solver/hypothesis/jsonschema/psycopg2-binary/semgrep/playwright but NOT k6, Lighthouse, or axe-core; and no workflow runs perf/a11y as a status check, so the capability has no execution path in CI. Add k6 / Lighthouse / @axe-core/playwright (or playwright-axe) to the task-1 dependency pins, and add a CI step (extend coverage-gate.yml or add a perf-a11y job) that runs the perf/a11y/render checks for NFR-subtype items — mirroring the Playwright step in task 15.1.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a declared-screen/state data structure so REQ-VERIFY-008 has an enumerable source
confirmed_edits.anchor:
"definitions": {
    "CoverageItem": {
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-VERIFY-008 (requirements.md:449) is Ubiquitous: 'every declared screen/state (incl. empty, loading, error) SHALL have a behavioral test asserting it renders' — but no schema field enumerates screens/states; there is no screens/states/ui-declaration structure in the feature_list.json schema, so 'every declared screen/state' is not enumerable and the obligation cannot be checked against a defined declaration set. Add a screen/state declaration structure to the coverage model — e.g. a WIRING/NFR item with subtype 'ui-screen' carrying a `declared_states` array (enumerating at least empty/loading/error/ready) — so the render assertion is enforceable. Note (per critic findings): the render assertion is a BEHAVIORAL test and its actual mechanism is the existing Playwright behavioral layer (design.md:260 / task 15) plus the WIRING integration-test-evidence obligation (Req 8.3 / task 47), NOT a perf/a11y tool — the design subsection should route ui-screen render evidence through Playwright/WIRING, not k6/Lighthouse/axe.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a dedicated perf_a11y_verifier design subsection and expand task 51 into subtasks

This is the umbrella edit; the sub-points are covered by the subtype/evidence-mapping/CI/property/ui-screen edits and should be folded into this one subsection.

NFR being first-class is satisfied. The actual gap is the missing within-NFR discriminator (subtype), already captured by the coverageitem-subtype-field edit — not a deficiency of Req 5.4 itself.

refuted.item: ears: Property 23 (Line Coverage Threshold) — 'gap'
refuted.why: Property 23 itself (design.md:1058-1062, Validates Req 9.6) is correct and not in scope for perf/a11y; the only defect is task 51 borrowing it. That is already captured by the confirmed task51-property23-miscitation edit. Property 23 needs no change.

The substantive claim (no VERIFY-007/008 references in the harness) is verified true via grep, and is already actioned by the confirmed property-and-z3-check edit. No edit to a canonical artifact follows from the phantom line number; the harness file needs no change for this item.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix REQ-ID mis-citation on secrets-scan.yml inventory row (17.5 -> 17.2)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 147: | `secrets-scan.yml` | `.github/workflows/secrets-scan.yml` | 1 | CI secrets-scanning workflow using **gitleaks** [K17]; fails the build on a detected secret (REQ-17.5). |
confirmed_edits.change: Change the trailing criterion reference from `(REQ-17.5)` to `(REQ-17.2 / REQ-SEC-002)`. REQ-17.5 (requirements.md:314) is the PreToolUse runtime prohibition on secrets in prompts/spans/URLs (enforced by PreToolUse_Hook, validated by Property 20); the CI diff-scan obligation is REQ-17.2 (requirements.md:311: 'IF a secret ... is detected in a diff, THEN ... block the commit or merge'), which is what task 40.2 (tasks.md:698) and task 45 (tasks.md:957) already cite. Append a parenthetical clarifying that REQ-17.5 is the distinct PreToolUse commit-side/pre-emission prevention gate covered by Property 20, so the merge-side (17.2/secrets-scan) and commit-side (17.5/PreToolUse) halves of 'block the commit or merge' are explicitly split.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve or strip undefined [K17]/[K9]/[K12] citation markers
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 147 [K17] ('gitleaks [K17]'); line 213 [K9] ('domain_baseline_checklists [K9]'); line 265 [K12] ('coverage.py) [K12]')
confirmed_edits.change: The bracketed K-tags [K17], [K9], [K12] have no resolver: there is no K-citation legend/table anywhere in the spec set or docs (verified: only these 3 occurrences exist, all in design.md). Either add a 'K-citations' reference table to design.md defining K9/K12/K17 (and any others) with their Kiro/source provenance, or strip the three [K9]/[K12]/[K17] markers as noise. Apply one resolution consistently to all three.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve phase inconsistency: Phase-1 inventory row vs Phase-4/un-phased build tasks
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 40.2 at line 695 (under task 40 'Phase 4 hardening'); task 45 at line 957 (un-phased 'Added tasks' block); design.md:147 labels the component Phase 1
confirmed_edits.change: secrets-scan.yml is labelled Phase 1 in the design inventory (design.md:147) but its only build tasks are task 40.2 (Phase 4 hardening) and task 45 (un-phased). Pick one phase and align all three. Recommended: move the workflow-creation work into Phase 1, co-located with the other CI verification-depth gates (near task 23 codeql.yml / task 24 Semgrep, tasks.md:376-388), and tag task 45 with the same phase. Alternatively relabel design.md:147's Phase column to 4. The chosen phase must be identical in design.md:147, task 40.2, and task 45.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a dedicated design subsection for secrets-scan.yml (currently one inventory sentence only)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component inventory row at line 147 (secrets-scan.yml has exactly one sentence and no dedicated subsection, unlike coverage_query.rego, the hooks, and the audit log)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a short design subsection for secrets-scan.yml specifying: (a) `on: pull_request` trigger (and whether push to protected branches is also scanned); (b) the gitleaks action/version and config/allowlist path; (c) baseline/allowlist strategy for false positives; (d) the report/SARIF artifact contract; (e) scope = the PR diff vs whole git history; and (f) explicit clarification that REQ-17.2's 'block the commit' clause is satisfied at merge in CI (a CI workflow cannot block a local commit), with the commit-side/pre-emission half delegated to the PreToolUse path (REQ-17.5). This also subsumes the diagram-absence observation: the CI architecture subgraph (design.md:50-55) draws only OPA/SAST/SLSA and equally omits zap-baseline, deepeval-gate, and audit-chain-verify, so the subgraph is illustrative and the inventory+subsection is the canonical home for this gate.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a correctness property (or explicit no-Z3 note) for the REQ-17.2 CI secrets diff-scan
confirmed_edits.anchor: Properties section near Property 20 (design.md:1034-1038, which validates 17.5 not 17.2); 'no Z3 check' note pattern at design.md:1110 (Property 28) and 1120 (Property 29)
confirmed_edits.change: secrets-scan.yml (REQ-17.2) has neither a correctness Property nor a Z3 CHECK nor a documented 'no formal check' rationale (verified: formal_verification_merged.py has no 17.2/secrets check; Property 20 covers only 17.5). Add a property: 'For any PR diff containing a known secret pattern, the secrets-scan check fails and merge is blocked; for a clean diff it passes' validated against Requirements 17.2, OR add an explicit '(no Z3 check — runtime/CI verified by fixture test)' note mirroring Properties 28/29. Cite the chosen mechanism from the design inventory row (design.md:147).

confirmed_edits.title: Make scanner choice consistent between design.md and tasks.md
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:147 ('using **gitleaks**') vs tasks.md:696 / tasks.md:957 ('gitleaks (or trufflehog)')
confirmed_edits.change: design.md fixes the scanner as gitleaks while tasks.md offers 'gitleaks (or trufflehog)' with no selection criterion. Pick one default (gitleaks, per design.md) and demote trufflehog to an explicit 'acceptable alternative' note in a single place, or state the selection criterion. Make design.md:147 and tasks.md (696, 957) say the same thing about which scanner is the default.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a glossary term for the secrets-scanner tool family
confirmed_edits.anchor: Glossary, after line 47 ('Semgrep_CodeQL: The SAST tools used in CI ...')
confirmed_edits.change: The secrets-scanner is the only security-gate tool family with no glossary entry (Semgrep_CodeQL, Playwright, OPA_Conftest, SLSA all defined). Add: 'Secrets_Scanner: the CI tool (gitleaks by default; trufflehog as an acceptable alternative) used to detect committed secrets/credentials/tokens in a PR diff and fail the build (REQ-17.2).'

confirmed_edits.title: Define an acceptance/fixture test for secrets-scan.yml ('done' is currently undefined)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 45 at line 957 and task 40.2 at lines 695-698 (neither defines any fixture/acceptance test); contrast Phase-0 spine 'Test to pass' column at design.md:621-636
confirmed_edits.change: No acceptance/integration test is specified for this component anywhere (the Phase-0 spine table has a 'Test to pass' column but no secrets-scan row since it is Phase 1, and tasks 40.2/45 define no fixture). Add an explicit pass/fail test to task 45 (or task 40.2), e.g. 'a fixture PR diff containing a fake AWS key -> workflow fails (merge blocked); a clean diff -> workflow passes', placed in the existing integration suite (e.g. tests/integration/test_github_actions.py). This both gives the component a 'done' definition and supplies the fixture referenced by the new correctness property/no-Z3 note.

confirmed_edits.title: Add explicit on: trigger block to secrets-scan task (sole sibling workflow lacking one)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 40.2 at line 696 ('Run `gitleaks` (or `trufflehog`) on the PR diff ...') — specifies only 'on the PR diff', no on: block
confirmed_edits.change: Every comparable required-check workflow in tasks.md states its trigger explicitly (codeql.yml 'Trigger on push and pull_request' tasks.md:379; coverage-gate 'Trigger on pull_request' tasks.md:263; formal-verification 'Trigger on push and pull_request' tasks.md:251; property-tests 'Trigger on every pull_request' tasks.md:707); secrets-scan.yml is the sole exception. Add an explicit trigger line to task 40.2, e.g. 'Trigger on pull_request (scan the PR diff)', matching the sibling-workflow convention.

confirmed_edits.title: Designate a single canonical required-checks enumeration and a design spec for docs/github-ruleset.md
confirmed_edits.anchor: Required-checks list triplicated at tasks.md:270 (14.2), 697 (40.2), 957 (45); docs/github-ruleset.md referenced only in tasks.md, never specified in design.md
confirmed_edits.change: The required-merge-gate list (formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify, deepeval-gate, zap-baseline, + human reviewer) is enumerated in three task locations with no single canonical source, and docs/github-ruleset.md has no design.md specification of its contents/schema. Designate task 14.2 (tasks.md:270) as the single canonical enumeration of required status checks and have tasks 40.2/45/40.3 reference it instead of re-listing, and add a brief design.md description of docs/github-ruleset.md (what status checks it must enumerate and the reviewer requirement). This is a shared CI-ruleset artifact issue, not secrets-scan-specific.

refuted.item: Critic: secrets-scan.yml is ABSENT from the system architecture data-flow diagram (design.md:50-55 CI subgraph draws OPA/SAST/SLSA but not secrets-scan)
refuted.why: DUPLICATE/low-value as a standalone edit. Verified the CI subgraph (design.md:50-55) draws only OPA, SAST, SLSA, GH_RULESET and equally omits zap-baseline, deepeval-gate, and audit-chain-verify — four required CI gates are absent, so the subgraph is a deliberately abstracted/illustrative view, not the canonical enumeration of gates (that role belongs to the inventory table + docs/github-ruleset.md). Singling out secrets-scan for the diagram while leaving the other three out would create a new inconsistency. The real remedy (a dedicated design subsection for the component) is captured under dedup_key unique:secrets-scan-design-subsection; no separate diagram edit is warranted.

refuted.item: Critic: the ears_stories survey of Requirement 17 is INCOMPLETE — REQ-17.3 (SLSA) and REQ-17.4 (sandbox) siblings not enumerated
refuted.why: REFUTED as an artifact gap. This is a critique of the draft findings' survey completeness, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. Verified REQ-17.3 is correctly built by task 32 (tasks.md:497, _Requirements: 17.3_) and REQ-17.4 by task 46 (tasks.md:958, _Requirements: 17.4_). Both siblings are fully tasked and mapped; there is nothing to add or fix in requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md. No edit.

refuted.item: Critic: the 'block the commit' half of REQ-17.2 is addressed more completely by REQ-17.5's live PreToolUse prevention gate than the draft acknowledges
refuted.why: REFUTED as a separate edit. This is correct context but not an independent artifact gap — it is a refinement of the mis-citation fix. The point (that 17.5 is the commit-side prevention and 17.2 is the merge-side CI scan) is already folded into the parenthetical added by dedup_key unique:secrets-scan-reqid-17.2. No additional edit.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: REQ-17.5 mis-cited against secrets-scan.yml; Property 20 governs 17.5 not the CI scan; Requirement 17 baseline mapping (B16) does not enumerate per-criterion mechanisms
refuted.why: DUPLICATE of already-confirmed edits, not separate add-wiring. The 17.5 mis-citation is fixed under unique:secrets-scan-reqid-17.2; the missing property for the CI scan is added under unique:secrets-scan-property; the glossary gap under unique:secrets-scanner-glossary. The B16 matrix row (requirements.md:398) correctly marks Requirement 17 MAPPED at the baseline-block granularity — the matrix intentionally maps blocks, not per-criterion mechanisms, so it is not a defect that it doesn't catch the 17.2-vs-17.5 split; the inventory-row citation is the correct place to fix that. No new edit beyond the three already emitted.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a subagent-output JSON Schema artifact making omission_declaration required & non-nullable (REQ-SPEC-018.3 has no target)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory table — row `| `schema/` | `schema/feature_list.schema.json` | 0 | JSON Schema for feature_list.json. PreToolUse validates against this. |` (design.md:138); and Subagent Definitions section (design.md:226-281) which declare behaviors but no output schema
confirmed_edits.change: Add a new component-inventory row `| schema/subagent_output.schema.json | schema/subagent_output.schema.json | 0 | JSON Schema for initializer/research/verifier subagent outputs; omission_declaration is a required, non-nullable property. SubagentStop omission-guard validates against this. |`. Add a Data Models subsection 'Subagent Output Schema' defining the object with omission_declaration required (type object/non-null, six keys Primary/Alternate/Exception/Recovery/Non-Functional/Edge-Case, each an array of [Gap]-marked strings). Reference this schema from the initializer.md/research.md/verifier.md subagent definitions (add an 'Output contract' line to each). This gives REQ-SPEC-018.3 ('JSON schema for each subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration as required, non-nullable') a concrete target artifact, mirroring how feature_list.schema.json is the target for EvidenceRecord.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve REQ-SPEC-018.1 structured-content enforcement gap (categorical [Gap] enumeration is unenforced; mechanism is null/non-null boolean only)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 29 Structured Omission Declaration, criterion 1 (requirements.md:476): 'WHEN a subagent ... SHALL include a non-null omission_declaration field listing, by EARS scenario category ... using a [Gap] marker for each omitted class.' vs criterion 2 (requirements.md:477) + CHECK-13a/13b which only model a single boolean omissionDeclNull
confirmed_edits.change: State explicitly which of two paths governs the structured-content half. EITHER (a) add a sentence to 29.1/29.2 clarifying the SubagentStop guard enforces presence/non-null ONLY, and the per-category [Gap] enumeration is a non-gating authoring convention (downgrading the machine-checked claim); OR (b) extend 29.3 / the subagent_output schema to require the six category keys (each an array) plus a [Gap]-marker format constraint, and add a property-test obligation for it. Pick one and record it, so that omission_declaration: "" or {} omitting categories cannot silently satisfy a criterion the harness never checks.

confirmed_edits.title: Non-emptiness inconsistency: omission gate accepts 'absent or null' only, Evidence sibling gate rejects 'absent or empty'; REQ-SPEC-018.3 silent on empty
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 29 criterion 2 (requirements.md:477): 'THE SubagentStop hook SHALL reject any subagent result whose omission_declaration field is absent or null, with exit 2.' — contrast with Requirement 5.6 (requirements.md:127): 'rejected if any of the four fields is absent or empty', and criterion 29.3 (requirements.md:478) which is silent on empty
confirmed_edits.change: Decide and state uniformly whether an empty-but-non-null omission_declaration ('', {}, []) blocks. If yes: change 29.2 wording to 'absent, null, or empty', mirror in design.md:148 and design.md:218 and Property 30 (design.md:1128), make 29.3 require non-empty (analogous to the EvidenceRecord additionalProperties:false + non-empty CHECK at design.md:474-477), and add an omissionDeclEmpty predicate to the harness so CHECK-13 covers the empty case. If no: add a one-line note to 29.2 that empty IS accepted by design, to make the divergence from the Evidence gate explicit and intentional.

confirmed_edits.title: Audit-call inconsistency: omission-guard wiring row calls audit_log.append only 'on the block decision', not on allow (violates REQ-AUDIT-001 / task 52.3 'every allow/block')
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table row `| SubagentStop — omission-declaration guard | ... | Checks omission_declaration field in the subagent output; calls audit_log.append(...) on the block decision ... |` (design.md:218)
confirmed_edits.change: Reword 'calls audit_log.append(...) on the block decision' to 'calls audit_log.append(...) on the allow/block decision' to match REQ-AUDIT-001 (requirements.md:457, 'every gate decision'), task 52.3 ('on every allow/block decision', tasks.md:970), and the sibling SubagentStop Evidence row (design.md:217, which already reads 'on the decision'). As written the allow path of the omission guard skips the audit append.

confirmed_edits.title: Two gates in one host hook with no specified ordering, combined exit-code semantics, or joint failure reporting
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table rows design.md:217 (SubagentStop Evidence_Record gate) and design.md:218 (SubagentStop omission-declaration guard); and Phase 0 Component List row #5 (design.md:627): 'SubagentStop hook | Validates Evidence_Record has all four fields | Missing output_hash → blocks. All four fields present → proceeds.'
confirmed_edits.change: Add prose (in a new 'SubagentStop omission-declaration gate' design subsection, or adjacent to rows 217-218) specifying: (1) evaluation order of the two co-resident gates inside subagent_stop_hook.py (Evidence_Record four-field validation and the omission guard); (2) that exit 2 is returned if EITHER gate fails, with both failure reasons surfaced to stderr; (3) how a single exit code is composed when both fail. Add an omission-path row/clause to the Phase 0 build-order acceptance test at design.md:627 (e.g. 'null/absent omission_declaration → blocks; present → proceeds').

confirmed_edits.title: No Property-30 test subtask; task-39 header says '29 properties' while line 717/979 say 30 (count mismatch)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 39 header (tasks.md:569): '- [ ] 39. Complete PBT suite — all 29 correctness properties ...' vs task 42.1 (tasks.md:717) 'Confirm all 30 properties pass' vs tasks.md:979 'extend the PBT suite to 30' listing Property 30; and task 54 (tasks.md:974) 'Property 30 test in subtask' with NO 54.x subtask and NO 39.20
confirmed_edits.change: Reconcile the task-39 header from 'all 29 correctness properties' to 'all 30 correctness properties' (Properties 1–24 + 25–30) to match tasks.md:717, tasks.md:979-980, and requirements.md:514. Add a concrete test subtask for Property 30 — e.g. 39.20 `tests/property/test_omission.py` (Property 30, CHECK-13a/13b, asserts null/absent omission_declaration → exit 2; present → accepted) OR a 54.x subtask — since task 54 references a Property-30 test 'in subtask' that does not exist (the PBT block 39.15–39.19 covers Properties 25–29 only). Also fix the same '29' wording in the tasks.md:732 Notes line.

confirmed_edits.title: Phase-0 e2e spine tests (16.1, 17.1) invoke subagent_stop_hook.py but assert only the Evidence_Record/output_hash path, never the omission path
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 16.1 (tasks.md:287): 'Call subagent_stop_hook.py with complete Evidence_Record → assert exit 0; with missing output_hash → assert exit 2'; Task 17.1 (tasks.md:299-300): same Evidence-only assertions
confirmed_edits.change: Add omission-declaration assertions to both Phase-0 e2e tests that actually run the host hook: in 16.1 and 17.1, call subagent_stop_hook.py with a subagent result whose omission_declaration is null/absent → assert exit 2; and with a present non-null omission_declaration (+ complete Evidence_Record) → assert exit 0. This makes the omission guard part of the Phase-0 spine acceptance, consistent with omission_guard being a Phase-0 capability (design.md:148).

confirmed_edits.title: Glossary lacks an omission_declaration term and the SubagentStop_Hook term omits the omission-declaration responsibility
confirmed_edits.anchor: Glossary entry (requirements.md:24): '**SubagentStop_Hook**: The Claude Code SubagentStop hook that validates evidence schema before accepting a subagent's result.'; and Evidence_Record term (requirements.md:29) — there is no omission_declaration term
confirmed_edits.change: Extend the SubagentStop_Hook glossary term to mention BOTH responsibilities: '...validates evidence schema AND enforces the omission-declaration gate (rejects any subagent result whose omission_declaration is absent/null) before accepting a subagent's result.' Add a new glossary term '**omission_declaration**: A structured field on initializer/research/verifier subagent outputs, keyed by the six EARS scenario categories (Primary/Alternate/Exception/Recovery/Non-Functional/Edge-Case), each a list of [Gap]-marked uncovered scenario classes; non-null and required (REQ-SPEC-018).' — mirroring how Evidence_Record is fully glossed.

confirmed_edits.title: No dedicated design subsection or task-54 subtasks for the omission guard (thin coverage vs the Evidence_Record sibling); audit field-content for the omission decision undefined
confirmed_edits.anchor: Only artifacts for this capability: component row design.md:148, wiring row design.md:218, Property 30 prose design.md:1124-1130, one build-order line design.md:627. Contrast: Evidence gate has task 8 (8.1/8.2), a build-order test, and round-trip tests
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design subsection 'SubagentStop omission-declaration gate' specifying: input payload shape (the subagent result JSON), the null/absent(/empty) predicate, the exact stderr message naming `omission_declaration`, allow/block audit behavior, and which audit fields the omission block records — i.e. the requirement_id value (REQ-SPEC-018, since the gate is a structural presence check not tied to a single REQ) and the actor_agent value (the subagent whose result is rejected), closing the REQ-AUDIT-001 seven-field ambiguity for this decision. In tasks.md, add task-54 subtasks mirroring 8.1/8.2 (54.1 implement the omission guard in subagent_stop_hook.py; 54.2 unit tests: null→exit 2 naming omission_declaration, present→exit 0).

confirmed_edits.title: Host SubagentStop hook fires on EVERY subagent result, but omission scope is three subagents (initializer/research/verifier) — no subagent-type discriminator specified
confirmed_edits.anchor: Hook Wiring Table: omission-guard TRIGGER scoped to '(initializer, research, verifier)' (design.md:218) vs the unconditional SubagentStop command registration (design.md:181-186) and the unmatched Evidence SubagentStop row (design.md:217); implementer results are out of scope but nothing says how the hook distinguishes them
confirmed_edits.change: Specify the discriminator the omission guard uses to scope itself to initializer/research/verifier results and EXCLUDE implementer results: e.g. read a subagent-type/agent-name field from the SubagentStop payload (or the subagent_output schema's role field) and skip the omission check when the producing agent is implementer.md. State this in the omission-guard wiring row (design.md:218) and in the new omission-guard design subsection, and add it to the subagent_output schema (a required `agent_role` enum). Without it, an implementer result with no omission_declaration would be wrongly blocked or the scope is unenforceable.

confirmed_edits.title: Phase-label inconsistency: omission_guard is Phase 0 (design.md:148) but its host file is Phase 0/1 (design.md:130) and its only build task (54) sits in the undated P3 'Added tasks' block with no phase
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 54 (tasks.md:974) in the '### Added tasks — P3 market-research capabilities' block with no phase label; vs design.md:148 (omission_guard Phase 0) and design.md:130 (subagent_stop_hook.py Phase '0/1')
confirmed_edits.change: Pin the omission guard's phase explicitly as Phase 0 (per design.md:148) and either (a) fold task 54's hook-extension into the Phase-0 build order (alongside task 8), or (b) annotate task 54 that the omission guard is a Phase-0 capability delivered by this task and must be runnable end-to-end before Phase 1. Align design.md:130's '0/1' label for subagent_stop_hook.py with the Phase-0 placement of both gates it hosts.

refuted.why: Verified true on disk (.claude/hooks/ contains only .gitkeep, .claude/agents/ only .gitkeep, no schema/ dir). No requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md edit is warranted to 'fix' the absence of code — the spec already specifies it. Out of scope for an artifact-reconciliation edit set.

refuted.item: Open gap: settings.json does not exist on disk, so the SubagentStop hook never fires
refuted.why: Verified: find returns no settings*.json anywhere. But the binding is already fully specified in the artifacts — design.md:181-186 gives the exact SubagentStop command-hook JSON and task 9.1/9.2 task its authoring plus the command-type smoke test. The missing file is implementation status, not an artifact gap. No spec edit needed.

refuted.item: Critic (missing): OTel gate-decision emission obligation (REQ-OBS-003 / task 36.1) is absent for the omission_guard allow/block decision
refuted.why: Task 36.1 (tasks.md:539-542) explicitly extends ALL SIX hooks — including SubagentStop — to emit a hook.decision OTel span with hook.event/tool.name/decision/reason/requirement.id, enforcing REQ-OBS-003 (req 12.3). The obligation is generic and already wired for the host hook; the omission guard inherits it. The Evidence SubagentStop row (design.md:217) likewise does not re-state OTel in the wiring table because task 36.1 covers it cross-cuttingly. No omission-specific artifact gap.

refuted.item: Critic (missing): REQ-AUDIT-003 (audit-log retention as execution-proof) not addressed for this component
refuted.why: REQ-AUDIT-003 (req 27.3, requirements.md:459) is a system-wide retention property and is already carried as an explicit obligation on the wiring task: task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) is tagged '_Requirements: REQ-AUDIT-001, REQ-AUDIT-003'. The retention guarantee applies to the whole hash-chained gate_audit_log that the omission guard appends to; it is fully present in the artifacts and not a per-omission-component gap.

refuted.item: Critic (contradiction): CHECK-13b is overstated — it proves non-vacuity, not that the guard ALLOWS a present-field result
refuted.why: This is a critique of the draft's reasoning, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The harness (formal_verification_merged.py:496-500) and design.md:1130 are accurate: CHECK-13b asserts Not(omissionDeclNull) ∧ subagentAccepted13 is SAT (rule non-vacuous), which is exactly what design.md:1130 claims ('result MAY be accepted'). No artifact wording overstates this. Nothing to fix.

refuted.item: Critic (overstatement): the draft labels CHECK-13a/13b the component's 'FORMAL OWNER' though the harness is a generic two-boolean implication with no hook/exit/scope reference
refuted.why: Critique of the draft findings' framing, not of the canonical artifacts. The harness models the abstract gate shape (Implies(omissionDeclNull, Not(subagentAccepted13))), and design.md:1130 / requirements.md:512 cite CHECK-13a/13b correctly as the machine-checked piece while acknowledging the categorical half is prose-only. The artifacts make no overstated 'formal owner' claim. The genuine residual concern (categorical half unmodeled) is already captured by the confirmed 'omission-categorical-enforcement' edit.

refuted.item: ears_needing_work: REQ-SPEC-018 / 29.2 (SubagentStop rejects absent/null, exit 2) — status partial because no implementation exists
refuted.why: The EARS mapping itself is fully present and wired in the artifacts: design component row 148, wiring row 218, Property 30 (design.md:1128), and CHECK-13a/13b. 'partial' here means code-not-written (implementation status), not a missing artifact mapping. No add-wiring edit needed for the criterion-to-artifact mapping; the residual artifact gaps (empty-semantics, audit allow-path, dual-gate ordering) are captured separately as confirmed edits.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a task to create .github/workflows/deepeval-gate.yml and register deepeval-gate as a REQUIRED status check
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 55. Eval-gating in CI (**REQ-EVAL-001**) — implement `tools/deepeval_gate.py` with DeepEval `assert_test()` ... register `deepeval-gate` as a REQUIRED CI status check at merge.
confirmed_edits.change: Add a subtask 55.1 (mirroring task 40.3 / task 56) 'Create .github/workflows/deepeval-gate.yml running `pytest tools/deepeval_gate.py` (or `deepeval test run`), and register `deepeval-gate` as a REQUIRED status check in the GitHub repository ruleset; document in docs/github-ruleset.md.' Today task 55 only implements the Python module — unlike zap-baseline (task 56) which creates its `.github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml`, and secrets-scan/audit-chain-verify (tasks 40.2/40.3) which create their workflows. The design.md:149 row points at `tools/deepeval_gate.py` (a module), so the 'REQUIRED CI status check at merge' claim has no workflow file or ruleset-registration task behind it.

confirmed_edits.title: Add deepeval-gate (and zap-baseline) to the canonical required-status-checks doc list
confirmed_edits.anchor: Document (in `docs/github-ruleset.md`) the required status checks: `formal-verification`, `coverage-gate`, `secrets-scan` (task 45), `audit-chain-verify` (task 52), plus human PR reviewer requirement
confirmed_edits.change: Update the required-status-checks enumeration at task 14.2 to also list `deepeval-gate` (task 55) and `zap-baseline` (task 56), so the single documented source of truth for the ruleset matches requirements.md:484/489 and design.md:149/150, both of which declare these two P3 checks REQUIRED at merge. As written the doc list contradicts those REQUIRED-check declarations by omitting both P3 gates.

confirmed_edits.title: Register the 0.8/0.7 eval thresholds in the Requirement-20 threshold registry with an owner and override path
confirmed_edits.anchor: 3. THE System SHALL register the following additional DEFAULT thresholds in the threshold registry *(Reconciliation 2026-06-15: numeric defaults added)*:
confirmed_edits.change: Add two entries to the Req 20.3 registry: 'faithfulness threshold = 0.8 (DEFAULT, configurable) — OWNER: the quality owner (Req 9)' and 'answer-relevancy threshold = 0.7 (DEFAULT, configurable) — OWNER: the quality owner (Req 9)', each with an explicit operator-override path, and drop the 'e.g.' qualifier at requirements.md:483/484 so 0.8/0.7 are authoritative DEFAULTs not illustrations. Req 20.2 mandates ALL numeric thresholds be configurable DEFAULTs requiring operator override; the eval metrics currently appear only as 'e.g.' examples and are unowned, violating that invariant.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the eval input/dataset source and reconcile criterion-2 WHEN-guard with the unconditional REQUIRED check
confirmed_edits.anchor: 2. WHEN a CI run includes LLM output evaluation, THEN the eval step SHALL gate on configured metric thresholds (e.g., faithfulness ≥ 0.8, answer relevancy ≥ 0.7) using DeepEval `assert_test()`, failing the build if any threshold is not met.
confirmed_edits.change: Name the producer of the evaluable output and the dataset source for the DeepEval LLMTestCase fields (input / actual_output / retrieval_context) — e.g., a golden-set fixture under tests/eval/ or evidence captured from Verifier runs — and reconcile the WHEN-guard with criterion 3: state what happens when a CI run contains NO LLM output (the REQUIRED `deepeval-gate` check must define whether it passes vacuously or is skipped, so the unconditional REQUIRED status at criterion 3 is not either always-green-and-meaningless or blocking-with-nothing-to-evaluate). Faithfulness and answer-relevancy are RAG-style metrics needing (input, actual_output, retrieval_context) tuples; no producer is sourced anywhere in the spec.

confirmed_edits.title: Name the evaluator (judge) LLM and reconcile the LLM-as-judge gate with the governing 'predictions never gate' invariant
confirmed_edits.anchor: | `deepeval_gate.py` | `tools/deepeval_gate.py` | 3 | DeepEval pytest-native eval step; calls `assert_test()` on configured metric thresholds (faithfulness ≥ 0.8, answer relevancy ≥ 0.7); runs as REQUIRED CI status check `deepeval-gate` at merge (REQ-EVAL-001). *(P3 market-research 2026-06-15.)* |
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design subsection (or expand the component row) for deepeval_gate.py that: (a) names the evaluator model + provider and pins it with a fixed temperature/seed for reproducibility; and (b) explicitly reconciles this LLM-as-judge gate with the governing invariant at design.md:7 / requirements.md:7 ('Model self-assessment and probabilistic predictions only inform; they never gate'). DeepEval faithfulness/answer-relevancy are LLM-judge metrics, so a REQUIRED merge gate computing them directly contradicts the top-level invariant unless reclassified (e.g., advisory-quality vs the deterministic gates, or scoped to non-self-assessment of agent output) — currently the contradiction is unresolved and no judge model is named.

confirmed_edits.title: Make REQ-EVAL-001 traceability concrete: add a verification mechanism (smoke test) and/or a cross-ref-table row
confirmed_edits.anchor: | — (omission-declaration gate) | Property 30 | CHECK-13a/13b | Requirement 29 |
confirmed_edits.change: Add a row to the property↔requirement cross-reference table (requirements.md:505-512) for Requirement 30 stating it carries NO Z3 Property/CHECK and is verified by CI behavior (the `deepeval-gate` named tool) — making the PRD:130 traceability claim ('every new requirement carries a Z3 check OR a named verification tool') concrete and killing the Property-30-vs-Requirement-30 collision trap (Property 30 belongs to Requirement 29; Requirement 30 has no property). Pair with a tasks.md smoke/integration test task asserting `deepeval-gate` fails the build when a metric is below threshold, since the harness stops at CHECK-13b and no PBT property covers REQ-EVAL-001.

confirmed_edits.title: Pin deepeval in the dependency manifest
confirmed_edits.anchor: Create `pyproject.toml` (or `requirements.txt`) pinning `z3-solver`, `hypothesis`, `jsonschema`, `psycopg2-binary`, `semgrep`, `playwright`
confirmed_edits.change: Add `deepeval` (version-pinned) to the dependency-pin list in task 1 (line 15), alongside the existing pins. tools/deepeval_gate.py (task 55) imports DeepEval but the package is never declared, so a Phase-3 build would have an undeclared/unpinned dependency.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a fail-mode row for deepeval-gate to the Hook/gate Failure Modes table
confirmed_edits.anchor: | `spec_validator.py` Z3 timeout | Returns `{violation_count: -1, error: "validator_timeout"}`. Stop hook treats `violation_count < 0` as a blocking error. DEFAULT timeout: 60 seconds. |
confirmed_edits.change: Add a row to the Error Handling / failure-modes table defining deepeval-gate's behavior on evaluator-LLM failure (API timeout, rate-limit, unavailability, non-determinism/flake): specify fail-closed vs fail-open and any retry/flake mitigation. Every other gate with a non-trivial failure mode (spec_validator Z3 timeout, Postgres unavailable, hook crashes) has an explicit fail-closed row; a REQUIRED merge gate whose judge-LLM can time out has no defined fail behavior.

confirmed_edits.title: Define eval metric terms in the requirements glossary
confirmed_edits.anchor: - **Playwright**: The E2E test framework used to produce behavioral proofs (captured traces/screenshots) as evidence.
confirmed_edits.change: Add glossary entries defining the DeepEval-specific terms that gate a REQUIRED merge check: 'faithfulness', 'answer relevancy', and 'eval-gate' (deepeval-gate). The requirements.md glossary (lines 15-48) defines OTel, SLSA, Semgrep_CodeQL, etc., but none of the three eval terms that a REQUIRED check depends on — leaving the gate's core metrics undefined.

confirmed_edits.title: Document whether deepeval-gate decisions are captured in the gate_audit_log, or why CI-status-check gates are out of its scope
confirmed_edits.anchor: 4. **Producer:** a new `tools/audit_log.py` SHALL expose `append(event, tool, decision, reason, requirement_id, actor_agent)`, and it SHALL be CALLED by `stop_hook.py`, `pre_tool_use_hook.py`, and `subagent_stop_hook.py` on every allow/block decision; `tools/audit_verify.py` SHALL recompute the chain.
confirmed_edits.change: Add a one-line scope note to REQ-AUDIT (Req 27) stating that the hash-chained gate_audit_log captures Claude Code hook decisions, and that CI-status-check gates (deepeval-gate, zap-baseline, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify) are recorded via GitHub check-run history / SLSA rather than the hook audit log — OR, if a deepeval-gate block should be in the chain, add it to the producer caller list. Today a REQUIRED merge-gate decision (deepeval-gate) is captured by neither the audit-log caller list nor an explicit out-of-scope note, leaving a hole in the 'we can prove what happened' chain.

refuted.item: open_gaps[7] / PRD:45 — reword promptfoo 'Phase 3+' deferral to a concrete later phase
refuted.why: PRD-only finding. The cited text lives in PRD_MERGED_canonical_spec_to_evidence.md:45 (and requirements.md only references 'promptfoo deferred to Phase 3+' parenthetically at line 484). The PRD is outside the four target files for this validation (requirements/design/tasks/formal_verification_merged.py). The phrasing nit ('Phase 3+' for an alternative to a Phase-3 component) does not constitute a gap in any of the four target artifacts; the requirements.md:484 parenthetical is accurate as a back-reference. Out of editable scope.

refuted.item: critic requirements.md:417 — B08/B11 baseline mapping for eval-gating is 'nominal'
refuted.why: Not a defect or inconsistency. Requirement 30 IS mapped to B08/B11 at requirements.md:417, and the addendum text at 403 explicitly states the added requirements extend existing blocks without introducing new ones. The critique that the extension is 'narratively nominal' (B08 is the verifier, B11 is OTel, neither literally produces evals) is a quality observation, not a contradiction — the mapping is present, defensible (eval-gating is a verification+observability concern), and matches how every Req 20-32 addendum item is baseline-traced. No artifact change is warranted.

refuted.item: critic design.md:54 — the CI/Merge-Gate mermaid subgraph omits a deepeval node
refuted.why: Correct observation but not an actionable artifact gap. The CI subgraph (design.md:50-55) is generic — it has a single GH_RULESET node ('required status checks') and does NOT enumerate ANY individual check (no coverage-gate, formal-verification, secrets-scan, or zap-baseline node either). Diagrams at this altitude are not expected to list each check; the 'unwired' substance is already captured by the confirmed missing-workflow-task and missing-ruleset-doc-list edits. Adding a deepeval-specific node would be inconsistent with how the diagram treats every other check.

refuted.item: critic PRD:17 — '32-term glossary' completeness claim contradicted by undefined eval terms
refuted.why: Duplicate of the actionable glossary edit, and the PRD self-assessment sentence itself is PRD-only (out of the four target files). The substantive, in-scope remedy — defining faithfulness/answer-relevancy/eval-gate — is already emitted as the requirements.md glossary edit (dedup_key unique:eval-glossary-terms). Editing the PRD's prose claim is out of scope; no separate edit needed.

refuted.item: open_gaps[8] omnibus 'design/tasks coverage is thin' as a standalone edit
refuted.why: Collapsed, not refuted as substance. Every concrete sub-item it lists is already emitted as a specific edit: design subsection + judge model (unique:eval-judge-llm-vs-invariant), error-handling row (unique:eval-fail-mode-row), task-55 subtasks (unique:deepeval-workflow-task + the smoke-test half of unique:eval-no-property-traceability), threshold-config location (unique:eval-thresholds-registry), eval input source (unique:eval-input-source-and-when-guard). Emitting a separate omnibus 'expand everything' edit would duplicate all of them. The Phase-0 build-table omission (design.md:621-636) is correct-by-design — that table is the Phase-0 spine only, and deepeval is a Phase-3 component, so its absence there is not a gap.

confirmed_edits.title: Fix B15→B16 baseline-block collision for Requirement 31 (DAST) in both matrix row and header
confirmed_edits.anchor: Line 418 matrix row `| 31 DAST Baseline Security Scan | B15 | REQ-SEC-008 |` AND line 487 header `**Baseline block:** B15 | **PRD references:** REQ-SEC-008` (Requirement 31)
confirmed_edits.change: Reassign Requirement 31 / REQ-SEC-008 from B15 to B16. B15 is 'Durable storage' (line 397, mapped to Requirement 16 / REQ-STORE-001..003); B16 is 'Supply-chain & security gates' (line 398, REQ-SEC-001..005) which is the correct home for a DAST security scan. Change BOTH the traceability-matrix row (line 418) and the Requirement-31 header (line 487) from `B15` to `B16`. Do NOT use B17 ('Human-in-the-loop approval') — the kill-switch (Req 32) is dual-tagged B16/B17 only because it spans gates+approval; a pure DAST scan belongs to B16 alone. B16 is already defined in the baseline-block list (line 398), so no list change is needed.

Resulting list: `formal-verification`, `coverage-gate`, `secrets-scan` (task 45), `audit-chain-verify` (task 52), `zap-baseline` (task 56), `deepeval-gate` (task 55), plus human PR reviewer requirement.

confirmed_edits.title: Add task 40.4/40.5 subtasks that create-and-register zap-baseline (and deepeval-gate) as REQUIRED CI checks
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 40 'Integrate `formal_verification_merged.py` as required CI check (Phase 4 hardening)' (line 689); existing subtasks 40.1 (line 690), 40.2 secrets-scan (line 695), 40.3 audit-chain-verify (line 700); dependency-graph wave 33 `"tasks": ["40.1", "40.2", "40.3"]` (line 875)

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the zap-baseline workflow `on:` trigger (on every PR)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 56, line 976: '56. DAST baseline security scan (**REQ-SEC-008**) — add `.github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml` using `zaproxy/action-baseline@v0.12.0` with `fail_action: true`; register `zap-baseline` as a REQUIRED CI status check; baseline passive scan only ...'
confirmed_edits.change: Add the workflow trigger to task 56 (and the design.md:150 inventory row): `on: pull_request` (optionally with a target-branch filter for the protected branch), so REQ-SEC-008.1's 'on every PR' prose has a concrete mechanism. Mirror how task 41.1 (property-tests, line 707) states 'Trigger on every `pull_request`'. No canonical file currently defines any `on:` block, branch filter, or event scope for zap-baseline.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the v1 baseline DAST scan target
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component-inventory row, design.md:150 `| zap-baseline.yml | .github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml | 2 | OWASP ZAP baseline passive scan ... |` (no target specified anywhere in canonical artifacts)
confirmed_edits.change: Add a design.md detail (in the inventory row note or a CI-workflows subsection) defining WHAT ZAP scans for v1: how the app is stood up in CI (e.g. spin up the app against a known localhost URL, or scan a deployed preview/ephemeral environment) and how the action's target/URL input is populated. confirmed_edits.title: Add zap-baseline (and deepeval_gate, flagd, omission_guard) to the design.md 'New / changed components' Phase-2 reconciliation list
confirmed_edits.anchor: '### New / changed components' list, design.md:1179-1185 (enumerates state_integrity, audit_log/audit_verify, perf_a11y_verifier, REASONING span, research fact-check, optional role subagents — OMITS the four P3 CI/feature-flag additions)
confirmed_edits.change: Add bullet entries to the 'New / changed components' list (line 1179) for the four Phase-2 P3 additions that are present in the component-inventory table (design.md:148-151) but missing from this reconciliation list: `zap-baseline.yml` (Phase 2, OWASP ZAP baseline passive scan, trigger/target/fail_action semantics, ruleset registration, REQ-SEC-008); `deepeval_gate.py` (REQ-EVAL-001); `flagd` kill-switch (REQ-CTRL-001); `omission_guard` (REQ-SPEC-018). At minimum add zap-baseline with a one-line description of its trigger, scan target, fail_action semantics, report handling, and ruleset registration, parallel to the audit_log/audit_verify treatment at design.md:1181.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Glossary entry for 'DAST'

confirmed_edits.change: Add a Glossary entry after the Semgrep_CodeQL entry (line 47): '**DAST**: Dynamic Application Security Testing — black-box scanning of a running application; here the OWASP ZAP baseline passive scan (REQ-SEC-008).' confirmed_edits.title: Annotate REQ-SEC-008 as a [DET] CI-gate obligation explicitly outside the formal-verification harness scope

confirmed_edits.title: Fix property-count contradiction: tasks.md:732 says '29 correctness properties' but canonical count is 30

Line 732 is the sole '29' outlier and is internally contradicted by line 979 in the same file. NOTE: shared/spec-wide inconsistency, not specific to zap-baseline.

refuted.item: Critic 'overstatement': draft behavior claims zap-baseline 'acts as the DAST member of the CI / Merge-Gate security-check set ... cites design.md:50'
refuted.why: The factual core is correct (verified: design.md CI subgraph lines 50-55 lists only OPA, SAST, SLSA, GH_RULESET — neither zap-baseline nor secrets-scan appears, and no prose names a 'security-check set'). But this critiques a DRAFT behavior write-up, not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The design.md CI subgraph is a deliberately abstracted mermaid diagram (it also omits secrets-scan, coverage-gate, audit-chain-verify, deepeval-gate — i.e. it is not an exhaustive gate list). The substantive missing-wiring (zap-baseline absent from required-checks) is already captured by the tasks.md:270 + task-40.x edits. No standalone canonical edit warranted.

refuted.item: Open-gap 'deepeval-gate has the identical omission' treated as a separate zap-component edit; critic finding #3 (deepeval standalone-confirmed)
refuted.why: Confirmed as real (verified: deepeval-gate absent from tasks.md:270 and lacks any 40.x subtask, identical to zap-baseline). confirmed_edits.title: flagd kill-switch has no enforcement-mechanism design subsection
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory row: | `flagd` | `flagd/` (self-hosted service, Apache 2.0, CNCF Incubating) | 2 | OpenFeature + flagd agent kill-switch; near-real-time flag updates (≤ 30 s polling) without process restart; self-hosted, no SaaS dependency (REQ-CTRL-001).
confirmed_edits.change: Author a new design subsection 'Agent Kill-Switch (REQ-CTRL-001)' parallel to the 'Sandbox & Worktree Isolation (REQ-17.4)' subsection (design.md:514). It MUST specify: (a) ENFORCEMENT POINT — each agent entry point (initializer/implementer/verifier subagents) queries the OpenFeature client at start-of-turn; a set kill flag causes the gated capability to hard-refuse, analogous to a PreToolUse exit-2 block; define 'disabled' operationally (refuse the tool call / skip the capability). (b) FLAG SCHEMA — enumerate the kill-switch flag name(s) and the agent capability each gates. (c) PROVIDER CHOICE — pick a default (gRPC streaming for true near-real-time, OR file-based with an explicit poll interval ≤30 s) and tie the ≤30 s propagation guarantee to that provider's mechanism. (d) FAIL-CLOSED behavior when flagd is unreachable (see separate edit). (e) AUDIT/OTel wiring on every toggle (see separate edit). Also add a Hook-Wiring-Table row or equivalent showing where the flag is checked.

confirmed_edits.title: flagd unreachable behavior (fail-open vs fail-closed) unspecified — conflicts with fail-closed invariant
confirmed_edits.anchor: New 'Agent Kill-Switch (REQ-CTRL-001)' subsection (and Requirement 32 in requirements.md:492-496)
confirmed_edits.change: Specify the unreachable-flagd behavior explicitly as FAIL-CLOSED: when the self-hosted flagd server / flag source is unavailable, the gated agent capability defaults to DISABLED (not enabled). Document this in the new design subsection and reconcile it with REQ-GATE-005 (requirements.md:207: 'ambiguous states SHALL resolve to blocked, not passed'). Add a corresponding acceptance criterion to Requirement 32 (e.g. 32.4: 'IF the flagd flag source is unreachable, THEN the affected agent capability SHALL default to disabled (fail-closed), consistent with REQ-GATE-005.').

confirmed_edits.title: flagd provider choice 'file-based or gRPC' left open; ≤30s guarantee unbacked
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 57. Agent kill-switch (**REQ-CTRL-001**) — deploy self-hosted flagd service (`flagd/` directory, Apache 2.0, CNCF Incubating); wire OpenFeature SDK into agent entry points; verify near-real-time flag propagation (≤ 30 s) without process restart.
confirmed_edits.change: Amend task 57 to pick a concrete provider rather than leaving 'file-based or gRPC' open: specify either gRPC streaming (near-instant propagation) OR file-based polling with an explicit poll interval ≤30 s, and tie the ≤30 s verification step to the chosen provider's mechanism. Also add the scaffold deliverables: create the `flagd/` directory with flag-definitions file (flagd.json), provider config, and a self-hosted deployment manifest, plus the kill-switch flag schema enumerating flag name(s) and gated capabilities.

confirmed_edits.title: Kill-switch ≤30s polling interval missing from Requirement-20 threshold registry
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 20.3 list (requirements.md:356-359): '- **Cost/token budget** = 1,000,000 tokens per Slice ... - **Reasoning-loop K** = 3 ... - **Retry budget** = 3 per Slice — OWNER: the implementer loop ...'
confirmed_edits.change: Register the kill-switch polling interval as a new bullet under Requirement 20.3: '- **Kill-switch flag-propagation interval** = ≤30 s (configurable) — OWNER: the Delivery Owner / Operator (owns the flagd deployment); the affected agent capability is disabled within this interval without a process restart (REQ-CTRL-001 / Requirement 32.2).' confirmed_edits.title: No accountable owner/role named for kill-switch flag flips

confirmed_edits.title: B16/B17 coverage matrix never updated to show Requirement 32 extends those blocks
confirmed_edits.anchor: Domain-Baseline Coverage Matrix rows: '| B16 | Supply-chain & security gates | Requirement 17 (REQ-SEC-001..005) | MAPPED |' and '| B17 | Human-in-the-loop approval | Requirement 18 (REQ-HITL-001..003) | MAPPED |' (requirements.md:398-399)

refuted.item: open_gaps[1]: Task 57 (flagd build) is NOT scheduled in the dependency-graph waves; tasks 54-57 'appended below the wave block and never assigned a wave id'; component 'has no place in the build DAG — unscheduled.'
refuted.why: FACTUALLY WRONG (matches the critic_findings contradiction). The Task Dependency Graph JSON DOES schedule task 57: wave id 48 = ["57"] (tasks.md:934-935), with tasks 54/55/56 in waves 45/46/47 (tasks.md:921-932). All of tasks 53-57 sit INSIDE the waves array (ids 0-48), not appended below it. The suggested fix ('Extend the waves JSON with waves scheduling tasks 54-57') is moot/harmful — it would create duplicate wave entries. The REAL defect (stale prose count-note at tasks.md:950) is captured separately under dedup_key shared:tasks-wave-count-note-36-48.

refuted.item: open_gaps[3]: design.md gives flagd ONE inventory row and nothing else — no design subsection, no enforcement architecture, no flag schema, no provider decision, no fail-open/closed, no audit wiring.

Emitting one omnibus 'thinness' edit would duplicate those; the catch-all is therefore folded into them rather than re-emitted.

refuted.item: open_gaps[0] sub-claim: the `flagd/` path is 'purely notional ... with no scaffold' and should perhaps be marked 'to be created by task 57'.

The genuinely actionable residue (missing flag schema + provider config + deployment manifest scaffold deliverables) is folded into unique:flagd-provider-choice; no separate 'dangling path' edit is warranted.

This aligns the glossary with Req 1.4's 'exactly one of the five EARS patterns', Property 16's five-value enum, and the reconciliation note already in design.md:1187-1188.

confirmed_edits.title: Make Requirement 1.2 cite the canonical vague-adjective set
confirmed_edits.anchor: 2. IF a candidate requirement contains a non-deterministic adjective (e.g., "fast", "secure", "scalable", "optimized") without a quantified numeric bound, THEN THE Spec_Compiler SHALL reject it and require a quantified criterion before acceptance.
confirmed_edits.change: Change Req 1.2's parenthetical from the 4-example illustrative list to the canonical 7-member set used by the spec_validator scanner (tasks.md:68) and Property 17 (design.md:1012), or explicitly mark it non-exhaustive: 'a non-deterministic adjective (the canonical reject-set is {fast, secure, scalable, optimized, efficient, reliable, performant}; the validator may extend it)'. confirmed_edits.title: Name the persisted compiled-spec artifact for Requirement 1.3

confirmed_edits.title: Specify Research-claim epistemic precondition (Req 24.2) on the initializer's discovery pass

confirmed_edits.title: Wire task 10.1's loop/timeout defaults to the task-44 config module

The current task 10.1 references the 7-pass cap with no link to its owning config module.

confirmed_edits.title: Decompose task 10.1 into testable subtasks with acceptance tests
confirmed_edits.anchor: - [ ] 10.1 Write `.claude/agents/initializer.md` — Spec Compiler + Coverage Model Builder
confirmed_edits.change: The initializer carries the heaviest requirement load of any subagent yet task 10.1 is a single subtask whose only acceptance test (design.md:628) is 'Hand-run: produces valid feature_list.json from sample intent'. Decompose into testable steps each with an acceptance check: (a) spec-compile to EARS + atomic IDs; (b) validator-loop with 7-pass cap and strict-decrease no-progress → HANDOFF; (c) discovery + UNMAPPED flagging; (d) provenance marking (stated/inferred) with per-inferred-item human confirmation gating before items enter feature_list.json (Req 2.3 — currently only bulk plan-mode review); (e) WIRING/NFR first-class emission (5.4); (f) feature_list.json seed+populate; (g) plan-mode handoff without writing plan-approved.json; (h) omission_declaration emission (29.1). Add a behavioral fixture beyond the single hand-run.

confirmed_edits.title: Highest-priority selection predicate is underspecified (no tie-break, no dependency respect)

confirmed_edits.title: omission_declaration scope omits the implementer; resolve 18.1-vs-18.3 inconsistency at the implementer SubagentStop boundary
confirmed_edits.anchor: 1. WHEN a subagent (initializer, research, verifier) completes a discovery, research, or verification pass, THEN its output SHALL include a non-null `omission_declaration` field
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-SPEC-018.1 (requirements.md:476) enumerates only '(initializer, research, verifier)' while REQ-SPEC-018.3 (requirements.md:478) says 'THE JSON schema for EACH subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration as a required, non-nullable field' and the SubagentStop omission guard (design.md:218) rejects any subagent result lacking it. This leaves the implementer's SubagentStop return contract ambiguous: the implementer legitimately emits NO Evidence_Record, so subagent_stop_hook (tasks.md:177-183, REQ-VERIFY-005/9.5) and the omission guard need an explicit rule for the implementer→verifier handoff. Resolve by either (a) explicitly listing the implementer in 18.1 with its own omission_declaration obligation, or (b) stating in 18.1/design.md:218 that the implementer subagent is exempt from the evidence-field and omission-declaration checks at SubagentStop (it produces a commit, not evidence) — and reflect the chosen rule in subagent_stop_hook.py task 8.1 and the omission_guard component (design.md:148).

confirmed_edits.title: Fix acting_agent→actor_agent field name in Property 24 PBT task
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task 39.13: "Generate evidence chains with arbitrary `acting_agent` identities; assert any Evidence_Record where `acting_agent == \"implementer.md\"` is rejected"

confirmed_edits.title: Add runtime enforcement mechanism for Property 24 (implementer-cannot-self-verify)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Property 24: Subagent Role Separation (design.md:1066-1070); evidence_records.actor_agent column comment (design.md:473)
confirmed_edits.change: Property 24 has no enforcement: actor_agent is only a SQL column comment, and no hook/tool validates actor_agent != implementer at evidence-write time. Add an actor-identity check to the design: in subagent_stop_hook.py (or the PreToolUse status guard), block any feature_list.json status→proven write or evidence_records insert whose actor_agent equals the implementer that produced the slice. Cite this new mechanism from Property 24, and add the corresponding Hook Wiring Table row (REQ-VERIFY-002 / Req 9.2). Verified: grep of formal_verification_merged.py returns zero hits for actor_agent/verifier/implementer/role — no Z3 check models role separation; CHECK-13 uses a generic subagentAccepted13 boolean with no per-subagent identity. Optionally add a Z3 CHECK modeling actor_agent==verifier for any proven transition.

confirmed_edits.title: Resolve Verifier write-scope inconsistency: 'status field only' vs 'Evidence_Records to feature_list.json'
confirmed_edits.anchor: design.md:267 'Read/write on `tests/`, `feature_list.json` status field only' vs design.md:629 (Phase-0 component #7 test) 'can write Evidence_Records to `feature_list.json`'
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:267 limits the Verifier to writing the feature_list.json status field only, while the Phase-0 spine acceptance test at design.md:629 says it 'can write Evidence_Records to feature_list.json' (the status+evidence sub-object). tasks.md:214 echoes 'status field only'. Reconcile to one scope: state the Verifier writes the status field AND the attached evidence sub-object (since a status→proven flip requires writing the Evidence_Record per the schema at design.md:381-384). Align design.md:267, design.md:629, and tasks.md:214.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Verifier WIRING-failed transition behavior consuming wiring_checker output (Req 8.2)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Verifier key-behaviors list (design.md:257-265); Req 8.2/REQ-EXEC-011 'THE Verifier SHALL mark the corresponding WIRING item failed'
confirmed_edits.change: Req 8.2 names the Verifier as the actor that marks unreachable WIRING items failed, and wiring_checker.py (task 19) emits WIRING candidates, but the Verifier's key-behaviors list (design.md:257-265) never mentions WIRING handling and no design step connects the Verifier to consume wiring_checker output or perform the unproven→failed transition. Add a Verifier key-behavior: ingest wiring_checker.py findings and set unreachable WIRING items to failed (unproven→failed edge). Reference REQ-EXEC-011 and requirements.md:129 ('unproven→failed (Verifier on a dead check)').

confirmed_edits.title: Add Verifier WIRING integration-test evidence behavior (Req 8.3 / task 47)
confirmed_edits.anchor: Verifier key-behaviors list (design.md:257-265); Req 8.3/REQ-EXEC-012; tasks.md:959 task 47
confirmed_edits.change: Req 8.3 requires an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record when a WIRING item is claimed proven, and task 47 (tasks.md:959) adds this evidence requirement, but the Verifier's key-behaviors never describe the WIRING-integration-test special case that the Verifier must attach. Add a Verifier behavior: when proving a WIRING item, require/attach an integration-test Evidence_Record exercising the real path. Cross-reference task 47 and REQ-EXEC-012.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify artifact-guard carve-out allowing the Verifier to write tests/
confirmed_edits.anchor: PreToolUse artifact guard row (design.md:214, protects `tests/`); Implementer denied tests/ (design.md:251); Verifier read/write tests/ (design.md:267)
confirmed_edits.change: The artifact guard (design.md:214) blocks any tool targeting tests/ as a protected artifact and the Implementer is denied tests/ writes (design.md:251), yet the Verifier has read/write on tests/ (design.md:267) — with no exception path defined for how the Verifier writes through the guard. Specify the carve-out: key the artifact guard on actor_agent (allow verifier.md test writes) OR restrict the guard to test-file deletion/rewrite vs addition, while still blocking the Implementer and destructive ops. Add the qualifier to the design.md:214 artifact-guard row and the design.md:267 Verifier permissions.

confirmed_edits.title: Add Verifier frontmatter (tools allowlist / model / description) to design
confirmed_edits.anchor: Subagent Definitions §`.claude/agents/verifier.md` (design.md:253-267); 'following the Claude Code agent spec' (design.md:224)
confirmed_edits.change: design.md:224 says each subagent follows the Claude Code agent spec (which requires name/description/tools/model frontmatter), but the verifier.md section gives only prose permissions — no concrete tools allowlist, model, or description. Permission scoping in Claude Code is enforced via the tools allowlist, not prose, so 'no write access to implementation' is currently unenforceable as written. Add the verifier.md frontmatter to the design: explicit tools allowlist (Read, Bash for test runners, the evidence_collector tool; NO Write/Edit/MultiEdit on src/), model selection, and a description. Mirror into tasks.md task 10.3 (anchor: tasks.md:213-216).

confirmed_edits.title: Reconcile Phase-0 five-layer claim with Phase 1/3 perf_a11y_verifier deferral
confirmed_edits.anchor: Requirement 9.1 (requirements.md:186) 'verify each Slice across five layers ... performance + accessibility'; perf_a11y_verifier Phase '1/3' (design.md:146, design.md:1182); task 51 (tasks.md:966)
confirmed_edits.change: requirements.md:186 asserts the Verifier runs all five layers unconditionally, but the fifth layer (perf_a11y_verifier) is Phase 1/3 (design.md:146, design.md:1182) and its implementation is task 51 in the Phase-4-era added-task block (tasks.md:966), while the Verifier itself ships in Phase 0. The Phase-0 spine therefore cannot deliver the fifth layer. Scope requirements.md:186 to note the performance/accessibility layer activates in Phase 1/3 (the structural/semantic/behavioral/security layers ship in Phase 0), OR move perf_a11y_verifier to Phase 0. Keep consistent with the design Component Inventory Phase column.

confirmed_edits.title: Specify research.md frontmatter: web-search tool grant and model, reconciled with permission scope

confirmed_edits.title: Specify the DB-row producer for domain_baseline_checklists vs research.md baselines/-only scope
confirmed_edits.anchor: -- domain_baseline_checklists: version history of product-class checklists ... CREATE TABLE domain_baseline_checklists (design.md:498-509)
confirmed_edits.change: No actor is specified for INSERTing the `domain_baseline_checklists` row (the persisted record of research.md's output, carrying sha/version/approved_at/approved_by). research.md has `baselines/`-only filesystem scope and no DB write (design.md:280), so it cannot write Postgres under its current scope. Name the producer — the human-approval action or a SessionStart/PostToolUse hook that records the row on approval — and reconcile it against research.md's permission scope. Add this to the Domain-Baseline Checklist Lifecycle section (design.md:609-613) and/or the checklist-approval guard row (design.md:213).

confirmed_edits.title: Define the per-subagent research-output JSON schema and checklist file-format schema
confirmed_edits.anchor: Component Inventory + Data Models sections (no research-output schema artifact; design.md:124-155, 286-510)
confirmed_edits.change: REQ-SPEC-018.3 (requirements.md:478) requires 'THE JSON schema for each subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration', but no per-subagent output JSON schema artifact for research is named anywhere (only feature_list.schema.json exists), and no checklist file-format schema defines what a `baselines/*.md` must contain (per-item fields, per-claim source/authority-tier/fact-check structure). Add: (1) a research-output JSON schema (e.g. `schema/research_output.schema.json`) carrying a required non-nullable `omission_declaration`, and (2) a checklist-item schema specifying claim/source/authority-tier fields — and cite both from task 10.4 and task 54. Without a defined output format, REQ-SPEC-014/017/018.3 cannot be machine-validated.

confirmed_edits.title: Define the 'new product class' detection mechanism and owning component
confirmed_edits.anchor: **Lifecycle rules:** 4. The Research_Sub_Agent is triggered only once per product class ... Re-research is triggered only by an explicit operator request. (design.md:613)
confirmed_edits.change: Req 3.1 says research runs 'WHEN a new product class is encountered for the first time' and design.md:613 says 'only once per product class', but neither defines HOW first-encounter / newness is DETECTED or which component decides. Add: the Initializer detects newness via a lookup against `domain_baseline_checklists` by `product_class` (no approved row for that class ⇒ new ⇒ trigger research); cite this from the research.md trigger behavior (design.md:274 'Triggered by the Initializer when a product class has no existing checklist') and the lifecycle rules.

confirmed_edits.title: Add a phase-ordering constraint so research.md's epistemic gates exist before research is invoked
confirmed_edits.anchor: Task Dependency Graph wave 9: tasks ['9.2','10.1','10.2','10.3','10.4','11.1'] (tasks.md:779); tasks 50/54 in waves 41/45 (tasks.md:907,923)
confirmed_edits.change: research.md (task 10.4) is in the Phase-0 parallel wave 9 with NO dependency edge to its epistemic-integrity gate (task 50, REQ-SPEC-016/017) or its omission-declaration gate (task 54, REQ-SPEC-018) or the checklist-approval test (39.17/39.19), all of which are Phase 3+ (waves 41/45). The agent is buildable-and-mergeable ~3 phases before its gates exist, so during Phase 0-2 research.md could emit unlabeled/unfact-checked DRAFT claims and an un-gated DRAFT checklist could be used for discovery. Either (a) add a Notes constraint that research must not be INVOKED on an un-gated product class until tasks 50/54 land, or (b) add a dependency edge / note that task 10.4's research-invocation path is gated by 50/54. Document the intentional Phase-0-build / Phase-3-gate deferral explicitly in the task 10.4 body.

confirmed_edits.anchor: ### Requirement 3: Domain-Baseline Checklist Sourcing — **PRD references:** REQ-SPEC-013..015 (requirements.md:87); and Requirement 2 — REQ-SPEC-010..012 (requirements.md:73)

Add an explicit mapping note under Requirement 2 and Requirement 3 stating that REQ-SPEC-010=2.1, 011=2.2, 012=2.3 and REQ-SPEC-013=3.1, 014=3.2, 015=3.3 (or render them as explicit EARS sub-IDs). This is a cross-component issue shared with the Proactive-Discovery component (Requirement 2).

refuted.item: open_gap: research.md component file does not exist on disk (.claude/agents/ has only .gitkeep); task 10.4 is [ ]
refuted.why: This is expected pre-implementation Phase-0 state, not a defect in the canonical ARTIFACTS this validator governs. Task 10.4 exists and is correctly unchecked (tasks.md:218-221), design.md:155/631 list research.md, and the PRD 'four subagent definitions' claim is a forward-looking spec promise. No requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md edit is warranted — the on-disk absence is the implementation backlog, not a spec gap. (The genuine spec-level weaknesses around research.md are captured by the confirmed edits above.)

refuted.item: critic: Property 18 (Checklist Version Link Fidelity) is absent from the draft's ears_stories/properties
refuted.why: This critiques the DRAFT's coverage, not the artifacts. Property 18 fully EXISTS and is correctly specified in design.md:1018-1022 with a dedicated property test (task 39.9, tasks.md:626-630, tagged) and is cited from task 39.7. The artifact pins checklist_ref.path/.version/.sha and SHA-must-match-git-blob. No artifact edit is needed.

refuted.why: Requirement 28 EXISTS and correctly names planner/implementer/verifier/research as the four fixed roles with the 'none SHALL have verifier write access' constraint (requirements.md:466). This is a draft-coverage observation, not a gap in the artifacts. research.md's permission scope (design.md:280, no verifier/tests/CI write) is already consistent with that constraint. No artifact edit needed.

refuted.item: critic: numeric COLLISION between tasks.md and design.md for the domain_baseline_checklists migration number
refuted.why: REFUTED — there is no collision. tasks.md assigns 006=domain_baseline_checklists (28.6), 007=requirement_versions (28.7), 008=gate_audit_log (28.8); design.md:427 says 'six original + requirement_versions + gate_audit_log; migrations 001-008' and design.md:140-142 lists 007/008 as the two NEW tables — fully consistent with 006 being the sixth original (domain_baseline_checklists). The only real (weaker) gap is that design.md does not explicitly state the 006 filename, which is captured as an underspecified documentation fix in the confirmed edits.

refuted.item: critic: overstatement of Property 29's test reach in the draft's ears_story
refuted.why: This is a critique of the DRAFT's wording (whether it over/under-states the test), not a defect in the canonical artifacts. The artifact-level gap it points at — Property 29 has a test (39.19) but no runtime enforcement owner — is genuine and is already captured by the confirmed edit 'Name a concrete owner for the Property 29 fact-check'. No separate artifact edit.

refuted.item: critic (weak): Z3 CHECK-12a/12b, CHECK-13a/13b, CHECK-9a operate on bare Booleans and do not reference research.md / checklist / claims / authority tiers concretely
refuted.why: The Z3 harness is, by design and by its own documentation, an abstract Boolean logic model (design.md:531-591 describes the EARS→SMT-lib abstraction; checks operate on abstract propositions like checklistIsDraft/checklistUsedForDisc/omissionDeclNull). Abstracting concrete entities to Booleans is the intended modeling approach, not a defect to fix in formal_verification_merged.py. The concrete enforcement is correctly delegated to runtime hooks + property tests (39.17/39.19). No actionable harness edit; the substantive coverage gap (no runtime owner for Property 29) is already a confirmed edit.
